title: PharmaCorp Temperature-Controlled Distribution — Agreement for Temperature-Controlled Pharmaceutical Distribution between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and PharmaCorp Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
Agreement No. CTR-2025-001
PharmaCorp Temperature-Controlled Distribution

Parties
1. Carrier: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Centralna 12, 00-001 Warszawa, NIP: PLXXXXXXXXX, hereinafter Carrier.
2. Client: PharmaCorp Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Farmaceutyczna 5, 00-100 Warszawa, NIP: PLYYYYYYYYY, hereinafter Client.

Effective Date and Term
This Agreement becomes effective on 2025-01-01 and shall remain in force for an initial term of three (3) years. Unless terminated in accordance with Section Termination, the Agreement will automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods subject to mutual written notice no later than ninety (90) days prior to expiry.

Scope of Services
Carrier shall provide temperature-controlled pharmaceutical distribution services (zlecenie transportowe) across the Republic of Poland on the Warszawa–Krakow–Katowice–Wroclaw network and feeder routes to Gdansk and Poznan depots, serving 45 pharmacy chain locations designated by Client. Services include receipt of product, storage, consolidation, transport under GDP-compliant conditions, delivery to pharmacies, and generation of transport documentation (CMR, list przewozowy) and faktura pursuant to agreed pricing.

Compliance and Standards
Carrier warrants compliance with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) as required by the Polish Prawo Farmaceutyczne and EU Guidelines 2013/C 343/01, applicable provisions of Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Carrier will maintain procedures, personnel training, hygiene, traceability, and recordkeeping to satisfy inspections by Urząd Transportu Kolejowego (UTK), Główny Inspektorat Transportu Drogowego (GITD), and other competent authorities. Client may audit Carrier's GDP compliance subject to audit notice and confidentiality conditions.

Temperature Requirements
Carrier will ensure temperature-controlled carriage according to Client product specifications:
- 2–8°C: vaccines and sensitive biologics
- 15–25°C: standard pharmaceuticals
- -20°C: select biologics and frozen products
All shipments require real-time temperature monitoring and electronic temperature records retained for a minimum of five (5) years.

Fleet and Equipment
Carrier will deploy eight (8) dedicated refrigerated vehicles (reefer) equipped with dual-redundant cooling systems and calibrated temperature control. All vehicles will be equipped with SensoGuard TM-400 real-time temperature and GPS monitoring devices with alerts, tamper evidence, and remote access for Client. Carrier will maintain spare capacity and contingency plans including cross-docking and priority maintenance to preserve service levels and GDP integrity.

Service Levels and Key Performance Indicators (KPI)
- On-time delivery: >= 98.5% measured monthly per zlecenie transportowe.
- Temperature excursion rate: < 0.1% of shipments measured monthly.
Carrier shall provide monthly KPI reporting and quarterly business reviews (QBR). Monthly reports will include on-time delivery, temperature logs (SensoGuard TM-400), exception reports, incidents, corrective actions, and inventory reconciliation. Quarterly meetings will review performance, capacity, pricing, and regulatory changes.

Pricing and Annual Value
The anticipated annual value of services under this Agreement is EUR 1,200,000. Invoicing shall be issued by Carrier as faktura in accordance with agreed rates and payment terms net 30 days, unless otherwise agreed in writing.

Insurance
Carrier shall maintain at its expense:
- Cargo insurance: EUR 500,000 per shipment covering loss or damage in transit.
- Product liability insurance: EUR 5,000,000 aggregate per annum.
Certificates of insurance shall be provided to Client upon execution and on renewal.

Service Credits and Penalty Structure
Failure to meet SLA metrics will trigger penalties as follows (per event unless specified):
- Late shipment: EUR 500 per late shipment (applicable when on-time delivery metric missed and confirmed delay attributable to Carrier).
- Temperature excursion: EUR 2,000 per affected shipment plus Carrier obligation to replace full affected batch at Carrier expense and retrieve and dispose of compromised product in accordance with GDP and Client instructions.
- GDP audit failure: EUR 10,000 per failure where Carrier is found non-compliant in an audit that materially affects distribution integrity.
Penalty Escalation Matrix:
1. First incident in a rolling 12-month period: Assessment and written corrective action plan within 10 business days; penalty as above.
2. Second incident: Additional suspension of new zlecenia for up to 14 days unless corrective action accepted; doubled penalty amounts.
3. Third incident: Right of Client to require immediate replacement of dedicated vehicles and personnel; penalty tripled and right to terminate for cause if critical.
4. Recurrent GDP audit failures (two or more within 12 months): Client may initiate termination for material breach and claim damages in accordance with Kodeks Cywilny.
Penalties shall be offset against Carrier invoices. If penalties exceed invoiced amounts, Carrier shall pay Client within 15 days of invoice adjustment.

Force Majeure
Neither party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by events beyond reasonable control, including but not limited to acts of God, war, strikes, pandemic measures, embargoes, regulatory actions by competent authorities (UTK, GITD), or significant infrastructure failures. The affected party shall provide prompt notice and use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate effects. If a force majeure event continues for more than 60 consecutive days, either party may terminate upon 14 days' written notice without penalty, except for payment obligations accrued prior to the event.

Termination
- For Convenience: Either party may terminate after the initial 12 months on 90 days' prior written notice, subject to completion of in-flight zlecenia and settlement of outstanding invoices and penalties.
- For Cause: Immediate termination is permitted by Client for material breach, including repeated SLA failures, GDP non-compliance that endangers public health, insolvency, or willful misconduct. Termination for cause does not relieve Carrier of liability for damages and obligations to replace compromised product.
- Effect of Termination: Carrier shall deliver all in-transit product to designated delivery points. All proprietary information, temperature logs, and corrective action documentation shall be transferred to Client. Final accounting to be completed within 60 days.

Confidentiality and Data
Both parties shall maintain confidentiality of trade secrets, pricing, routes, and Client product information. Carrier will comply with data protection laws when processing personal data of pharmacy contacts and shall not transfer such data outside the EEA without written consent.

Audit and Inspection
Client reserves the right to perform GDP audits and inspections at Carrier facilities with reasonable notice. Carrier shall provide access to SensoGuard TM-400 telemetry, maintenance records, calibration certificates, and training logs. Material non-compliance discovered during audit may trigger penalties and remedial actions.

Warranties and Liability
Carrier warrants performance in accordance with this Agreement, GDP, and applicable law. Carrier liability shall be governed by Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe to the extent applicable, subject to insurance limits provided above. Carrier shall indemnify Client for recalls, product replacement costs, regulatory fines, and third-party claims arising from Carrier negligence or willful misconduct.

Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
This Agreement is governed by Polish law. Parties shall seek amicable resolution; disputes unresolved within 30 days shall be referred to arbitration in Warszawa under the rules applicable at the time. Each party reserves the right to seek injunctive relief in Polish courts.

Miscellaneous
Notices shall be in writing and delivered to party addresses. Amendments require written agreement signed by authorized representatives. Assignment of rights requires prior written consent, excluding assignment to affiliates.

Signatures
For Carrier: LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Name: _________________________
Title: _________________________
Date: _________________________

For Client: PharmaCorp Polska Sp. z o.o.
Name: _________________________
Title: _________________________
Date: _________________________

Appendices (incorporated by reference)
A. Route and depot list (Warszawa, Krakow, Katowice, Wroclaw, Gdansk, Poznan)
B. Approved temperature profiles and product list
C. SensoGuard TM-400 specifications and telemetry access
D. Audit protocol and corrective action template

Executed as of Effective Date.

title: FreshFoods Refrigerated Transport Agreement — Contract CTR-2025-002 between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and FreshFoods S.A.
text:
Agreement No.: CTR-2025-002
Date: 01 March 2025
Parties:
1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszalkowska 10, 00-590 Warszawa, NIP XYZ, hereinafter "Carrier"; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań.
2) FreshFoods S.A., ul. Przemyslowa 2, 30-001 Kraków, NIP ABC, hereinafter "Customer".
Preamble
This Refrigerated Transport Agreement ("Agreement") is concluded pursuant to the Polish Kodeks Cywilny and in reference to Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and governs carriage, handling and distribution of fresh and frozen food products for the Customer across designated routes in Poland.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall provide refrigerated distribution services (transport, delivery, returns handling, crate management) on 12 fixed routes covering the voivodeships of Mazowsze (Mazowieckie), Małopolska and Śląsk. Routes include regular runs connecting depots and retail endpoints in Warszawa, Radom, Płock, Kraków, Nowy Sącz, Tarnów, Katowice, Gliwice, Sosnowiec, Częstochowa, Bielsko-Biała and Rybnik (12 route schedule attached as Annex A).
1.2 Carrier will serve 180 retail locations comprising network stores (Biedronka, Lidl, Żabka) according to the weekly zlecenie transportowe issued by Customer.
2. Regulatory and Safety Standards
2.1 All operations shall comply with HACCP principles and Ustawa o bezpieczenstwie zywnosci (food safety law), including traceability, allergen control and recall procedures. Carrier represents and warrants it holds documented HACCP plans, internal SOPs and access to UTK/GITD guidance as required.
2.2 Drivers and handling staff must possess up-to-date food hygiene certificates (książeczka sanepid) and receive hygiene training annually. Uniforms shall be clean, marked with Carrier logo and replaced when soiled.
3. Temperature Control and Vehicles
3.1 Temperature requirements: fresh produce and chilled items must be maintained at 2–6°C; frozen products at −18°C or below. Dual-compartment vehicles (separate temperature zones) are mandatory for mixed loads.
3.2 Carrier duty: continuous monitoring of temperature with calibrated data loggers; data to be provided with each lista przewozowa / CMR where applicable. A temperature deviation is a material breach (see penalties).
3.3 Vehicle cleaning schedule: daily sanitization (after each shift or per load), weekly deep clean (full interior disinfection), quarterly third‑party hygiene audit with report to Customer.
4. Delivery Windows and Performance
4.1 Standard delivery windows to stores: morning 05:00–07:00 and afternoon 14:00–16:00. Carrier shall arrive within the window and complete delivery within agreed appointment times. Customer shall provide access and unloading staff as specified in the operational SLA.
4.2 Timeliness KPI: Carrier must meet on-time delivery to each store ≥ 98% monthly. Failure to meet KPI may trigger review and corrective action plans.
5. Penalties and Remedies
5.1 For late store delivery: penalty PLN 900 per late delivery (approx. EUR 200 at 1 EUR = 4.50 PLN). For temperature breach: penalty PLN 4,500 per incident (approx. EUR 1,000). In addition to monetary penalties, Customer may require immediate replacement of spoiled product at Carrier expense; Carrier shall reimburse replacement cost and transport.
5.2 Product replacement: where temperature breach or contamination is confirmed, Carrier shall collect and replace affected goods at its cost, including associated freight and disposal costs, and issue corrected faktura/credit note as required.
6. Pricing, Value and Seasonality
6.1 Annual contract value is agreed at EUR 650,000 (approx. PLN 2,925,000 at 1 EUR = 4.50 PLN). For domestic accounting and invoicing the Parties will use PLN as primary currency with EUR equivalents shown for reference.
6.2 Peak season surcharge: November–December +15% on base rates for services executed in those months.
6.3 Invoicing: monthly faktura based on completed zlecenie transportowe and signed list przewozowy / POD. Payment term: 30 days net, PLN transfer to Carrier account.
7. Returns and Crate Deposit System
7.1 Returns handling is included. Carrier will manage reverse logistics for customer returns, expired items and damaged goods as per Customer returns policy attached as Annex B.
7.2 Reusable crate deposit: EUR 5 per crate (approx. PLN 22.50) deposit recorded on delivery. Returned crates credited against next invoice. Lost or damaged reusable crates charged at replacement cost plus handling.
8. Staff and Subcontracting
8.1 Drivers must hold książeczka sanepid, valid driving licenses, ADR where applicable, and wear clean uniform. Carrier remains responsible for subcontractors and compliance with this Agreement. Subcontracting requires prior written consent if outside Carrier’s approved operator list.
9. Documentation, Traceability and Audits
9.1 Documentation: each delivery must be accompanied by zlecenie transportowe, list przewozowy / CMR (if applicable), temperature log and POD signed by retail representative.
9.2 Audit rights: Customer may audit Carrier’s HACCP records, cleaning logs, vehicle temperature logs and driver certifications quarterly with 5 business days’ notice. Quarterly audit results to be shared; failure to comply may lead to termination.
10. Liability and Insurance
10.1 Carrier liability in relation to carriage governed by Prawo Przewozowe and applicable provisions of Kodeks Cywilny. Carrier shall maintain minimum insurance: cargo insurance, third-party liability and contamination cover. Certificates to be provided on request.
11. Term and Termination
11.1 Term: 12 months from date of signature, renewable by agreement. Early termination for material breach (including repeated temperature breaches or safety non-compliance) with 30 days’ cure period. Immediate termination allowed for gross negligence affecting food safety.
12. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
12.1 This Agreement governed by Polish law. Disputes first by negotiation, failing which by competent courts in Warszawa. Parties acknowledge obligations under UOKiK and will not engage in unfair commercial practices.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: _______________________  Name / Title
For FreshFoods S.A.: ___________________________  Name / Title
Annexes: A — Route Schedule (12 routes); B — Returns & Quality SOP; C — HACCP Summary; D — Vehicle & Cleaning Log Template
Notes: Monetary values invoiced in PLN as primary currency; EUR shown for reference (1 EUR = 4.50 PLN approx.). This Agreement integrates commercial terms with statutory obligations (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Ustawa o bezpieczenstwie zywnosci) and applicable regulatory guidance from UTK, GITD and UOKiK.

title: AutoParts Express Non-Perishable Distribution Agreement — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and AutoParts Express Sp. z o.o.
text:
Transport Agreement (CTR-2025-003)

Between:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 10, 00-624 Warszawa, depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań (hereinafter "Carrier" or "LogiCore")
and
AutoParts Express Sp. z o.o., ul. Handlowa 2, 40-001 Katowice (hereinafter "Shipper" or "AutoParts Express").

Preamble
This Agreement sets out terms for distribution of non-perishable auto parts across Województwo Śląskie. The Parties agree to perform transport and related services in accordance with applicable Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) and relevant regulations of UTK, GITD and directives of UOKiK where applicable.

1. Term and Value
1.1 Effective date: upon signature. Term: 24 months. Either party may give 90 days' notice to terminate for material breach.
1.2 Estimated annual value: EUR 320,000 (approx. PLN 1,472,000 based on internal reference) per year; Parties acknowledge annual volumes may vary and adjustments follow section 9.

2. Scope of Services
2.1 Carrier shall provide road transport of standard dry cargo (non-temperature-controlled) to a network of up to 120 workshops and dealerships in Śląskie.
2.2 Fixed primary routes (eight main corridors from Katowice hub): Katowice–Bielsko-Biała; Katowice–Częstochowa; Katowice–Gliwice; Katowice–Rybnik; Katowice–Sosnowiec; Katowice–Tychy; Katowice–Dąbrowa Górnicza; Katowice–Zabrze. Sub-routes and last-mile deliveries within these corridors are included.
2.3 Dangerous goods excluded. Batteries, oils and other ADR-classified materials must be shipped under separate ADR-compliant zlecenie transportowe and are outside this Agreement.

3. Orders and Delivery Times
3.1 Order placement: Shipper issues electronic zlecenie transportowe and list przewozowy/CMR as required. Orders received by Carrier before 10:00 local time shall be eligible for same-day delivery; orders received thereafter will be delivered next business day.
3.2 Carrier shall maintain staffing, fleet and routing to achieve same-day/next-day service for the covered network subject to traffic and force majeure.

4. Packaging, Reusable Crates and Deposit System
4.1 Shipper will pack goods in reusable plastic crates supplied by Shipper or Carrier. Crates are subject to a deposit: EUR 5 per crate (approx. PLN 23). Deposit collected on first delivery and credited on return of undamaged crates.
4.2 Returns: Carrier shall collect empty crates on reverse flows or scheduled pickups at workshops/dealerships. Parties will maintain mutual crate inventory records; discrepancies reported within 5 business days. Lost or irreparably damaged crates will be charged at replacement value equal to deposit plus administrative fee.

5. Liability and Penalties
5.1 Carrier liability follows provisions of Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny. Carrier will be responsible for loss or damage to goods where original packaging was intact at origin and damage occurred in transit.
5.2 Penalties: for late delivery Carrier pays EUR 100 (approx. PLN 460) per incident; for damaged goods where Carrier is liable, EUR 500 (approx. PLN 2,300) per incident. Penalties are payable against faktura or credit note within 30 days.

6. Insurance
6.1 Carrier shall procure and maintain cargo insurance with minimum coverage EUR 50,000 (approx. PLN 230,000) per shipment. Certificates to be provided on request.

7. Performance and KPIs
7.1 Agreed KPIs: On-time delivery ≥97%; Damage rate <0.5%; Order accuracy ≥99.5%.
7.2 Monthly reporting: Carrier shall deliver KPI reports, exception reports and supporting CMR/list przewozowy data. Failure to meet KPIs triggers remediation plan; persistent underperformance (three consecutive months) permits Shipper to impose agreed financial deductions or to terminate under clause 1.1.

8. Fees and Invoicing
8.1 Fees: as per Annex A (rate card). Unless otherwise agreed, domestic currency settlement will be in PLN (with EUR equivalents used for reference only). Carrier issues VAT faktura monthly for completed shipments. Payment terms: 30 days net.

9. Adjustments and Extraordinary Costs
9.1 Fuel surcharges, route changes, and regulatory fees (e.g., tolls) shall be passed through only if pre-agreed in writing or required by law.

10. Compliance and Documentation
10.1 Parties shall comply with CMR/list przewozowy requirements, ADR where applicable for excluded goods, and cooperate in audits by UTK/GITD. Data protection obligations for customer details to comply with applicable law.

11. Force Majeure
11.1 Neither party liable for delays caused by force majeure (natural events, strikes, governmental actions). Affected party must notify the other without undue delay.

12. Termination and Liability Cap
12.1 Termination for convenience permitted with 90 days' notice. Liability cap for direct damages shall not exceed annual value of the Agreement except for willful misconduct or gross negligence. Penalty payments do not preclude claim for larger proven damages.

13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
13.1 This Agreement governed by Polish law. Disputes to be resolved by competent courts in Warszawa, unless parties agree otherwise.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________  Date: _____
For AutoParts Express Sp. z o.o.: ____________________  Date: _____

Annexes: Annex A (Rates/Rate Card), Annex B (Route List and Delivery Points — inventory of 120 workshops/dealerships), Annex C (Insurance Certificates, crate inventory template).

End of Agreement CTR-2025-003.

title: ChemTrans Hazardous Materials Transport Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and ChemTrans S.A.
text:
CONTRACT NO. CTR-2025-004
ChemTrans Hazardous Materials Transport Agreement

This Agreement ("Agreement") is made on this day, by and between:
- LogiCore Sp. z o.o., a company incorporated under Polish law, headquartered in Warszawa, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter "Carrier" or "LogiCore"), and
- ChemTrans S.A., a joint-stock company with registered office in Płock (hereinafter "Shipper" or "ChemTrans").

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in road transport and logistics and is duly licensed to perform carriage of hazardous materials in accordance with ADR and applicable Polish law. ChemTrans operates a refinery in Płock and requires ADR-certified carriage of hazardous substances to chemical plants in Tarnów, Police and Puławy. The parties agree the Carrier shall perform transport services under the terms of this Agreement, subject to compliance with ADR, Ustawa o przewozie towarów niebezpiecznych, Prawo przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and relevant regulations of UTK, GITD and UOKiK.

1. Definitions
- ADR: European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road and its national implementation.
- DGSA: Doradca ds. bezpieczeństwa ADR / Dangerous Goods Safety Advisor (osoba odpowiedzialna za bezpieczeństwo).
- zaświadczenie ADR: driver ADR certificate required by ADR and Polish law.
- EX/III: Equipment and vehicle type rating as required for certain packing groups and classes.
- CMR / list przewozowy: consignment documentation as required.

2. Scope of Services
2.1 Carrier shall provide ADR-certified transport services for hazardous goods listed by Shipper, collected at the Płock refinery and delivered to chemical plants in Tarnów, Police and Puławy on the routes agreed by the parties. Services include loading supervision, carriage, unloading supervision, and supply of required ADR documentation (CMR, list przewozowy, Safety Data Sheets).
2.2 Total annual contract value: EUR 2,100,000 (two million one hundred thousand euros) — the highest-value contract between the parties for the Contract Year.

3. Compliance with ADR and Polish Law
3.1 Carrier warrants that all transports shall be performed in strict compliance with ADR (including ADR Chapter 1.8 — emergency provisions, Chapter 6 — packing and UN-approved packaging, and other applicable chapters), Ustawa o przewozie towarów niebezpiecznych, Prawo przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.
3.2 All packaging used by the Carrier or provided on behalf of the Shipper must be UN-approved per ADR Chapter 6 and bear appropriate marks and certificates.

4. DGSA, Oversight and Reporting
4.1 Carrier shall employ or retain a DGSA (Doradca ADR) to oversee all ADR operations. The DGSA is responsible for quarterly written reports to the Shipper and monthly safety briefings. Quarterly DGSA reports shall include compliance audits, incident trends, training records and corrective actions.
4.2 DGSA contact details and qualifications shall be provided to Shipper prior to commencement.

5. Driver Requirements
5.1 All drivers must possess a valid zaświadczenie ADR (ADR certificate) and maintain an annual refresher course; records of training and certificates shall be provided to Shipper upon request.
5.2 Drivers must carry a copy of the CMR/list przewozowy, Safety Data Sheets, and remain available for emergency coordination.

6. Vehicle and Equipment Requirements
6.1 Vehicles used for carriage of Class 3 (flammable liquids) and Class 8 (corrosives) must be EX/III-rated where required, equipped with ADR-compliant equipment kits (fire extinguisher, absorbents, protective clothing, signage, warning triangles), spill containment and grounding arrangements.
6.2 Vehicles shall carry an up-to-date ADR equipment kit, placarding consistent with transported goods, and be subject to pre-departure checks and periodic documented maintenance.

7. Emergency Response and Hotline
7.1 The Carrier shall maintain and execute an Emergency Response Plan in accordance with ADR 1.8.3.1. The Plan shall be provided to Shipper and to receiving plants in Tarnów, Police and Puławy.
7.2 Carrier shall operate a 24/7 emergency hotline staffed by competent personnel (DGSA or designated emergency coordinator). Hotline details and escalation procedures shall be provided to Shipper prior to first shipment.

8. Insurance and Liability
8.1 Carrier shall maintain insurance consistent with the risks: hazmat liability coverage of EUR 10,000,000 (ten million euros) and environmental damage insurance of EUR 5,000,000 (five million euros). Evidence of insurance (polisa, certificates) shall be provided to Shipper annually and upon renewal.
8.2 Carrier remains liable for loss or damage to cargo, third-party damage and environmental harm to the extent provided by ADR, CMR and applicable Polish law.

9. Audits, Inspections and Safety Controls
9.1 Monthly safety audits shall be conducted by Carrier and made available to Shipper within five (5) business days of completion. Audits shall include driver files, vehicle checklists, equipment inventories and incident logs.
9.2 Shipper reserves the right to perform reasonable on-site audits at Carrier depots with prior notice; urgent inspections may be performed after incidents.

10. Incident Reporting and Investigation
10.1 Notification: In the event of any compliance violation, spill, fire, release, collision or other incident affecting safety or environment, Carrier shall notify Shipper and emergency services within two (2) hours by telephone to the 24/7 hotline and to designated Shipper contacts.
10.2 Written Report: A preliminary written report shall be provided within 24 hours, followed by a full written incident report within ten (10) business days including root-cause analysis, corrective measures and DGSA commentary.
10.3 Preservation of Evidence: Carrier shall preserve vehicle and cargo evidence and cooperate with Shipper and authorities (UTK, GITD) during investigations.

11. Penalties and Remedies
11.1 For each verified compliance violation (procedural non-conformity, documentation failure, improper packaging, driver or vehicle non-compliance), Carrier shall pay a contractual penalty of EUR 5,000 per event, payable within thirty (30) days of invoice.
11.2 For each safety incident causing injury, significant release, environmental damage or regulatory enforcement action, Carrier shall pay a contractual penalty of EUR 25,000 per incident in addition to liability for damages.
11.3 Willful violation of ADR, deliberate falsification of documents or concealment of incidents shall constitute material breach and permit immediate termination for cause by Shipper and recovery of all damages.

12. Term and Termination
12.1 Term: This Agreement shall commence on signing and run for an initial period of twelve (12) months, renewable by mutual written agreement.
12.2 Termination for Cause: Immediate termination for willful violations as set out in Clause 11.3. Either party may terminate for material breach with thirty (30) days' cure period, except where safety demands immediate suspension.

13. Miscellaneous
13.1 Confidentiality: Parties shall treat operational details, pricing and safety reports as confidential (faktycznie "informacje poufne").
13.2 Governing Law and Jurisdiction: This Agreement shall be governed by Polish law. Disputes shall be submitted to the competent courts in Warszawa unless otherwise agreed.
13.3 Notices: Notices shall be delivered to business addresses and email contacts provided by each party.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
Name: Title: Date:

For ChemTrans S.A.: ______________________
Name: Title: Date:

Appendices (incorporated): Appendix A — List of goods and UN numbers; Appendix B — Emergency Response Plan (ADR 1.8.3.1); Appendix C — DGSA qualifications and contact; Appendix D — Insurance certificates; Appendix E — Route schedules (Płock → Tarnów / Police / Puławy).

This Agreement (CTR-2025-004) constitutes the entire understanding between the parties regarding carriage of hazardous materials and supersedes prior agreements. Parties acknowledge awareness of UTK, GITD and UOKiK oversight and agree to cooperate with regulatory inspections and reporting obligations.

title: ElectroHub Consumer Electronics Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and ElectroHub Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
ELECTROHUB CONSUMER ELECTRONICS DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT
Contract No.: CTR-2025-005
Date: [_____/2025]
Parties:
  1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter "LogiCore");
  2) ElectroHub Polska Sp. z o.o., headquartered in [address], (hereinafter "Customer").
Preamble
  This Agreement governs the national distribution of high-value consumer electronics from Customer's central warehouse in Stryków (near Łódź) to eighty-five (85) retail stores across the Republic of Poland. The parties agree as follows, in accordance with applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and in compliance with regulatory guidance from UTK, GITD and UOKiK.
1. Scope of Services
  1.1 LogiCore will provide scheduled distribution services (zlecenie transportowe) for smartphones, laptops, televisions and related accessories ("Goods"). Average shipment value per consignment is EUR 45,000. Annual contract value is EUR 480,000.
  1.2 Services include: collection at Stryków central warehouse; sorting and palletising; road transport to eighty-five (85) retail stores; delivery within specified delivery windows; proof of delivery using signed CMR/list przewozowy and electronic POD; returns and defective-product pickup as detailed in Section 6.
  1.3 Seasonal peaks: volume increases circa Black Friday and Christmas (November–December) by approximately +60%. Parties will plan capacity and rates accordingly.
2. Operational Requirements
  2.1 Delivery windows: all retail stores open at 10:00 local time. Deliveries must be completed between 07:00 and 09:30 local time. Deliveries outside this window constitute a breach subject to penalties per Section 7.
  2.2 Anti-theft and security: for all shipments LogiCore shall use GPS-tracked sealed trailers, tamper-evident seals, and maintain continuous GPS monitoring. Drivers must follow a driver verification protocol on pickup and delivery: presentation of company ID, electronic signature capture, vehicle and seal inspection recorded by smartphone photo timestamp and uploaded to LogiCore TMS. LogiCore shall maintain chain-of-custody records and CCTV footage where available.
  2.3 Packaging and manifest: Customer will supply packing lists and serial-numbered manifests for high-value Goods. LogiCore will verify manifest against physically loaded pallets and record any discrepancies at loading (protokół załadunku).
3. Insurance and Liability
  3.1 Insurance: LogiCore shall procure and maintain third-party all-risk cargo insurance covering theft and physical loss or damage to a limit of EUR 200,000 per shipment. Evidence of insurance (polisa) will be provided to the Customer on commencement and upon renewal.
  3.2 Liability: In addition to insurance, LogiCore accepts contractual liability for loss or theft up to full replacement value of lost Goods. For damage caused by LogiCore negligence, compensation shall be market replacement cost. For claims, parties will comply with procedures under Prawo Przewozowe and the processes set out herein.
4. Pricing and Payment
  4.1 Annual contract value is EUR 480,000. Pricing per consignment and surcharge for seasonal peak periods will be set in Annex A (Rate Card). All invoices (faktura) shall be issued in EUR, net 30 days from receipt of correct invoice and signed POD. VAT and applicable taxes will be charged according to Polish tax law.
5. Compliance and Regulatory
  5.1 LogiCore will comply with Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and directives or inspections by UTK, GITD and consumer protection obligations under UOKiK where applicable. Drivers and vehicles shall meet licensing and technical standards; LogiCore will provide documentation upon request.
6. Returns and Reverse Logistics
  6.1 LogiCore shall operate returns logistics for defective products: pickup from stores, consolidation at nearest depot (Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań as operationally appropriate) and transport to Customer's designated service centre. Returns shall be processed under separate return manifests, with serial numbers and defect descriptions. Costs for standard returns are included in the Rate Card; warranty returns subject to Customer instructions.
7. Delivery Performance and Penalties
  7.1 Late delivery: EUR 300 per incident for deliveries arriving after 09:30 but before store closing, provided POD is signed. 7.2 Missed delivery window (failure to deliver within 07:00–09:30): EUR 250 per missed window. 7.3 Theft or total loss attributable to LogiCore fault: full replacement of Goods at Customer's verified invoice or market value. Penalties are in addition to insurance recoveries where LogiCore is liable. Penalties are subject to standard mitigation and dispute processes; gross negligence or criminal acts by carrier or subcontractor remove limit caps.
8. Subcontracting
  8.1 LogiCore may subcontract transport services but remains fully responsible to Customer for fulfilment of obligations, compliance with security protocols, insurance cover and data/reporting. Subcontractors must meet the same anti-theft standards and be pre-approved in writing.
9. Force Majeure
  9.1 Neither party shall be liable for delays or non-performance due to force majeure events beyond reasonable control (strikes, severe weather, road closures). The affected party shall notify the other immediately and use commercially reasonable efforts to resume performance.
10. Confidentiality and Data Protection
  10.1 Parties shall treat manifests, customer lists, pricing and route data as confidential. Personal data processed in connection with deliveries shall be handled in compliance with applicable data protection laws.
11. Term and Termination
  11.1 Term: 12 months from signature, renewable by mutual agreement. 11.2 Termination for cause: material breach including repeated security failures or failure to insure may result in termination with 14 days' notice if uncured.
12. Claims and Dispute Resolution
  12.1 Claims for loss/damage must be notified within 7 days of delivery; for concealed damage within 7 days of discovery. Parties shall attempt amicable settlement; disputes unresolved within 30 days shall be referred to arbitration in Warsaw or Polish courts where statutory rights apply.
13. Miscellaneous
  13.1 Amendments must be in writing. 13.2 Annexes: Rate Card, Service Levels, Incident Report Form, Driver Verification Protocol, Insurance Certificate.

title: Transport Agreement for Construction Materials between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and BudMat S.A. (CTR-2025-006)
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-006
"BudMat Construction Materials Transport" — Agreement for road carriage of heavy construction materials

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Centralna 12, 00-001 Warszawa, Polska, with operational depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter "Carrier" or "LogiCore");
2. BudMat S.A., ul. Inwestorska 5, 00-100 Warszawa, Polska (hereinafter "Principal" or "BudMat").

Preamble
This Agreement is concluded in accordance with the applicable provisions of Polish law including the Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and the Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and with due regard to decisions and guidance issued by UTK, GITD and market practice monitored by UOKiK. The parties agree carriage services under terms set out below for heavy construction materials from manufacturing plants to construction sites located in the regions of Mazowsze and Łódzkie.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall perform scheduled and ad-hoc transports of heavy construction materials including, but not limited to: cement (worksite bags and bulk), steel rebar (pręty zbrojeniowe), prefab concrete panels (płyty prefabrykowane), and roofing materials (dachówki, membrany) ("Cargo").
1.2 Routes: Carrier will operate from six manufacturing plants located in the catchment area serving Mazowsze and Łódzkie (representative plants in Płock, Radom, Siedlce, Skierniewice, Piotrków Trybunalski and Łowicz) to multiple construction sites designated by Principal across Mazowsze and Łódzkie. Specific origin-destination pairs will be issued by Principal via zlecenie transportowe.

2. Vehicles and Equipment
2.1 Carrier will provide appropriate vehicle types: flatbed trailers (naczepy platformy), curtain-siders (firanki), and crane-equipped trucks (samochody z HDS). All vehicles will be maintained to industry standards and carry required certificates.
2.2 For prefab concrete panels and other oversized pieces Carrier shall provide crane-equipped trucks or arrange for external lifting equipment as agreed in the zlecenie transportowe.

3. Permits and Special Authorisations
3.1 Oversized loads: Transport of prefab concrete panels classified as nienormatywny requires and will be conducted only with a valid zezwolenie na przejazd pojazdu nienormatywnego (oversized load permit). Carrier is responsible for obtaining such permits and for route notifications, escort arrangements and compliance with GITD/UTK requirements unless otherwise agreed in writing.
3.2 All convoy planning, temporary traffic restrictions, and local municipality notifications are the responsibility of the Carrier when required by law or by the terms of the permit.

4. Loading, Weighing and Documentation
4.1 Weight verification: All loads must be weighed at origin on certified scales and a weight certificate appended to the CMR or list przewozowy. The declared CMR weight must match weighbridge measurement within a tolerance of ±2%. Deviations beyond 2% will trigger immediate review and may result in refusal to load or adjustment of charges.
4.2 Carrier shall issue the CMR and list przewozowy in accordance with Prawo Przewozowe and deliver a signed copy to Principal upon completion of each carriage. Each consignment must be accompanied by a zlecenie transportowe and any necessary permit documentation.

5. Site Access, Offloading and Coordination
5.1 Offloading: Where offloading requires crane operations, Carrier will coordinate crane offloading (koordynacja HDS/dźwig) with site management. Principal shall provide access times, designated unloading point and any site-specific safety or coordination instructions in the zlecenie transportowe.
5.2 Site access restrictions: Principal shall notify Carrier in advance of known site limitations such as narrow roads, bridge weight limits, restricted hours, or temporary access changes. Carrier shall plan vehicle selection and routing to respect posted weight limits and road width; if exceptional measures are required, parties shall agree costs and schedule adjustments in writing prior to execution.

6. Delivery Times, Performance and Penalties
6.1 Time is of the essence for many deliveries. Agreed delivery windows will be specified in each zlecenie transportowe. Late deliveries interrupting construction crew work shall incur a penalty of EUR 150 per late delivery occasion, payable by Carrier to Principal, unless delay is caused by force majeure or Principal's failure to permit unloading.
6.2 Site damage: Carrier shall be liable for direct damage to site property caused by Carrier's vehicles or personnel; proven site damage will incur a fixed penalty of EUR 2,000 per incident in addition to costs of repair or replacement.

7. Contract Value and Seasonality
7.1 Estimated annual value of services under this Agreement is EUR 550,000 (five hundred fifty thousand EUR). Billing and faktura shall be issued in EUR unless Parties agree otherwise in writing.
7.2 Seasonality: Parties acknowledge peak season runs March–November. Expected transport volume is reduced December–February by 40% compared to peak season; parties shall adjust schedules and resource allocation accordingly.

8. Safety, PPE and Driver Obligations
8.1 Drivers and accompanying personnel visiting construction sites must wear required PPE (kask ochronny, kamizelka odblaskowa, buty ochronne z metalowym podnoskiem). Carrier warrants that drivers are trained for site-specific safety procedures and that HDS/crane operators hold required qualifications.
8.2 Carrier shall ensure vehicles carry necessary safety equipment and that drivers comply with site sign-in, toolbox talks and any site induction required by Principal.

9. Insurance and Liability
9.1 Carrier shall maintain liability and cargo insurance adequate for the types of load carried and meeting standard market cover for construction materials. Proof of insurance shall be provided to Principal on request.
9.2 Liability will be governed by this Agreement and applicable law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe). Carrier remains liable for loss or damage to Cargo except where loss is due to actions of Principal or force majeure.

10. Invoicing and Payment
10.1 Carrier will invoice Principal monthly by faktura VAT in EUR for services rendered with reference to zlecenie transportowe numbers and CMRs. Payment terms: 30 days net from date of correct invoice unless otherwise agreed.

11. Compliance and Dispute Resolution
11.1 Parties undertake to comply with all applicable transport regulations, including Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and instructions from UTK and GITD. Anti-monopoly conduct must comply with UOKiK standards.
11.2 Disputes will be resolved amicably; failing resolution, courts in Warszawa shall have jurisdiction unless Parties agree arbitration.

12. Term and Termination
12.1 This Agreement is effective from date of signature for 12 months and may be extended by mutual written agreement. Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days' cure period; immediate termination permitted for severe safety breaches or repeated non-performance.

13. Final Provisions
13.1 Any amendments must be in writing. Notices shall be sent to the registered addresses and by e-mail to the contract managers specified in each party's internal order confirmation.

Executed in two counterparts.

For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Carrier
Name: ____________________
Title: ____________________
Date: ____________________

For BudMat S.A. — Principal
Name: ____________________
Title: ____________________
Date: ____________________

Annexes: Sample zlecenie transportowe template, list of manufacturing plants and nominated site access contacts, insurance certificates, permit procedure checklist (zezwolenie na przejazd pojazdu nienormatywnego).

title: MedDevice Sterile Medical Equipment Transport Agreement — CTR-2025-007
text:
This Transport Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 01 May 2025 between:

LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, ul. Nowogrodzka 50, 00-695 Warszawa, NIP: PL526-000-00-00, with operational depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter "LogiCore" or "Carrier") and

MedDevice Europe Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, ul. Rzemieślnicza 12, 02-234 Warszawa, NIP: PL779-111-22-33 (hereinafter "Customer").

Contract Reference: CTR-2025-007
Annual Contracted Value: EUR 890,000 (nine hundred eighty-nine thousand euros)
Effective Date: 01 May 2025
Term: 24 months, renewable by mutual written agreement.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall provide specialized transport, warehousing and handling services for sterile medical devices belonging to Customer, including but not limited to surgical instruments, implants and diagnostic equipment (collectively "Products"). Services include scheduled routine deliveries to 35 hospitals across Poland and on-call emergency deliveries per Section 4.
1.2 All transports shall comply with EU MDR 2017/745, national requirements and oversight by Urząd Rejestracji Produktów Leczniczych, and applicable provisions of Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Carrier will operate under Transport Orders (zlecenie transportowe) and issue list przewozowy/CMR as required.

2. Packaging, Cleanroom and Handling Requirements
2.1 Products shall be transported and stored in cleanroom-compatible secondary packaging meeting ISO 14644 standards for particulate and microbiological control where required by Product specification. Carrier shall maintain dedicated, clearly marked handling areas at depots for sterile goods.
2.2 Anti-vibration fixtures, shock-absorbing restraint systems and calibrated mounting shall be used for sensitive diagnostic equipment and implants. Carrier shall maintain validated restraint and cushioning records for each vehicle and shipment.
2.3 Sterility assurance: any opening, perforation or compromise of sterile packaging detected at any point is a material breach ("Sterility Breach"). Sterility Breach requires immediate isolation of affected units and notification to Customer and Urząd Rejestracji Produktów Leczniczych as applicable.

3. Environmental Controls and Monitoring
3.1 All transports shall be performed in a controlled environment with temperature maintained between 15–25°C and continuous humidity monitoring. Vehicles and storage facilities shall be equipped with calibrated temperature and humidity loggers with tamper-evident seals and real-time alarm capability to Carrier’s Transport Management System (TMS).
3.2 Any excursion outside the 15–25°C range or humidity parameters shall trigger immediate corrective procedures, quarantine of affected Product, and notification to Customer. Records of environmental conditions shall be retained for minimum five (5) years and made available upon request.

4. Delivery SLA and Emergency Response
4.1 Routine Deliveries: standard delivery windows per hospital as agreed in Annex A. Carrier commits to on-time performance of routine deliveries at 98% monthly compliance.
4.2 Emergency Deliveries: Carrier shall maintain capacity to perform emergency deliveries with a four (4) hour SLA from time of order acceptance to arrival at hospital for all 35 named facilities. Emergency delivery requests will be made via Customer's certified ordering channel defined in Annex B.

5. Traceability, UDI and Documentation
5.1 Carrier shall provide full lot and serial-traceability for all Products. Unique Device Identification (UDI) scanning is mandatory at every handover: at Carrier pickup, depot transfers (if any), and hospital delivery. Scans must record timestamp, GPS coordinates, operator ID, vehicle ID, and condition of packaging.
5.2 Electronic chain-of-custody records shall be retained and available in CSV and human-readable PDF formats. Carrier shall provide real-time tracking links and daily manifests for all active shipments. All CMR/list przewozowy and zlecenie transportowe documents shall be supplied to Customer per statutory retention periods.

6. Personnel and Training
6.1 Carrier shall ensure that all drivers and handlers assigned to this Agreement hold Medical Device Handling Certification and have completed sterile protocol awareness training. Driver training shall be documented, renewed annually and include modules on EU MDR requirements, aseptic handling, UDI scanning and emergency procedures.
6.2 Carrier shall provide Customer with certificates of training and list of qualified personnel upon contract commencement and after each training cycle.

7. Compliance, Audits and Regulatory Support
7.1 Monthly Compliance Audits: Carrier shall perform monthly internal compliance audits covering packaging, temperature logs, UDI capture, driver competence and vehicle maintenance. Audit reports shall be delivered to Customer within five (5) business days of month-end.
7.2 Annual Regulatory Inspection Support: upon reasonable notice Carrier will support Customer and regulators (including Urząd Rejestracji Produktów Leczniczych) during inspections, providing access to records, personnel and premises. Carrier will cooperate with UTK, GITD and other relevant bodies for matters pertaining to transport compliance.

8. Penalties and Remedies
8.1 Late Routine Delivery: EUR 1,000 per late routine delivery incident.
8.2 Late Emergency Delivery: EUR 5,000 per late emergency delivery incident (failure to meet 4-hour SLA).
8.3 Sterility Breach: EUR 15,000 per confirmed sterility breach, in addition to Customer’s right to claim actual damages under Kodeks Cywilny.
8.4 Penalties are without prejudice to Customer’s right to terminate for cause and to claim further damages where applicable.

9. Insurance, Liability and Indemnification
9.1 Carrier shall maintain insurer-backed cargo insurance and third-party liability insurance adequate for the value and risk of Products transported, no less than EUR 2,000,000 per occurrence, and shall provide certificates upon request. Carrier is liable for loss, damage or contamination arising from its negligence, wilful misconduct, or breach of this Agreement.
9.2 Both Parties agree to limit indirect and consequential damages except for claims arising from Sterility Breach, personal injury or regulatory fines.

10. Fees, Invoicing and Payment
10.1 Fees for services and schedules are set out in Annex C. Invoices (faktura) will be issued monthly in EUR with detailed line items per zlecenie transportowe and delivered electronically. Payment terms: thirty (30) days net from invoice date, subject to dispute procedures.

11. Termination
11.1 Either party may terminate for material breach if not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice. Immediate termination for cause is permitted in the event of repeated SLA failures, Sterility Breach, or material regulatory non-compliance.

12. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
12.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be submitted to the competent court in Warszawa unless Parties agree to mediation. Relevant statutory frameworks include Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Regulatory bodies referenced: UTK, GITD, UOKiK and Urząd Rejestracji Produktów Leczniczych.

13. Confidentiality and Data Protection
13.1 Parties will treat all shipment details, UDI data and Customer information as Confidential. Personal data processing shall comply with applicable data protection laws and GDPR.

14. Miscellaneous
14.1 Subcontracting of core transport functions requires prior written consent of Customer. 14.2 Amendments to this Agreement require written form signed by authorized representatives.

Title: CEO                                       Title: Managing Director
