title: VinoPolska Wine and Spirits Distribution — Distribution and Transport Agreement (CTR-2025-008)
text:
Agreement CTR-2025-008

Agreement for Temperature-Controlled Distribution of Wine and Spirits between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (carrier/operator) and VinoPolska Sp. z o.o. (importer/distributor)

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 12, 00-001 Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań (hereinafter "LogiCore").
2. VinoPolska Sp. z o.o., ul. Puławska 88, 02-515 Warszawa (hereinafter "VinoPolska").

Preamble
This Agreement governs the transport and distribution services (zlecenie transportowe) provided by LogiCore for VinoPolska's wine and spirits stock originating from the bonded warehouse Warszawa Okęcie and delivered to over 200 HoReCa venues and retail chains across the Republic of Poland. The Parties agree to perform services subject to the applicable provisions of Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and relevant EU regulations.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall provide temperature-controlled transport for wine and ambient transport for spirits from Warszawa Okęcie bonded warehouse to recipient locations (restaurants, hotels, bars, retail chains). Deliveries shall be executed under CMR or list przewozowy as applicable and accompanied by faktura and complete EMCS PL2 documentation for movements under excise suspension.
1.2 Wine temperature control: mandatory maintained 12°C–16°C during storage, loading, transport and temporary holding. Spirits: ambient conditions unless otherwise instructed in writing by VinoPolska.
1.3 Routes and frequency: scheduled routes covering 200+ HoReCa venues and retail chains; priority service during peak periods (Nov–Dec and May–Jun).

2. Excise Compliance and Documentation
2.1 LogiCore shall verify banderol presence and authenticity on each excise-subject item prior to leaving the bonded warehouse. Any discrepancy shall be reported immediately to VinoPolska and relevant authorities (GITD, UTK if applicable).
2.2 For movements under excise suspension, LogiCore shall complete and transmit EMCS PL2 messages, maintain records sufficient to satisfy Urząd Celny and provide copies to VinoPolska upon request. Failure to execute proper EMCS PL2 procedures attributable to LogiCore will render LogiCore liable for penalties and costs resulting from non-compliance.

3. Handling of Fragile Cargo
3.1 Wine cases are classified as fragile cargo. LogiCore will employ cushioning, palletization, and vehicle stowage practices approved by VinoPolska; drivers and handlers will be trained in handling glass.
3.2 Target breakage rate: <0.3% (cases per annual shipped cases). Performance will be reviewed quarterly. In the event of breakage during transport, LogiCore shall provide full replacement of damaged goods at LogiCore's expense (full replacement, replacement value based on VinoPolska's last faktura price) and cover associated disposal costs.

4. Delivery Scheduling and Time Windows
4.1 Delivery windows: HoReCa (restaurants) preferred 10:00–14:00 (prior to service); retail chains preferred 06:00–08:00. LogiCore will schedule stops and routing to meet these windows. VinoPolska will provide delivery addresses and contact persons in each zlecenie transportowe.
4.2 Late delivery penalty: EUR 150 per late delivery (each occasion of arrival outside agreed window) payable by LogiCore, unless delay is caused by force majeure or recipient fault.

5. Age Verification and Acceptance
5.1 Drivers must verify that recipient is authorized to receive alcohol (legal age and business authorization). Verification methods include presentation of ID and confirmation against recipient contact details provided by VinoPolska.
5.2 If recipient refuses to accept alcohol or fails age verification, driver shall return goods to bonded status or to VinoPolska’s instruction; costs and liabilities determined per Kodeks Cywilny and parties’ instructions.

6. Insurance and Liability
6.1 LogiCore shall maintain cargo insurance covering EUR 100,000 per shipment, including explicit cover for glass breakage. Policy details and certificate to be provided to VinoPolska upon signing and upon renewal.
6.2 Liability for loss or damage during carriage governed by Prawo Przewozowe and CMR where applicable; not limited where caused by willful misconduct or gross negligence.

7. Commercial Terms
7.1 Annual value of services: estimated EUR 380,000. Payment terms: 30 days from date of faktura unless otherwise agreed in writing.
7.2 Peak season adjustments: Parties acknowledge increased volumes in Nov–Dec and May–Jun; LogiCore shall allocate resources and may propose surge pricing with 30 days prior notice.

8. Quality, Reporting and Regulatory Cooperation
8.1 LogiCore shall provide weekly reports of deliveries, temperature logs (12–16°C for wine) and breakage incidents. Quarterly KPI reports to include breakage rates; target <0.3%.
8.2 Both Parties shall cooperate with regulatory bodies (UTK, GITD, Urząd Celny) and UOKiK investigations where applicable.

9. Penalties and Remedies
9.1 Late delivery: EUR 150 per event.
9.2 Breakage during transport: full replacement by LogiCore and repair or disposal costs. Repeated failure to meet breakage target (<0.3%) may result in contract termination or renegotiation.

10. Term, Termination and Force Majeure
10.1 Term: 12 months from signing, auto-renewable unless 60 days' notice provided. Termination for cause permitted for material breach.
10.2 Force majeure exceptions apply as per Kodeks Cywilny.

11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes to be resolved by arbitration in Warszawa or competent Polish courts if arbitration not agreed.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________ Date: ______
For VinoPolska Sp. z o.o. __________________ Date: ______

Attachments: Rate card, Insurance certificate, Sample EMCS PL2 procedures, Standard zlecenie transportowe template, Temperature log template, KPI reporting template.

title: AgroTrans Grain and Agricultural Transport Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and AgroTrans Wielkopolska Sp. z o.o.
text:
AGREEMENT No. CTR-2025-009

AgroTrans Grain and Agricultural Transport Agreement
between LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 12, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland (hereinafter LogiCore), and AgroTrans Wielkopolska Sp. z o.o., ul. Rolna 8, 62-800 Poznań, Poland (hereinafter Client/AgroTrans).

Preamble
LogiCore is a carrier and logistics operator with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań and provides road transport services in accordance with Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Client requires transport of agricultural commodities as described below. Parties agree the following terms and conditions (Agreement, Umowa).

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Subject: carriage of bulk agricultural commodities consisting primarily of wheat, rapeseed, corn and associated aggregate agricultural loads from farms and collection points to silos, grain elevators and processing plants in the regions of Wielkopolska and Kujawy (Poland's grain belt). Services shall be provided as zlecenie transportowe issued by Client.
1.2 Vehicle types: tipper trailers with walking floor technology, bulk tankers for liquid feed, and other specialist bulk vehicles as agreed and listed in Annex A. All vehicles must meet technical and sanitary requirements and be operated by drivers holding required qualifications.
1.3 Routes: primary operations cover the counties and municipalities within Wielkopolska and Kujawy, including but not limited to Poznań, Konin, Kalisz, Gniezno, Inowrocław and Włocławek corridors. Additional routes may be added by mutual agreement.

2. Seasonal Volumes and Capacity
2.1 Harvest season: July 1 to October 31 each year. During the harvest peak Parties anticipate an increase of approximately +200% in transport volume compared to off-peak average. Client shall provide rolling forecasts and daily loading schedules no less than 48 hours in advance where possible.
2.2 Off-season: volumes are reduced; parties may modify frequency and vehicle allocation by written amendment.

3. Quality, Sanitation and Phytosanitary Compliance
3.1 All trailers and tanks must be clean, dry, free from previous cargo contamination, and suitable for the intended commodity. 'Clean trailers' are mandatory. Failure to comply constitutes breach and triggers remedies herein.
3.2 Where required by destination or commodity, Carrier shall provide fumigation certificate, disinfection records and other sanitary documentation prior to loading. Certificates must comply with standards of Państwowa Inspekcja Ochrony Roślin i Nasiennictwa and other applicable authorities.
3.3 Phytosanitary controls and compliance with inspection requirements are the responsibility of Client and Carrier jointly during loading. Carrier shall not accept cargo that violates sanitary controls or is presented without required certificates.

4. Weighing and Weight Tolerance
4.1 All loads shall be weighed at origin and at destination on certified scales. The net weight recorded on the CMR or list przewozowy shall be final for invoicing subject to a permitted tolerance of 0.5% between origin and destination weights.
4.2 Discrepancies beyond tolerance shall be investigated and if attributable to Carrier or improper loading, Carrier bears resulting costs; if attributable to Client, Client shall indemnify Carrier.

5. Documentation, Insurance and Regulatory Compliance
5.1 Each transport shall be accompanied by CMR or applicable list przewozowy, zlecenie transportowe, weighbridge receipts, and sanitary/phytosanitary certificates as required. Faktura will be issued by LogiCore after delivery.
5.2 Carrier maintains valid liability insurance, cargo insurance and vehicle insurance in amounts customary for the sector and compliant with UTK, GITD and other regulatory requirements. Certificates of insurance available on request.

6. Penalties and Liability
6.1 Time-critical harvest deliveries: late delivery during harvest period (July–October) shall incur a contractual penalty of EUR 100 per late delivery event when delay is attributable to Carrier and results in measurable loss or spoilage. Timeliness is essential during harvest logistics.
6.2 Contamination: if contamination or cross-contamination occurs due to Carrier fault or failure to deliver clean trailers, Carrier shall pay a penalty of EUR 5,000 per event and shall be responsible for remediation, replacement of contaminated commodity and related costs.
6.3 Aggregate liability shall be subject to mandatory limits under Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny; parties acknowledge that penalties stated are contractual and without prejudice to statutory claims for proven damages exceeding those amounts.

7. Payment Terms and Annual Value
7.1 Annual estimated value of services under this Agreement is EUR 290,000. This estimate is variable and may be adjusted according to actual harvest volume and agreed rates.
7.2 Invoicing: LogiCore will issue faktura within 7 days after delivery and signature of CMR/list przewozowy. Payment terms are 30 days netto from the date of CMR signature. Seasonal prepayment option: Client may request a prepayment facility for harvest peak; terms to be agreed in writing and documented as advance on account against future faktury.

8. Force Majeure and Operational Interruptions
8.1 Events beyond reasonable control including extreme weather, natural disasters, strikes, governmental measures, quarantine restrictions, or extraordinary traffic bans shall be treated as force majeure. Affected party shall notify the other without undue delay.

9. Term, Termination and Amendments
9.1 This Agreement enters into force on signature and continues for 12 months, renewable automatically unless terminated with 60 days' notice prior to renewal. Immediate termination for material breach including repeated contamination or failure to comply with phytosanitary rules.

10. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
10.1 This Agreement governed by the laws of Poland, with reference to Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe. Disputes shall be resolved by negotiation and if unresolved, by common courts competent for Warsaw or by arbitration if agreed. Regulatory matters subject to oversight by UTK, GITD, UOKiK and relevant inspection authorities.

11. Miscellaneous
11.1 Notices in writing by email and confirmed by post. Annexes: A — vehicles and technical specs; B — rate card and harvest surcharges; C — sample fumigation certificate and phytosanitary checklist.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. .......................... Date: ______
For AgroTrans Wielkopolska Sp. z o.o. .......................... Date: ______

End of Agreement CTR-2025-009

title: Agreement for Museum Art and Exhibition Logistics Services — MuseumTrans Art and Exhibition Logistics (CTR-2025-010)
text:
Agreement No.: CTR-2025-010
Date: _______________
PARTIES
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with its registered office in Warszawa, ul. __________________, NIP __________________, hereinafter “LogiCore” (operator and carrier), with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań;
2. Muzeum Narodowe w Warszawie, represented by MuseumTrans Sp. z o.o. for logistics coordination, hereinafter “Museum” (shipper/consignor);
Together the “Parties”.
RECITALS
A. The Parties wish to establish terms under which LogiCore will provide specialised museum-grade transport, packing, handling and temporary import/export support for artworks, sculptures and historical artifacts for national and international exhibitions (Poland and EU: Berlin, Prague, Vienna, Budapest and other points).
B. This Agreement governs carriage contracts, ancillary services and obligations consistent with applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and EU regulations where applicable. Relevant authorities include UTK, GITD and UOKiK.
AGREEMENT
1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
1.1 LogiCore shall provide white-glove handling, museum-standard custom crating, climate-controlled transport, vibration-dampened vehicles, condition reporting, GPS tracking and security measures for items listed in each zlecenie transportowe. Services include loading, securing, transport, temporary storage at LogiCore depots and delivery to exhibition venues.
1.2 Routes include domestic inter-museum loans across Poland and international routes to/from Berlin, Prague, Vienna, Budapest and other EU locations as specified per shipment.
2. PACKING, CRATING AND CONDITION REPORTS
2.1 LogiCore will design and supply museum-standard wooden crates with foam inlay and archival materials where required. Crates will meet conservation specifications and be marked with handling instructions.
2.2 Condition reports (protokół stanu) completed jointly with Museum representatives or Museum-appointed conservator shall be made at pickup and at delivery. All reports form an integral part of the transport documentation (CMR / list przewozowy and attachment).
3. CLIMATE CONTROL, VIBRATION AND VEHICLE SPECIFICATIONS
3.1 Vehicles shall maintain 18–22°C and 45–55% relative humidity during transit and when stationary in vehicle during operational stops. Environmental parameters shall be logged continuously and retained for a minimum of 24 months.
3.2 Vehicles shall be equipped with vibration-dampening suspension and interior fixation systems suitable for fine art and sculpture.
4. SECURITY AND ESCORTS
4.1 All shipments shall be fitted with GPS tracking with real-time monitoring. Compartment seals shall be applied and recorded on list przewozowy/CMR.
4.2 For items with declared value exceeding EUR 100,000 an armed or unarmed escort (as agreed) will accompany the shipment at sender's or LogiCore's expense as specified in zlecenie transportowe. LogiCore shall provide seat in the cab for a Museum courier when a museum courier accompanies high-value shipments.
5. COURIERS
5.1 Museum may appoint a museum courier to accompany shipments. LogiCore will provide conveyance and a seat in the cab and will cooperate in onboard conditions (climate, rest breaks) necessary for the courier to perform duties.
6. INSURANCE, DECLARED VALUE AND LIABILITY
6.1 Insurance: Nail-to-nail insurance coverage shall be procured by LogiCore (or by Museum if agreed) for all shipments, covering loss and damage from collection to return. Policy limits shall be declared per item in zlecenie transportowe; certain items have declared values in excess of EUR 1,000,000.
6.2 Liability: For damage to artworks, LogiCore agrees a contractual penalty of EUR 10,000 per damaged artwork for proven damage not caused by gross negligence or willful misconduct. In cases of negligence, LogiCore shall be liable for full restoration costs and market value replacement without monetary cap where fault is established. This clause supplements and does not limit rights under Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe.
6.3 Nail-to-nail: Coverage commences at physical acceptance (pickup) and continues until delivery and formal release. Exclusions must be notified in writing and conform to statutory exclusions.
7. CUSTOMS, ATA CARNET AND TEMPORARY IMPORT/EXPORT
7.1 LogiCore will handle ATA Carnet preparation and carnet formalities for temporary export/import for exhibitions. LogiCore will act as agent for customs clearance on terms set out in each zlecenie transportowe and will comply with applicable customs law and EU practices.
8. DOCUMENTATION
8.1 Each shipment will be accompanied by zlecenie transportowe, CMR or list przewozowy, condition reports, declared values, insurance certificates, and ATA Carnet where applicable.
9. COMPENSATION, INVOICING AND ANNUAL VALUE
9.1 The Parties estimate an annual contract value of EUR 180,000 reflecting low volume but very high-value items. Specific fees per shipment, escorts, custom crating and insurance are set out in price annex A and confirmed per booking.
9.2 LogiCore will issue faktura in EUR for international legs and PLN for domestic legs as required by tax rules. Payment terms: 30 days from date of faktura unless otherwise agreed in writing.
10. COMPLIANCE
10.1 LogiCore warrants compliance with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny, and will ensure drivers and vehicles meet UTK and GITD requirements. Anti-corruption, data protection and export controls must be observed.
11. REPORTS, AUDIT AND MAINTENANCE OF RECORDS
11.1 LogiCore shall retain continuous environmental logs, GPS traces, condition reports and transport documentation for a minimum of 5 years and provide copies to Museum upon reasonable request.
12. FORCE MAJEURE
12.1 Neither Party shall be liable for delayed performance due to force majeure as defined by Kodeks Cywilny. The affected Party shall notify the other immediately.
13. TERMINATION
13.1 Either Party may terminate for material breach after 30 days' cure period. Termination does not affect liability for damage or obligations accrued prior to termination.
14. APPLICABLE LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION
14.1 This Agreement is governed by Polish law. Disputes shall be referred to the competent courts in Warszawa unless Parties agree to mediation or arbitration.
15. FINAL PROVISIONS
15.1 Modifications must be in writing. Annexes (price annex, insurance certificates, vehicle specifications) form part of this Agreement.
Signatures:
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
For Muzeum Narodowe w Warszawie (via MuseumTrans Sp. z o.o.): ____________________
Attachments: Annex A — Price Schedule; Annex B — Vehicle & Crate Specifications; Annex C — Insurance Cover Notes; Sample zlecenie transportowe and condition report template.

title: EventPro Concert and Exhibition Logistics Agreement (CTR-2025-011) between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and EventPro Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-011
EventPro Concert and Exhibition Logistics Agreement

This Agreement is made on 15 March 2025 between:
1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 88, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland ("LogiCore"), registered in KRS, VAT: PL…; and
2) EventPro Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Wschodnia 12, 01-234 Warszawa, Poland ("Client" or "EventPro").

Preamble
LogiCore is a logistics operator with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań and specialist event logistics capability. EventPro organises concerts, exhibitions and trade shows requiring time-critical transport, setup and inter-event storage. The Parties agree the following terms for provision by LogiCore of transport, loading/unloading, staging and storage services for concert equipment, trade show booths, staging, lighting rigs and related materials.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall perform transport and on-site logistics (zlecenie transportowe) for event equipment including but not limited to concert equipment, trade show booths, staging, lighting rigs and ancillary fixtures ("Goods"). Services include collection, secure transport, provision of crew for loading/unloading, delivery into venue, assembly assistance where agreed, and interim storage in heated space.
1.2 Services shall be provided for major venues across Poland including Tauron Arena Kraków, Atlas Arena Łódź and PGE Narodowy Warszawa and other venues agreed in writing.
1.3 Time-critical obligations: venue access windows and show start times are absolute. Deadlines provided in each zlecenie are critical. Failure to meet absolute deadlines shall incur penalties as set out in this Agreement.

2. Vehicles, Equipment and Crew
2.1 LogiCore will supply vehicles suitable for event logistics: 40-foot trailers, tail-lift trucks and low-loader vehicles for heavy staging. All vehicles comply with Polish vehicle standards and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Certificates and inspections to be available on request.
2.2 Standard crew per shipment: driver + 1 helper provided by LogiCore for loading/unloading on site. Additional crew can be supplied by request and priced separately.
2.3 Night and weekend operations are standard; Parties acknowledge events typically on Saturday/Sunday and LogiCore will provide 24/7 readiness for agreed events.

3. Timeframes and Access
3.1 Client shall provide venue access windows, load-in/load-out schedules and detailed floor plans at least 7 calendar days prior to each move. For last-minute changes, Client accepts potential surcharge.
3.2 Absolute deadlines: Any lateness that risks or causes delay to venue show start times constitutes breach. LogiCore's crew standby cost for delays is EUR 2,000 per hour of confirmed delay beyond agreed access window. If a show is delayed due to LogiCore logistics fault, a penalty of EUR 5,000 shall be payable by LogiCore to Client per occurrence, without prejudice to Client's right to claim further proven damages under Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe where applicable.

4. Penalties and Liability
4.1 Penalties specified above (EUR 2,000/hour standby; EUR 5,000 show-delay) are liquidated damages agreed by the Parties. Payment of penalties does not waive Client's right to withhold payments for non-performance or claim additional direct losses.
4.2 Insurance: LogiCore shall maintain transport insurance covering Goods up to EUR 300,000 per shipment and shall issue relevant insurance certificates upon request. Liability for loss or damage follows provisions of CMR where applicable and Prawo Przewozowe; insofar as higher liability is required by law, Parties shall follow mandatory provisions.
4.3 Storage liability: LogiCore's liability for Goods in storage is limited to insurance coverage and shall exclude consequential losses except where caused by gross negligence or wilful misconduct. Client must declare full value of high‑risk items prior to loading.

5. Storage
5.1 LogiCore provides 200 m² heated, secure storage for inter-event equipment at its Warszawa depot (or other depot by agreement). Storage terms, access, and handling to be agreed per magazynowanie order. Monthly storage rate to be set in price annex.

6. Rates, Payment and Invoicing
6.1 Annual contracted value of services is estimated at EUR 420,000 with seasonal peaks in summer festival season. Specific zlecenia will set price per load. Additional charges for overtime, night work, road tolls, permits, and extraordinary fuel surcharges apply.
6.2 Invoices (faktura) to be issued monthly in EUR, payable within 14 days net. Late payment interest per Kodeks Cywilny applies. LogiCore may require advance payment for major festival sequences.

7. Compliance and Permits
7.1 LogiCore warrants compliance with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Prawo Przewozowe, and applicable EU regulations. Vehicles and crew comply with GITD and UTK requirements. Client is responsible for obtaining event permits, venue-specific authorisations and for compliance with venue regulations. LogiCore will assist with transport-related permits on request.
7.2 Both Parties shall comply with UOKiK rules on competition and data protection obligations when handling personal data.

8. Force Majeure
Extraordinary events such as acts of God, strikes, epidemic, war, or official restrictions that prevent performance relieve the affected Party from liability for the period of impediment. The affected Party must notify the other promptly and mitigate effects.

9. Term and Termination
9.1 Term: 12 months from execution, renewable by written agreement. Either Party may terminate for material breach if not remedied within 14 days after written notice.

10. Confidentiality, Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
10.1 Parties shall keep confidential commercial terms and technical specifications. 10.2 Governing law: Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be resolved amicably; failing that, by arbitration in Warszawa under Polish law or by common courts of Warszawa if requested by the claimant.

11. Notices and Execution
All notices in writing to registered addresses. Performance orders issued as zlecenie transportowe shall reference CTR-2025-011 and specify venue, access windows, list przewozowy/CMR details.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.                       For EventPro Polska Sp. z o.o.
Name: _____________________                  Name: _____________________
Title: ______________________                 Title: ______________________
Date: _______________________                Date: _______________________

Annexes: Price Annex, Depot Addresses, Insurance Certificate Sample, Standard Zlecenie Transportowe Form.

title: E-Fulfillment Last-Mile Delivery Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and ShopNow.pl Sp. z o.o.
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-012
E-Fulfillment Last-Mile Delivery Agreement

This Agreement (Umowa) is made on this day between:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań and a fulfillment point in Piotrków Trybunalski ("LogiCore"), and
ShopNow.pl Sp. z o.o., an e-commerce platform with headquarters in Warszawa ("ShopNow").
Collectively: the Parties.

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in transport, logistics and last-mile distribution services. ShopNow operates an e-commerce marketplace and requires e-fulfillment and last-mile delivery services for parcels dispatched from the fulfillment center in Piotrków Trybunalski to end consumers throughout Poland. The Parties agree the terms below pursuant to applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and under the supervision of relevant authorities (UTK, GITD, UOKiK as applicable).

1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall provide last-mile delivery services for ShopNow parcels (list przewozowy/dowód nadania) originating from the fulfillment center in Piotrków Trybunalski to end consumers across all 16 voivodeships (województwa) of Poland.
1.2 Coverage and SLA: LogiCore guarantees next-day delivery (standard) to at least 90% of domestic addresses in Poland. Express (same-day) delivery is offered in major cities (Warszawa, Kraków, Łódź, Wrocław, Poznań, Gdańsk, Katowice) subject to cut-off times. Scheduled delivery with a 2-hour time window is available as an option.
1.3 Delivery options, definitions and target times:
- Standard: Next-day delivery target (price EUR 4.50/parcel).
- Express: Same-day delivery in major cities (price EUR 8.50/parcel).
- Scheduled: 2-hour delivery window (additional fee agreed by separate price list).

2. Volumes and Annual Value
2.1 Forecast volumes: 5,000–8,000 parcels/day during normal operation; anticipated peaks of 15,000+ parcels/day during Black Friday, Cyber Week, Christmas and similar events.
2.2 Annual contract value is estimated at EUR 1,800,000 based on expected volumes and per-parcel pricing. Final settlement is volume-based and invoiced monthly (faktura).

3. Capacity and Peak Season
3.1 LogiCore shall provide necessary capacity, including temporary driver hiring and engagement of a vetted subcontractor network, to meet peak volumes. LogiCore remains responsible for service levels and compliance with Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym even when subcontractors perform services.
3.2 ShopNow shall provide weekly forecasts and immediate escalation notices for promotional events. LogiCore will mobilize additional resources upon receipt of confirmed zlecenie transportowe.

4. Performance, POD and IT Integration
4.1 Proof of Delivery (POD) requires a photo of delivered parcel plus recipient signature captured on LogiCore's mobile application. Electronic signatures and photos constitute prima facie evidence of delivery for invoicing and dispute resolution.
4.2 Real-time status updates (pickup, en route, out for delivery, delivered, failed attempt, return collected) will be pushed via API to ShopNow's system. Parties will agree API specification and SLA for message delivery.

5. Returns and Cash on Delivery (pobranie)
5.1 Returns handling: Customer returns will be collected at the consumer's address, consolidated and returned to the fulfillment center in Piotrków Trybunalski within 48 hours from pickup confirmation where geographically feasible. Returns tracking and POD apply as for outbound parcels.
5.2 Cash on delivery (pobranie) handling is included. Collections shall be remitted to ShopNow by daily settlement, net of agreed fees, with detailed settlement reports accompanying daily faktura or reconciliation file.

6. Penalties and Liability
6.1 Service credits/penalties apply as follows (expressed in EUR):
- Failed first delivery attempt: EUR 3.00 per parcel (when a scheduled/attempted delivery is not executed as per agreed SLA without prior notice).
- Late delivery: EUR 10.00 per parcel for any parcel delivered more than 2 calendar days beyond the agreed delivery target for the chosen service level.
- Lost parcel: EUR 1,000.00 per parcel (applies where parcel cannot be located and liability is established under the Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny). Insurance and declared value procedures will be agreed in Appendix A.
6.2 Limits of liability: Except as expressly provided (lost parcel above), LogiCore's liability shall be limited to direct damages and subject to statutory limits under Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny. Indirect damages, lost profit and reputational damage are excluded to the extent permitted by law.

7. Pricing, Invoicing and Settlement
7.1 Unit pricing: EUR 4.50 per standard parcel; EUR 8.50 per express parcel. Other services (scheduled windows, reverse logistics beyond standard returns) are priced per Appendix B.
7.2 Invoicing: LogiCore shall issue monthly faktura in EUR. Payments due within 21 days net. Daily settlements for pobranie remittances will be reported daily and settled by LogiCore to ShopNow per agreed procedure.

8. Subcontractors and Compliance
8.1 LogiCore may use subcontractors (including during peaks) but remains fully responsible for their performance and for compliance with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, GITD and UTK regulations. Subcontractors shall be bound by confidentiality and data protection obligations consistent with GDPR and Polish law.

9. Data Protection and Confidentiality
9.1 The Parties will process personal data in accordance with GDPR. Delivery data, recipient details and POD images are to be used solely for performance of this Agreement and returned to ShopNow on request.

10. Term, Termination and Dispute Resolution
10.1 Term: This Agreement is effective as of the signature date and continues for an initial term of 24 months, renewable by mutual written agreement.
10.2 Termination: Material breach not cured within 30 days may lead to termination. Immediate termination for insolvency or repeated safety violations is permitted.
10.3 Governing law and jurisdiction: Polish law applies. Disputes shall be referred to the common courts in Warszawa unless Parties agree mediation. Applicable statutory provisions include Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.

11. Miscellaneous
11.1 Notices: Written notices to the registered offices or via agreed electronic addresses. Electronic messages for operational matters shall be sent via the API or designated operational channels.
11.2 Force Majeure: Performance affected by force majeure (including strikes, extraordinary weather, regulatory actions) shall be excused for the duration; Parties will cooperate to mitigate effects.
11.3 Entire Agreement: This Agreement, Appendices (A: insurance/declared value, B: price list, C: API spec) and accepted zlecenie transportowe constitute the entire agreement.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.:
Name: ____________________
Title: ____________________
Date: ____________________

For ShopNow.pl Sp. z o.o.:
Name: ____________________
Title: ____________________
Date: ____________________

title: Agreement for Transport and Management of Industrial Waste (WasteManagement Industrial Waste Transport)
text:
Agreement No. CTR-2025-013

Agreement for Transport and Management of Industrial Waste between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and EkoRecykling Sp. z o.o.

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., registered seat Warszawa, depots Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw, Poznan, acting as carrier and service provider (hereinafter LogiCore).
2. EkoRecykling Sp. z o.o., registered seat Katowice, acting as principal and waste holder (hereinafter EkoRecykling).

Preamble
This agreement is concluded pursuant to the Civil Code (Kodeks Cywilny), the Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and with respect to waste management obligations under the Ustawa o odpadach and related secondary legislation. Parties acknowledge obligations under BDO (Baza Danych o Produktach i Opakowaniach, Opakowania i Gospodarce Odpadami) and agree to cooperate to ensure full regulatory compliance.

Scope
3. Subject matter: transport, handling and delivery of industrial waste generated by EkoRecykling factory points in Silesia to recycling and processing facilities designated by EkoRecykling or approved third parties.
4. Waste types: packaging waste, scrap metal, used oils, electronic waste (WEEE). Hazardous fractions when present shall be handled in accordance with ADR requirements.
5. Vehicle types: skip containers, roll-on/roll-off vehicles, ADR-certified vehicles for hazardous loads, and any specialist equipment required for safe loading and unloading.

Term and Annual Value
6. Term: 12 months commencing 1 April 2025, renewable by agreement. Annual estimated value of services: EUR 340,000. Prices and fees set out in Annex A.

Operational Requirements
7. Collection points: regular collections from factory collection points across Silesia to recycling/processing facilities specified in Annex B. Regular scheduling to be weekly or fortnightly per site as agreed by site schedule in Annex C.
8. On-call spill response: LogiCore shall provide on-call response for spill or incident response 24/7, mobilization within agreed SLA per Annex D.

Documentation and Regulatory Compliance
9. BDO Registration: EkoRecykling shall ensure its BDO registration is current and provide LogiCore with BDO numbers and any necessary authorizations prior to first collection. LogiCore shall maintain its own required registrations and permits.
10. Transport documents: every shipment shall be accompanied by a Karta przekazania odpadu (KPO) and, where applicable, a CMR or list przewozowy. For hazardous wastes ADR documentation and declarations are mandatory.
11. KPO requirement: a Karta przekazania odpadu shall be completed at collection and signed by both parties. Incomplete documentation shall trigger penalties as set forth below.

Weighing and Reconciliation
12. Weighing: all loads shall be weighed at collection and at delivery. Gross and tare weights shall be recorded on the KPO and on the carrier s weighbridge ticket. Differences greater than 2% shall be investigated immediately.
13. Monthly reconciliation: LogiCore shall deliver a monthly KPO reconciliation report listing KPOs, weights, waste codes, recipient facility and BDO confirmation. Parties shall reconcile within 14 days of report delivery.

Safety, Environmental Standards and Penalties
14. Safety: all transport operations shall comply with Ustawa o odpadach, ADR, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and applicable UTK and GITD guidance. Drivers shall be trained in waste handling and spill response.
15. Insurance: LogiCore shall maintain liability insurance covering environmental incidents and third party damages. Certificates to be supplied annually or upon request.
16. Penalties: a) EUR 500 per shipment for incomplete or missing documentation (KPO, BDO, CMR/list przewozowy) attributable to LogiCore. b) EUR 2,000 per environmental incident proven to be caused by LogiCore s negligence, in addition to remediation costs and regulatory fines imposed by competent authorities (including UOKiK where applicable).

Prices, Invoicing and Payment
17. Prices per service and surcharges are in Annex A. Invoices (faktura) issued monthly based on reconciled KPOs. Payment term: 30 days net. Disputed amounts to be notified within 14 days.

Audit and Regulatory Cooperation
18. Parties shall cooperate in audits and inspections by regulatory bodies including UTK, GITD and waste authorities. Records shall be retained for minimum 5 years.

Force Majeure and Termination
19. Force majeure: events beyond reasonable control relieve performance for duration of event. Affected party shall notify within 48 hours.
20. Termination: either party may terminate for material breach after 30 days cure period. Immediate termination permitted for repeated regulatory non-compliance or significant environmental harm.

Liability
21. Liability governed by applicable Polish law. Parties liability limited to direct damages except in cases of gross negligence, willful misconduct or breach of environmental law.

Miscellaneous
22. Amendments in writing. Disputes resolved by courts in Warszawa unless parties agree mediation. This agreement drafted in English with Polish legal and operational terms; in case of conflict Polish law applies.

Signatures

Position: __________________
Date: _____________________

For EkoRecykling Sp. z o.o.
Name: ____________________
Position: __________________
Date: _____________________

title: DefenseLogistics Military Equipment Transport Agreement — CTR-2025-014 between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Polska Grupa Zbrojeniowa S.A. (MON Framework)
text:
CONFIDENTIAL — All pages and ancillary documentation of this Agreement are marked CONFIDENTIAL. Unauthorized disclosure is prohibited.

1. Parties and Scope
This Transport Agreement (Agreement) CTR-2025-014 is entered into by and between LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with headquarters in Warszawa and depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań ("LogiCore"), and Polska Grupa Zbrojeniowa S.A., acting under the Ministry of National Defence (MON) framework ("PGZ"), collectively "Parties". The Agreement governs carriage, handling and related services for non-classified military equipment, spare parts, uniforms and provisions ("Goods"). The Parties acknowledge that this Agreement is a framework arrangement; individual transports will be executed by separate zlecenie transportowe issued under this framework.

2. Value, Term and Orders
This framework has an annual value estimated at EUR 750,000. The Agreement term commences on the Effective Date and remains in force for 24 months unless terminated earlier pursuant to this Agreement. Individual shipments shall be confirmed by zlecenie transportowe issued by PGZ specifying CMR/list przewozowy details, route grid references, weight and dimensions.

3. Confidentiality and Marking
All documentation, manifests, zlecenie transportowe, CMR and lists przewozowy, packing lists and communications shall be clearly marked "CONFIDENTIAL". Parties shall comply with Kodeks Cywilny obligations regarding confidentiality and data protection. Unauthorized disclosure to third parties is a material breach and grounds for immediate termination.

4. Security Clearances and Personnel
4.1 Drivers and personnel engaged by LogiCore for carriage under this Agreement must hold Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa at a minimum level of POUFNE. Copies of valid Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa shall be provided to PGZ upon request.
4.2 No person without an appropriate clearance shall have access to Goods, documentation or classified route information. Access by unauthorized persons is a material breach and subjects LogiCore to immediate contract termination and penalties.

5. Vehicles and Equipment Requirements
5.1 All transports shall be performed only by LogiCore-owned vehicles. Subcontracting or leasing of vehicles or drivers is strictly prohibited.
5.2 Vehicles must be fitted with military-spec tie-down points and restraint systems compatible with PGZ requirements. Vehicles must be maintained in accordance with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and relevant GITD/UTK guidance.
5.3 GPS tracking devices may be disabled upon PGZ request for a given shipment or segment; procedures for safe removal/disablement must be agreed in writing in advance and compliance documented. Vehicles must be capable of radio silence and electromagnetic emission control when required by PGZ.

6. Routes and Routing Confidentiality
The Parties acknowledge that routes involve military bases and installations across Poland. Exact locations are classified and will be communicated by PGZ through secure channels using grid references only. LogiCore shall not retain or reproduce route coordinates outside secure systems, and any retention must be approved by MON.

7. Escorts, Notifications and Oversized Loads
7.1 Convoys consisting of more than three (3) vehicles shall be accompanied by military escort provided by PGZ or MON. LogiCore shall coordinate convoy composition and timings with PGZ.
7.2 Oversized or ADR loads require police notification and compliance with relevant permits; LogiCore shall notify local Policja and coordinate any required convoy police support in accordance with Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.

8. Insurance and Liability
8.1 PGZ acknowledges government self-insurance for state property where applicable. Notwithstanding, LogiCore shall maintain commercial general liability insurance in the amount of EUR 2,000,000 per occurrence and present current policies on request.
8.2 Parties' liability is subject to limitations set by Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe except where otherwise agreed. Loss or damage resulting from LogiCore negligence, breach of security clearance obligations or unauthorized access will render LogiCore liable for direct damages and contractual penalties.

9. Penalties and Termination
9.1 A breach of security obligations, including but not limited to disclosure of routes, failure to ensure drivers hold required Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa, or allowing unauthorized personnel access to Goods or documentation, incurs an immediate contractual penalty of EUR 10,000 per breach and may lead to immediate termination for cause by PGZ.
9.2 PGZ reserves the right to terminate the Agreement with immediate effect upon any security breach, loss of control of Goods, or evidence of unauthorized subcontracting.

10. Inspections and Compliance
10.1 Żandarmeria Wojskowa (ŻW) shall have the right to conduct random vehicle and load inspections at origin, during staging and at destination without prior notice. LogiCore shall cooperate fully and facilitate inspections.
10.2 LogiCore shall comply with UTK, GITD and applicable instructions from MON and ŻW. LogiCore shall keep records for each zlecenie transportowe, including crew manifests, Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa copies, vehicle maintenance, CMR/list przewozowy and inspection logs.

11. Operational Procedures
11.1 Each zlecenie transportowe will specify load characteristics, grid-reference origin and destination, required clearance level, escort requirements and any GPS/radio restrictions. LogiCore shall confirm receipt and availability within agreed SLA.
11.2 Loading/unloading procedures will be agreed in advance. LogiCore drivers shall follow ŻW and PGZ instructions at all times while on military premises. Any deviation must be immediately reported to PGZ contact specified in the zlecenie transportowe.

12. Regulatory and Contractual Compliance
LogiCore declares it will perform services in accordance with Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and applicable EU regulations where relevant. Matters relating to competition law shall comply with UOKiK rules; any suspicion of anti-competitive behavior will be reported.

13. Force Majeure
Events beyond the Parties' reasonable control (force majeure) relieve performance obligations to the extent prevented, provided prompt notice is given. Security incidents, embargoes or orders by MON/ŻW shall be treated as sovereign instructions.

14. Records, Audit and Reporting
LogiCore shall maintain confidential records of all zlecenie transportowe and make them available for audit by PGZ, MON or their authorised representatives subject to applicable security procedures.

15. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be resolved by negotiation; failing resolution, disputes shall be referred to the common courts of Warszawa unless security considerations require alternate arrangements.

16. Miscellaneous
16.1 No subcontracting is permitted. All transports under this Agreement shall be performed on LogiCore-owned and registered vehicles by drivers cleared to the required level.
16.2 Any amendment must be in writing and marked CONFIDENTIAL.

Signatures
Executed by authorised representatives:
