title: AeroParts: Aerospace Component Transport Agreement
text:
This Transport Agreement (Contract No. CTR-2025-015) is entered into on this 1st day of March 2025 by and between:

LogiCore Sp. z o.o., a company incorporated under Polish law, with registered office in Warszawa and depots in Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw and Poznan (hereinafter LogiCore), an AS9100 certified logistics provider; and

PZL Mielec, a Lockheed Martin subsidiary, with principal operations in Mielec, Podkarpackie (hereinafter PZL Mielec or the Customer).

Preamble
LogiCore and PZL Mielec enter this Agreement for the transport, handling and logistics services of aerospace components, including but not limited to fuselage sections, avionics modules and landing gear assemblies (hereinafter the Goods). The Parties agree to perform services in accordance with the terms set below, applicable Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe), and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, as well as relevant international rules (CMR for road carriage where applicable). Regulatory authorities applicable include UTK, GITD and UOKiK.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Transport and handling between PZL Mielec (Podkarpackie) and the following nominated destinations: Kraków Airport (KRK), Rzeszów rail head, and other EU destinations as agreed on per zlecenie transportowe. International consignments shall be invoiced in EUR; domestic Polish legs may be invoiced in PLN.
1.2 Goods include fuselage sections, avionics, and landing gear assemblies. Wing sections and other oversized elements are treated as Oversized Loads per clause 4.
1.3 LogiCore shall provide end-to-end logistics including documentation, customs support, secure storage, onward carriage, and delivery to nominated consignee.

2. Quality, Certification and Compliance
2.1 LogiCore warrants it holds and will maintain AS9100 certification for the duration of this Agreement. All transport and handling shall meet aerospace industry standards.
2.2 Cleanroom-equivalent packaging during transport: all Goods will be transported in packaging providing ISO Class 8 equivalent cleanliness, with anti-static wrapping and sealed containers appropriate for avionics and sensitive components.
2.3 Temperature control: for avionics requiring 15-25°C, LogiCore shall use validated temperature-controlled units and continuous temperature monitoring with alarms and documented excursions. Thresholds and acceptable holding times shall be agreed per shipment.
2.4 Chain of custody: each shipment shall include pre-transport inspection, written inspection report, digital photo documentation, and a chain of custody log (lista łańcucha dostaw). A CMR or list przewozowy shall accompany each international or domestic movement respectively.

3. Vibration Monitoring and Handling
3.1 Every shipment shall be fitted with calibrated accelerometers to monitor vibration and shock. Data shall be recorded continuously and delivered to PZL Mielec within 24 hours of delivery.
3.2 Vibration threshold: maximum peak acceleration 0.5g. Any exceedance shall be reported immediately and investigated.
3.3 Penalties for vibration exceedance are set at EUR 5,000 per exceedance event, payable by the Party whose acts or omissions caused the exceedance, subject to investigation and evidence.

4. Oversized Loads and Route Surveys
4.1 Wing sections and other oversized parts shall require detailed route surveys, escort arrangements, and special permitting. LogiCore shall coordinate obtaining all necessary permits (road permits, temporary restrictions) and arrange pilot vehicles and police escort where required under Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and GITD guidance.
4.2 For rail movements to Rzeszów rail head or intermodal transfers to Kraków Airport, LogiCore shall liaise with UTK and rail operators to ensure gauge, loading and securing are compliant.

5. Customs, Export Control and Documentation
5.1 Where Goods or subcomponents are U.S.-origin or otherwise subject to ITAR, LogiCore will comply with ITAR requirements and will not export, re-export or transfer without appropriate authorization. PZL Mielec shall provide End User Certificates (EUC) and necessary export licences prior to shipment.
5.2 LogiCore will assist in preparation of commercial invoices (faktura), customs declarations, EUR1 or other documents as required for EU destinations, and will maintain records for audits.

6. Insurance, Liability and Penalties
6.1 Agreed annual value of services under this Agreement is EUR 620,000. Parties acknowledge declared annual contract value for purposes of insurance and liability.
6.2 Insurance: LogiCore shall maintain cargo insurance covering total insured value of Goods in transit at market replacement value, and shall provide certificates on request. Liability for loss, damage or shortage shall be governed by this Agreement and applicable law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe), subject to the specific penalties below.
6.3 Damage penalty: in the event of verified damage to a single aerospace component (e.g. avionics, landing gear, fuselage section) attributable to LogiCore or its subcontractors, LogiCore shall be liable for a liquidated damage amount of EUR 50,000 per damaged component, not excluding recovery of proven additional direct costs and remedial actions.
6.4 Vibration and environmental exceedance penalties as set in clauses 3 and 5 shall be payable within 30 days of confirmed breach. Payments shall be made in EUR for international claims and may be invoiced in PLN for domestic claims with appropriate exchange rate as agreed.

7. Performance, Subcontracting and AS9100 Requirements
7.1 LogiCore may subcontract carriage segments but remains responsible for full performance and compliance with AS9100 processes. Subcontractors engaged on shipments of Goods shall be approved in writing and meet AS9100 or equivalent standards where required.
7.2 Pre-transport inspection and photo documentation shall be performed by an authorized LogiCore inspector and copies provided to PZL Mielec with the zlecenie transportowe.

8. Reports, Records and Audit
8.1 LogiCore shall maintain complete transport records, accelerometer logs, temperature logs, chain of custody and photographic evidence for a minimum of 5 years and shall permit audits by PZL Mielec or its nominated representatives. Records shall also be available for regulatory inspections by UTK, GITD or customs authorities.

9. Term, Termination and Force Majeure
9.1 Term: This Agreement is effective for one year from the date herein and may be renewed by mutual written agreement. Either Party may terminate for material breach after a 30-day cure period.
9.2 Force Majeure: delays due to force majeure events (as defined under Kodeks Cywilny) shall suspend obligations. Parties must notify each other promptly and mitigate impacts.

10. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
10.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be resolved by amicable negotiation; failing resolution, disputes shall be submitted to the competent court in Warszawa.

11. Notices and Invoicing
11.1 Notices shall be sent to the registered offices of the Parties. Invoices (faktura) shall reference contract CTR-2025-015 and relevant zlecenie transportowe numbers. Payment terms shall be net 30 days unless otherwise agreed.

12. Miscellaneous
12.1 Confidentiality: Parties shall maintain confidentiality of technical data, ITAR-controlled information and commercial terms. Use of data shall comply with applicable export controls.
12.2 Competition law: Parties shall comply with UOKiK and EU competition rules. No anti-competitive sharing of pricing or confidential third party information shall occur.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.                         For PZL Mielec
_____________________________                    _____________________________
Name:                                            Name:
Title:                                           Title:
Date:                                            Date:

Annexes (incorporated): Annex A – Approved packaging and ISO Class 8 equivalent requirements; Annex B – Accelerometer calibration and reporting protocol; Annex C – Oversize route survey template; Annex D – Sample chain of custody log; Annex E – EUC/Export control checklist.

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties concerning the transport of aerospace components under CTR-2025-015 and supersedes prior representations. All references to transport documents shall include CMR where applicable and list przewozowy for domestic rail/road as required.

title: LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Service Agreement for High‑Value Luxury Fashion Logistics (LuxuryBrands High‑Value Fashion Logistics)
text:
CONTRACT No. CTR-2025-016

Agreement between:
1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań, KRS/Tax ID as provided on zlecenie, hereinafter "Carrier"; and
2) LuxuryBrands Distribution Polska Sp. z o.o., hereinafter "Client".

Preamble
The parties conclude this transport and logistics agreement ("Agreement") for the carriage, handling and custody of high‑value designer clothing, watches and jewellery (collectively "Goods"), including garment‑on‑hanger (GOH) shipments and seasonal collection cycles. This Agreement is governed by the provisions of the Polish Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and related regulations, and oversight by UTK, GITD and UOKiK where applicable.

1. Scope of Services
Carrier shall provide end‑to‑end logistics services from Client's bonded warehouse in Warszawa to twenty‑five (25) fixed boutiques across Poland and to seasonal pop‑up store locations (routes to be specified in periodic zlecenie transportowe). Services include: specialized GOH transport with hanging rails; white‑glove boutique delivery; return collection of unsold stock at end of season to regional hubs; storage, handling, item‑level tracking and reconciliation at every handover.

2. High‑Value Shipments and Commercial Terms
Average shipment value shall be recorded at EUR 120,000 per shipment (for insurance and handling reference); annual contracted value EUR 350,000. Invoicing for domestic services shall be issued in PLN (faktura), with EUR amounts noted for valuation and insurance purposes. Seasonal collection schedule: four (4) major deliveries per year — Spring/Summer, Autumn/Winter, Cruise and Pre‑Fall.

3. Equipment and Performance Standards
Carrier shall supply temperature‑controlled, GOH‑fitted vehicles with hanging rails for garment transport. Boutique deliveries to be performed by white‑glove teams: suited driver and uniformed attendant, branded delivery van, presentation of list przewozowy / CMR where applicable. All routes and time windows shall be agreed in zlecenie transportowe; standard on‑time delivery target 95% per season.

4. Security, GPS and Escort
For all shipments Carrier will maintain sealed compartments, tamper‑evident seals, 24/7 GPS real‑time tracking and alarm monitoring. Jewellery consignments or shipments exceeding thresholds defined by Client require armed escort services contracted by Carrier or authorised subcontractor in compliance with Polish law (licenced security personnel), with prior notice. Any use of armed escort shall comply with Ustawa o ochronie osób i mienia and vehicle security standards, and shall be documented in the chain of custody.

5. Chain of Custody and Item‑Level Tracking
Carrier shall implement item‑level tracking using barcode and RFID technologies. Every handover (warehouse → vehicle → depot → boutique → return) shall be reconciled, with signed electronic proof of delivery (POD) and updated inventory in Client's portal. Discrepancies must be reported within 2 hours for in‑transit variances and within 24 hours for delivered discrepancies.

6. Liability, Insurance and Penalties
Carrier's liability for loss or damage to Goods equals the full retail value of the lost or damaged items as declared on the shipment manifest (without limitation), subject to timely notification. For delayed deliveries the penalty is EUR 500 per incident (to be invoiced/agreed and offset against Carrier fees) — EUR amounts are binding for penalty calculation and will be converted to PLN at the exchange rate on the invoice date. Parties agree minimum insurance coverage to reflect declared values; Client shall provide declared value per shipment on the CMR/list przewozowy.

7. Returns and Season Close‑Out
At the end of each season Carrier shall collect unsold stock from boutiques and pop‑ups and return to Client's designated regional hub (Katowice/Gdańsk/Wrocław/Poznań as instructed). Returns to be processed with item‑level reconciliation and graneled on receipt. Carrier to provide transport documentation and signed PODs for each return.

8. Subcontracting and Personnel
Carrier may subcontract transport services to vetted carriers provided they meet security, GOH and tracking requirements. All personnel performing deliveries shall be trained in white‑glove standards; drivers and escorts shall carry ID and authorisations. Carrier remains fully responsible under Kodeks Cywilny for subcontractor performance.

9. Documentation and Regulatory Compliance
Carrier shall issue appropriate transport documents (CMR or list przewozowy), maintain logbooks and electronic records in compliance with Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Carrier will cooperate with inspections by UTK and GITD and comply with anti‑monopoly provisions of UOKiK.

10. Term, Fees and Invoicing
Term: 12 months from signature unless earlier terminated. Fees for services and armoured escort shall be set out in Annex A. Domestic services invoiced in PLN (faktura), due net 30 days. Parties may agree escalation clauses for fuel and extraordinary security costs.

11. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
Polish law governs this Agreement. Disputes to be resolved by courts of competent jurisdiction in Warszawa. Parties will attempt amicable settlement prior to litigation or arbitration.

12. Final Provisions
Amendments require written annex signed by authorised representatives. Confidentiality of Client's designs and pricing shall be preserved. This Agreement supplements standard zlecenie transportowe terms and any CMR notes.

Signatures:
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________    Date: ______
For LuxuryBrands Distribution Polska Sp. z o.o.: ____________________    Date: ______

Annexes: A (Fees & Schedule), B (Route List: Warszawa bonded warehouse → 25 boutiques + seasonal pop‑ups), C (Security & Escort SOP), D (Item‑Level Tracking Spec).

title: BioLab Clinical Sample Transport Agreement (BioLab Clinical Sample Transport — CTR-2025-017)
text:
This Transport Agreement (Agreement, CTR-2025-017) is entered into on this day by and between:

LogiCore Sp. z o.o., a company organized under Polish law, with registered office in Warszawa, hereinafter LogiCore, with operational depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań, and

BioLab Diagnostyka Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, hereinafter BioLab.

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in specialized transport and logistics services. BioLab operates a central diagnostics laboratory in Warszawa receiving clinical samples (blood, tissue, and other biological specimens). The parties wish to contract for time-critical transport of laboratory/biospecimen material described below in compliance with applicable Polish and EU law, including the Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, ADR provisions for the transport of infectious substances, and relevant guidance from UTK, GITD and UOKiK.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Subject to the terms of this Agreement, LogiCore shall provide collection, transport and delivery services for clinical samples classified as UN 3373 Category B infectious substances (Category B, UN 3373) from one hundred twenty (120) collection points (hospitals, clinics and collection centres) across Mazowsze to BioLab's central laboratory in Warszawa.
1.2 Samples include whole blood, serum/plasma, tissue specimens and other biological material as listed on individual zlecenie transportowe and accompanying documentation. Transport is time-critical: all samples must be delivered to BioLab within four (4) hours of collection for viability unless otherwise agreed in writing for specific sample types.

2. Operational Requirements
2.1 Volume and Frequency: Estimated volume is 800–1,200 pickups per day, six (6) days per week. Exact routes and daily pickup schedules will be agreed and published in annex A (route manifest). LogiCore will allocate appropriate vehicles and personnel from its depot network.
2.2 Time Windows and SLA: LogiCore shall maintain service levels ensuring collection at agreed time windows and delivery within the four-hour viability window. Failure to meet agreed pickup windows constitutes a breach subject to penalties per clause 9.

3. Packaging, Temperature Control and Safety
3.1 Packaging: All samples must be transported in triple packaging compliant with ADR P650 and UN 3373 Category B requirements. Packaging must include a primary leak-proof receptacle, leak-proof secondary packaging with sufficient absorbent material, and an outer packaging rigid enough to prevent damage. Biohazard marking and CMR/list przewozowy must be visible on outer packaging.
3.2 Temperature: Two validated temperature profiles apply: ambient (15–25°C) and cold (2–8°C). LogiCore will use validated shipping containers and active/passive refrigerated units as required. Temperature validation reports and calibration certificates for containers and data loggers shall be provided upon request.
3.3 Security and Chain of Custody: LogiCore will maintain chain of custody records for each zlecenie transportowe and provide electronic pickup confirmation and delivery confirmation to BioLab LIMS via API (see clause 6). Drivers shall secure samples during transit and follow spill response and infection control procedures.

4. Personnel and Training
4.1 Drivers and couriers assigned by LogiCore shall be trained in handling UN 3373 Category B, ADR P650 packaging inspections, spill response, basic infection control, use of PPE, and patient confidentiality. Training records shall be maintained and made available to BioLab or regulatory authorities upon request.
4.2 LogiCore will ensure personnel comply with GITD and UTK requirements and hold any necessary permits and certifications under Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and national law.

5. Documentation and IT Integration
5.1 Each transport must be accompanied by a completed list przewozowy/CMR and the BioLab zlecenie transportowe. Chain of custody documentation must include timestamps, driver ID, vehicle ID, and packaging condition.
5.2 IT Integration: LogiCore will provide real-time pickup confirmation via LogiCore API to BioLab LIMS. API messages will include pickup time, GPS coordinates, driver identifier, container ID, temperature profile and digital photos of packaging where requested. Delivery confirmations and temperature logs will be transmitted upon receipt at the BioLab central laboratory.

6. Insurance, Liability and Indemnity
6.1 LogiCore shall maintain insurance appropriate to the risks, including motor third-party liability, cargo insurance covering biological materials, and professional liability, with limits appropriate to cover potential losses and third-party claims. Certificates of insurance will be provided to BioLab on signature and annually thereafter.
6.2 Liability: Subject to mandatory legal limitations under the Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe, LogiCore shall be liable for direct losses resulting from breach of contractual obligations, negligence, willful misconduct, or failure to comply with ADR/UN 3373 requirements. BioLab's aggregate remedies for loss or damage relating to transported samples shall be limited as set forth in this Agreement and the penalty schedule in clause 9.

7. Penalties and Remedies
7.1 The parties agree a liquidated damages regime for measured operational failures: EUR 200 per incident for late pickup beyond agreed pickup window resulting in delivery after the 4-hour viability limit; EUR 1,000 per lost or irretrievably damaged sample classified as irreplaceable; EUR 5,000 per confirmed packaging violation or failure to use ADR P650-compliant packaging.
7.2 Penalties are without prejudice to BioLab's right to seek additional damages where permitted by law and where losses exceed liquidated damages. LogiCore retains the right to contest penalties with documented evidence within five (5) business days.

8. Compliance and Audits
8.1 LogiCore shall comply with ADR, UN 3373, Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and guidance from UTK, GITD and UOKiK. Non-compliance that materially affects safety or sample integrity is a material breach.
8.2 BioLab may audit LogiCore operations, trainings, vehicles and documentation with reasonable notice. Urgent inspections may be conducted without notice where biohazard risk is suspected.

9. Confidentiality and Data Protection
9.1 Each party shall keep confidential commercial and personal data exchanged under this Agreement. Processing of personal data shall comply with Polish data protection law and GDPR; BioLab data holders remain data controllers for patient information.

10. Term, Termination and Force Majeure
10.1 Term: This Agreement commences on date of signature and has an initial term of 12 months, renewable by written agreement. Annual value is estimated at EUR 520,000 subject to actual monthly faktura reconciliation.
10.2 Termination: Either party may terminate for material breach if not cured within thirty (30) days. Immediate termination is permitted for willful safety violations or illegal conduct.
10.3 Force Majeure: Neither party is liable for delay caused by events beyond reasonable control (force majeure) in accordance with Polish law.

11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall first be subject to good faith negotiation; unresolved disputes shall be submitted to the competent courts in Warszawa unless the parties agree arbitration.

12. Miscellaneous
12.1 Amendments must be in writing. Parties will appoint operational contacts to manage daily service levels and API integration. Monthly operational reports will be exchanged and monthly invoices (faktura) issued in EUR unless otherwise agreed.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Name: ____________________ Title: ____________________ Date: __/__/2025

For BioLab Diagnostyka Sp. z o.o.
Name: ____________________ Title: ____________________ Date: __/__/2025

Annexes: A — Route manifest and pickup schedule; B — Packaging and validation certificates; C — API specification and data fields; D — Training matrix and records.

title: WindPower Oversized Renewable Energy Transport Agreement – CTR-2025-018
text:
Agreement No. CTR-2025-018

Agreement for Transport and On-Site Delivery of Oversized Renewable Energy Components
Between:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 10, 00-001 Warszawa, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter "Carrier" or "LogiCore")
and
WindPower Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Energetyczna 5, 80-000 Gdańsk (hereinafter "Customer" or "WindPower")

Preamble
This agreement governs the carriage, delivery and related services for oversized wind turbine components (blades, nacelles, tower sections) under Polish law, including but not limited to the Polish Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and relevant regulations and executive acts of UTK, GITD and UOKiK. Transportation shall be documented by CMR where applicable and by Polish list przewozowy / zlecenie transportowe for domestic legs.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall provide project-based transport services for wind turbine components: blades of up to 75 metres length, nacelles up to 90 tonnes, and tower sections of varying dimensions, from ports (Gdynia, Świnoujście) to wind farm sites across Pomorze, Warmia-Mazury and Zachodniopomorskie. Annual contract value is estimated at EUR 1,500,000, covering approximately 3 wind farm installations per year.
1.2 Services include route surveys, permit procurement, provision of specialized trailers and SPMTs, pilot vehicles and coordination with local authorities, and site delivery to appointed laydown areas.

2. Route Surveys and Preconditions
2.1 Prior to each transport, Carrier shall conduct route surveys (inspekcja trasy) that include bridge clearances, vertical and horizontal clearances, turning radii, overhead line heights, pavement bearing capacity and identified pinch points. Survey reports shall be delivered to Customer at least 14 business days before planned movement.
2.2 Customer shall provide site access coordinates, laydown area grading and any known local restrictions. Absent timely information from Customer, Carrier shall reserve right to adjust schedule and charge additional costs.

3. Permits and Escorts
3.1 Carrier shall obtain all required administrative permits, including zezwolenie na przejazd pojazdu nienormatywnego. Blades classified as Kategoria VII shall be transported only on routes and times authorized by competent road authorities. Costs for permits are included in the project price unless extraordinary charges are assessed by authorities after contract signature.
3.2 Escort requirements: a minimum of two pilot vehicles (pilot cars) per convoy are mandatory. Police escort shall be engaged in urban or high-risk sections where legally required. Certain sections designated by authorities shall be transported at night only; Carrier will coordinate night windows with Customer and authorities.

4. Vehicles, Trailers and Equipment
4.1 Carrier will supply extendable blade trailers (manufacturers: Goldhofer, Scheuerle or equivalent) for blades, low-loaders and specialized wagons for nacelles, and self-propelled modular transporters (SPMTs) for heavy lifts. All equipment shall conform to technical and safety standards and be certified for the proposed loads.
4.2 Crane interfaces and on-site lifting points shall be coordinated in writing. Any required specialist lifting equipment mobilization is a separate berth/installation service to be confirmed by Customer.

5. Schedule, Delays and Penalties
5.1 Agreed project schedules shall be specified in zlecenie transportowe issued per shipment. Timely arrival of crane/installation crews at site is critical. Carrier acknowledges liability for crane crew standby costs resulting from Carrier-caused delays and agrees to pay EUR 5,000 per day of verified crane crew standby caused by late delivery.
5.2 Damage to a blade due to Carrier negligence entitles Customer to contractual liquidated damages of EUR 100,000 per blade in addition to repair/replacement and insurance recoveries.

6. Pricing, Invoicing and Payment
6.1 This agreement contemplates project-based pricing within the annual volume. Carrier will issue faktura in EUR per shipment. Payment terms: 30 days net unless otherwise agreed in a purchase order.
6.2 Road repair and route restoration after heavy transport (including remediation of any damage caused by Carrier operations) is included in the contract price. Carrier shall remedy any damage within timelines specified by the local authority or reimburse Customer for remediation costs.

7. Insurance and Liability
7.1 Carrier shall maintain cargo insurance covering full replacement value for wind turbine components and third-party liability insurance in amounts customary for oversized transport in Poland and internationally. Copies of insurance certificates shall be provided upon request.
7.2 Carrier liability is subject to mandatory caps under applicable law except where higher contractual damages are stipulated (clause 5.2). Parties acknowledge public policy restrictions and statutory liability under Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe.

8. Environmental and Road Restoration
8.1 Carrier shall minimize environmental impact and comply with all environmental permits. After any movement affecting road surfaces or verges, Carrier shall undertake route restoration (naprawa drogi) and re-instatement of affected surfaces and vegetation. Such restoration work is included in the prices agreed per project.

9. Weather and Operational Restrictions
9.1 Transport of blades is prohibited in wind conditions exceeding 60 km/h. No transport shall take place in ice conditions or when local authorities restrict movements due to weather. Carrier shall monitor forecasts and notify Customer of any weather-related stoppages.

10. Force Majeure
10.1 Events outside reasonable control (siła wyższa) relieve affected party from performance for the duration. Parties shall promptly notify each other and take mitigation steps. Weather events causing delay shall be documented; commercial consequences are governed by clauses 5 and 6.

11. Termination and Suspension
11.1 Material breach, insolvency, or persistent failure to obtain necessary permits authorizes the non-breaching party to suspend or terminate after 14 days' cure notice.

12. Dispute Resolution and Applicable Law
12.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be settled by good faith negotiations and, if unresolved, by arbitration in Warsaw or by competent Polish courts where statutory matters apply.

13. Miscellaneous
13.1 Parties shall comply with UTK, GITD directives and competition law overseen by UOKiK. Documentation including CMR, list przewozowy, zlecenie transportowe, permits and survey reports shall be retained for statutory periods.

Execution
Signed for and on behalf of LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and WindPower Polska Sp. z o.o. Date and signatures to follow in final executed counterpart.

End of Agreement CTR-2025-018

title: HospitalSupply Healthcare Consumables Transport and Logistics Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and MedSupply Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
TRANSPORT AND LOGISTICS AGREEMENT
Contract No.: CTR-2025-019
Date: 01 March 2025
Parties:
1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 12, 00-590 Warszawa, depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań, hereinafter "LogiCore"; and
2) MedSupply Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Zdrowia 3, 00-001 Warszawa, hereinafter "Client".
Recitals
LogiCore provides transport and logistics services (logistyka transportowa) for medical supplies. Client requires transport from its central warehouse in Łódź to hospitals and clinics across central Poland. Parties wish to set out terms in compliance with applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and with respect to oversight by UTK, GITD and consumer authority UOKiK where applicable.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall perform transport of hospital consumable supplies including: gloves, syringes, bandages, PPE, cleaning supplies and office supplies (collectively "Consumables"). Sterile products (e.g. sterile surgical kits, sterile dressings) shall remain in sealed original packaging at all times; no repackaging, opening or alteration is permitted by carrier personnel.
1.2 Origin: Client central warehouse, Łódź. Destinations: up to 45 hospitals and clinics across central Poland (lista attachments and electronic zlecenie transportowe). Deliveries are to the hospital loading dock only. Internal distribution to wards is the responsibility of hospital porters; LogiCore drivers will not perform ward deliveries unless separately agreed in writing.
1.3 Each delivery must be reconciled with Client purchase order (PO) and zlecenie transportowe. Lot numbers and expiry dates for each sterile and batch-tracked item must be recorded and transmitted to the hospital's IT system or entered on the hospital list przewozowy at time of delivery.
2. Service Levels and Emergency Escalation
2.1 Routine deliveries: daily scheduled runs covering assigned hospitals per weekly rota.
2.2 Emergency deliveries: For critical items (surgical supplies, blood bags and other time-sensitive lifesaving goods designated by Client), LogiCore guarantees a 4-hour emergency delivery response time measured from Client notification to arrival at hospital loading dock within central Poland, subject to asset availability and traffic/force majeure exclusions.
2.3 Penalties: Late routine delivery: EUR 200 per incident. Late emergency delivery: EUR 2,000 per incident. In the event of loss or damage to product caused by LogiCore negligence, LogiCore shall replace affected product at its expense (product replacement) and reimburse reasonable documented costs incurred by Client as required by Kodeks Cywilny.
3. Tracking, Documentation and Compliance
3.1 Every consignment will be accompanied by a list przewozowy and electronic manifest linked to the Client PO. Lot numbers and serials must be recorded in the hospital system at point of delivery; driver must obtain signature and time stamp of receiving hospital representative.
3.2 For products requiring registration, Client represents and warrants that products are registered with Urząd Rejestracji Produktów Leczniczych (URPL) where applicable. LogiCore shall, upon reasonable request, provide transport documentation to support regulatory inspections by URPL, UTK, GITD or other competent authority.
3.3 Sterile product handling: All sterile items must be kept sealed in original packaging, protected from contamination, moisture and temperature excursions per manufacturers' instructions. No repackaging permitted. Any breach will be treated as damage and subject to product replacement and potential liability.
4. Pandemic and Surge Capacity
4.1 In the event of a pandemic, epidemic or public health emergency (including COVID-19 or successor events), LogiCore shall provide surge capacity up to +300% of contracted weekly volume for PPE, cleaning supplies and designated critical Consumables for a minimum 14-day rolling period, subject to mutual coordination. LogiCore shall prioritize emergency deliveries of critical items in coordination with Client.
4.2 PPE for drivers: LogiCore will supply appropriate PPE (masks, gloves, hand sanitizer) to all drivers and warehouse personnel when handling medical supplies and when required by public health guidance. LogiCore shall ensure staff vaccination and testing policies comply with Client site requirements where legally permissible.
5. Financials and Term
5.1 Annual value: The parties estimate the annual contract value at EUR 680,000. Charges, invoicing (faktura) and payment terms will follow Annex A rate card and standard 30-day net payment unless otherwise agreed. Domestic currency for other incidental charges shall be PLN as agreed in Annex A.
5.2 Insurance: LogiCore shall maintain cargo and liability insurance customary for transport of medical goods and shall provide certificates upon request.
6. Liability, Force Majeure and Governing Law
6.1 Liability limited as set out; LogiCore liable for proven direct damage caused by its breach, including product replacement. Indirect or consequential damages and loss of profit shall be limited to the value of the affected shipment, except for gross negligence or willful misconduct.
6.2 Force majeure (siła wyższa) such as natural disasters, war, pandemic restrictions, strikes shall suspend obligations to the extent affected. Parties will use reasonable efforts to mitigate impact and restore service.
6.3 Governing law: Republic of Poland. Disputes to be resolved by courts competent for Warszawa in accordance with Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe.
7. Termination and Miscellaneous
7.1 Term: 12 months from the date hereof, renewable by agreement. Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period.
7.2 Confidentiality: Both parties shall protect confidential information, including delivery manifests and pricing.
7.3 Notices, assignment, sub-contracting: Notices shall be in writing. LogiCore may subcontract deliveries but remains fully responsible for subcontractor compliance with this Agreement and applicable laws.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________
For MedSupply Polska Sp. z o.o. ____________________
Annexes: route list (45 hospitals), rate card, surge planning protocol, hygiene and PPE standard operating procedures.

title: DataCenter IT Equipment Logistics Agreement — CTR-2025-020
text:
Agreement (Umowa) CTR-2025-020
between
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 88, 00-999 Warszawa, Poland (hereinafter "Carrier" or "LogiCore")
and
CloudInfra Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Rondo 5, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland (hereinafter "Customer" or "CloudInfra").

Preamble
This Agreement governs carriage, handling and on-site positioning of high-value data center IT equipment (server racks, UPS systems, networking equipment, fiber optic cable) under zlecenie transportowe CTR-2025-020. Parties acknowledge that transport operations will be performed in conformity with applicable Polish and EU law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and supervision of UTK, GITD and with regard to competition rules of UOKiK where applicable.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall provide door-to-door international and domestic logistics for IT equipment listed in Annex A (server racks, UPS, networking, fiber optic cable). Individual server racks may exceed EUR 200,000 in declared value. Annual expected contract value: EUR 440,000.
1.2 Services include import clearance (Frankfurt am Main or Amsterdam) and airfreight arrival at Warsaw airport, first-mile collection, secure road transport to data centers in Warszawa, Kraków and Wrocław, last-mile white-glove placement, and return of any transport packaging when agreed.

2. Equipment Handling, Packaging and ESD Protection
2.1 All items must be packed and transported in ESD-safe packaging. Carrier will use anti-static pallet wrap, conductive crates and ESD-labeled skids. Grounding straps must be used by handling personnel when removing protective packaging and when moving components into final position.
2.2 Any evidence of electrostatic discharge (ESD) damage will trigger Customer notification and full replacement by Carrier of the damaged unit(s) at replacement value without deduction (full replacement for ESD damage). Parties agree to CMR/list przewozowy annotations in case of visible damage at receipt.

3. Climate and Environmental Controls
3.1 During carriage and while on any stop under Carrier control, ambient temperature shall be maintained between 15°C and 25°C and relative humidity below 60% with no condensation. Vehicle HVAC monitoring records shall be retained and provided upon request.
3.2 Failure to maintain climate parameters that causes loss or damage attributable to Carrier will be treated as breach and subject to liability per Clause 9.

4. Security and Continuity
4.1 Security measures: sealed trailer for each consignment, continuous GPS tracking, real-time location feed to Customer on request, driver signed NDA (umowa o poufności) and security vetting. No unscheduled stops between origin and destination, except for declared force majeure or police/road authority direction; any deviation must be reported immediately.
4.2 Personnel: each delivery of racks requires driver + two (2) trained helpers for white-glove positioning and rack alignment inside data halls. Helpers will have required clearances and wear ESD-safe attire.

5. Time-Critical Deliveries and Penalties
5.1 Rack deliveries for data center commissioning are time-critical. Each rack delivery must arrive and be ready for positioning within +/-2 hours of the scheduled appointment time on the booking confirmation. Carrier will coordinate to meet commissioning windows.
5.2 Penalties: EUR 1,000 per hour (or pro rata per minute) payable by Carrier to Customer for each hour of delay beyond the permitted +/-2 hour window, to cover data center crew standby and consequential costs. Such liquidated damages are without prejudice to other rights.

6. Handling Equipment and Access
6.1 Racks weigh between 500 kg and 1,200 kg. Carrier shall supply or arrange crane or hydraulic tail-lift and appropriate lifting accessories rated for loads and compliant with Polish safety regulations. Lifting plans must be provided for loads >750 kg.
6.2 Carrier's white-glove team is responsible for precise placement, leveling and temporary securing of racks in allocated rack positions. Customer will provide access, floor loading information and onsite power/environmental status.

7. Insurance, Valuation and High-Value Goods
7.1 Carrier shall maintain cargo insurance covering declared values, including declared single-rack values exceeding EUR 200,000. Insurance will cover theft, ESD and handling damage subject to insurer terms. Carrier will provide certificate of insurance upon signing.
7.2 For losses where ESD is proven, Carrier accepts full replacement liability regardless of insurance subrogation rights.

8. Documentation and Compliance
8.1 Carrier shall issue CMR or appropriate list przewozowy and deliver all customs/import documentation, bills of lading and packing lists. All invoices (faktura) for international legs will be in EUR. Domestic legs shall be invoiced in PLN where applicable.
8.2 Carrier will comply with transport permits, axle loads and occupational safety under Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and will cooperate with UTK/GITD inspections.

9. Liability and Limitation
9.1 Except for wilful misconduct and liabilities set out expressly (ESD replacement, punctuality penalties), liability shall be governed by Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny. Parties may not exclude liability for gross negligence toward life and personal injury.

10. Fees, Payment and Annual Value
10.1 The parties agree estimated annual contract value EUR 440,000. Individual shipments will be priced per zlecenie transportowe. Invoices are payable within 30 days of faktura date unless otherwise agreed.

11. Force Majeure and Changes
11.1 Force majeure events (including port strikes, airspace closure, extraordinary weather) relieve Carrier of strict compliance subject to immediate notice and duty to mitigate.

12. Term and Termination
12.1 Term: 12 months from signature or until annual value is reached. Early termination for material breach permitted with 30 days cure period. Termination does not affect accrued liabilities including penalties.

13. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
13.1 This Agreement is governed by Polish law. Disputes shall be referred to ordinary courts in Warszawa unless Parties agree mediation.

14. Miscellaneous
14.1 Amendments require written annexes signed by authorized representatives. Confidentiality obligations survive termination.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
Name, Title

For CloudInfra Polska Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
Name, Title

Annex A: Equipment List (server racks, UPS, networking equipment, fiber optic cable)
Annex B: Scheduled Routes and Booking Procedures (Frankfurt/Amsterdam/Warsaw airport -> Warszawa/Kraków/Wrocław)
Annex C: Driver NDA template and ESD handling checklist

CTR-2025-020 — End of Agreement

title: BrewCo Beverage Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Kompania Piwowarska S.A.
text:
CONTRACT NO. CTR-2025-021
BrewCo Beverage Distribution Agreement

This Transport and Distribution Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into on 1 March 2025 by and between:
- LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with its registered office in Warszawa, entered in KRS, having depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań ("Carrier" or "LogiCore"), and
- Kompania Piwowarska S.A., with its registered office in Poznań ("Shipper" or "Kompania Piwowarska").

Preamble
Kompania Piwowarska operates a brewery in Poznań and requires distribution of beer, soft drinks and water (heavy, high-volume product lines) to more than 500 retail and HoReCa points across Wielkopolska and Mazowsze. Carrier has capacity and expertise, and agrees to provide transport, delivery and collection services under the terms below.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall perform scheduled and ad-hoc transports of beverages (beer, soft drinks, bottled water) from Shipper's brewery in Poznań to approximately 500+ retail and HoReCa locations in the regions of Wielkopolska and Mazowsze. Services include loading, transport, tail-lift assisted delivery, settlement of returnable containers and collection of empties on the return leg ("Services").
1.2 Each transport shall be executed pursuant to a zlecenie transportowe and documented by a list przewozowy and, where applicable, CMR. Carrier shall issue a faktura monthly unless otherwise agreed.

2. Term
This Agreement commences on the date above and continues for 12 months, renewable by written agreement. Expected annual value: EUR 920,000 (nine hundred twenty thousand EUR). Parties acknowledge the contract is executed in PLN jurisdiction; where statutory domestic requirements demand, equivalent PLN amounts shall be calculated at the rate published by NBP on invoice date.

3. Routes, Frequencies and Delivery Windows
3.1 Primary origin: Brewery, Poznań. Destinations: retail chains and HoReCa outlets across Wielkopolska and Mazowsze (routing lists to be provided and updated monthly).
3.2 Delivery windows: Retail outlets 06:00-10:00; Restaurants and HoReCa 10:00-14:00. Carrier shall use curtain-siders with tail-lift for general palletised loads and specialized keg delivery trucks for kegs.
3.3 Seasonal peaks: Parties recognise variable volumes: summer peak:+40% volume; major sporting events and Christmas increases. Parties shall agree on supplementary zlecenia transportowe and resource planning at least 14 days prior to anticipated peaks.

4. Vehicles and Fleet Branding
4.1 Vehicle types: curtain-siders with tail-lift and specialized keg delivery trucks with appropriate securing and cooling where required. All vehicles shall meet Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and safety regulations.
4.2 Fleet branding: Five (5) vehicles will be fully wrapped in Kompania Piwowarska livery. Cost of wrapping is included in the price structure set out in Annex A and performed by Carrier.

5. Returnable Container System and Deposits
5.1 System: Kegs, crates and bottles are handled as returnable packaging. Carrier shall manage deposits and settlements per delivery and collection notes.
5.2 Deposits: Keg deposit EUR 30.00 per keg; Crate deposit EUR 5.00 per crate; Bottle deposit PLN 0.50 per bottle. Deposits collected on delivery are recorded on the list przewozowy and settled monthly on faktura.
5.3 Empties collection: Every delivery vehicle shall collect empty kegs/crates/bottles on the return leg. Missed pickups must be recovered within 48 hours unless otherwise agreed; failure to collect incurs penalties per clause 8.

6. Akcyza and Regulatory Compliance
6.1 Carrier acknowledges that beverages, in particular beer and certain soft drinks, may be subject to excise (akcyza). Drivers shall carry copies of all required excise documentation, transport permits and declarations for every load. Copies must be available for inspection by GITD, UTK or other competent authorities.
6.2 Carrier shall comply with Prawo Przewozowe, Kodeks Cywilny and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and co-operate with UOKiK where relevant.

7. Performance, KPIs and Reporting
7.1 On-time delivery target: 98% per month. Delivery confirmations and signed PODs shall be uploaded to the parties' shared portal within 24 hours.
7.2 Special handling and temperature requirements shall be recorded on each zlecenie transportowe.

8. Penalties and Charges
8.1 Late deliveries: EUR 100.00 per late delivery (defined as delivery outside the agreed window without prior written agreement). Late arrival attributable to force majeure documented per clause 12 is exempt.
8.2 Missed returns pickup: EUR 50.00 per missed pickup event.
8.3 Parties agree that penalties shall be invoiced monthly and may be deducted from payments due. Penalties are without prejudice to claims for additional proven damages under Kodeks Cywilny.

9. Pricing and Invoicing
9.1 Prices, including fuel surcharges and wrapping cost, are set in Annex A. Invoicing in EUR. Payments due within 30 days net from receipt of faktura. VAT treatment per Polish tax law; excise duties handled as per clause 6.

10. Liability and Insurance
10.1 Carrier maintains motor third-party liability insurance and cargo insurance covering loss, damage and theft. Liability limits shall conform to applicable laws and Annex B.

11. Subcontracting
Carrier may subcontract with prior written notice; Carrier remains fully liable for subcontractor performance.

12. Force Majeure
Events beyond reasonable control (including strikes, extreme weather, road closures) excuse performance to extent affected; party invoking force majeure shall promptly notify the other and take mitigation steps.

13. Confidentiality and Data
Parties shall treat commercial terms, routing data and pricing as confidential. Data processing shall comply with applicable data protection laws.

14. Termination
Either party may terminate for material breach after 30 days cure period. Termination handling of deposits and empties shall follow the final inventory and settlement.

15. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be resolved by competent courts in Warszawa unless parties agree arbitration.

16. Miscellaneous
Notices shall be in writing. Annexes (A—Price Schedule; B—Insurance; C—Service Level Agreement) form integral parts of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of the Parties:

LogiCore Sp. z o.o.           Kompania Piwowarska S.A.
-------------------           ---------------------------
Name:                         Name:
Title:                        Title:
Date:                         Date:

Attachments: Route list, Annex A (prices), Annex B (insurance), Annex C (SLA), sample zlecenie transportowe and list przewozowy templates.

title: PetFood Animal Feed Distribution Agreement (Contract CTR-2025-022)
text:
Contract No.: CTR-2025-022
Title: PetFood Animal Feed Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and ZooKarma Polska Sp. z o.o.
Parties:
- LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 87, 00-624 Warszawa, depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań (hereinafter "Carrier" or "LogiCore");
- ZooKarma Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Przemysłowa 12, 05-825 Grodzisk Mazowiecki (hereinafter "Customer" or "ZooKarma").
Preamble
This Agreement is governed by Polish law, including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Parties acknowledge regulatory oversight by UTK, GITD and competition rules of UOKiK. The Agreement establishes terms for transportation and distribution of dry pet food, wet pet food (canned) and animal feed supplements produced at Customer's factory in Grodzisk Mazowiecki to Customer's network of approx. 120 retail pet store locations and veterinary clinics across Poland.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall provide road transport, sorting, consolidation, and last-mile delivery services (zlecenie transportowe) for products including: dry pet food (bulk bags 25 kg), retail units (0.5–15 kg), wet pet food (canned), and animal feed supplements. Mixed pallets are common and carrier will accommodate mixed pallets containing both bulk bags and retail units.
1.2 Organic/Bio/Eko products and allergen-free products shall be transported under dedicated handling rules (clause 4) and recorded in an audit trail.
1.3 Delivery locations: approx. 120 pet store chain locations and veterinary clinics as per route manifests provided monthly by Customer. Primary dispatch point: Customer factory, Grodzisk Mazowiecki.
2. Duration and Annual Value
2.1 Term: 12 months from signature, renewable by mutual written agreement. Termination: 60 days' written notice. Immediate termination for material breach (contamination, repeated late delivery >5 incidents).
2.2 Annual estimated value: EUR 260,000. Charges invoiced in EUR; domestic transport pricing agreed in EUR for the purpose of this Contract.
3. Transportation Conditions
3.1 Temperature: ambient carriage; Carrier shall avoid exposure to temperatures >30°C to prevent product quality degradation. Carrier shall monitor transit temperatures and retain records for 12 months.
3.2 Packaging: Carrier shall transport mixed pallets while preserving integrity of retail units and bulk bags. Damaged packaging identified at delivery to be returned weekly under the Returns procedure (clause 8).
3.3 Documentation: For domestic legs, a Polish list przewozowy and packing list shall accompany shipments; where applicable, CMR consignment notes will be issued for international legs. Zlecenie transportowe and a copy of the invoice (faktura) shall be delivered electronically.
4. Allergen Separation and Organic Certification
4.1 Allergen-free products shall be segregated. Carrier shall provide dedicated trailer zones (physically separated compartments, dedicated liners or sealed segregated trailers) as indicated in each zlecenie. No allergenic product shall be loaded into the same trailer zone as allergen-free product.
4.2 Bio/Eko (organic) products require transportation in separate, clearly marked compartments and an audit trail documenting chain of custody from loading at Grodzisk Mazowiecki to delivery. Carrier shall permit Customer audits and produce records on request.
4.3 Contamination or allergen cross-contact is a material breach subject to penalties (clause 7).
5. Performance, KPI and Penalties
5.1 On-time delivery KPI: deliveries to scheduled windows. Late delivery penalty: EUR 100 per late delivery incident.
5.2 Contamination/allergen cross-contact penalty: EUR 500 per confirmed incident. Penalties are without prejudice to Customer's right to claim full damages under applicable law.
6. Liability and Insurance
6.1 Carrier Liability follows Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny where applicable. Carrier shall maintain insurance including cargo insurance and third-party liability adequate for the transported goods and values declared by Customer.
6.2 For loss or damage due to Carrier negligence, Carrier liability includes replacement cost and reasonable consequential losses, subject to statutory limits unless caused by gross negligence or willful misconduct.
7. Returns, Claims and Credits
7.1 Damaged packaging discovered at delivery shall be recorded on the delivery note and returned weekly to Customer's returns address unless otherwise agreed. Carrier shall provide a report of returned items with photos.
7.2 Customer will issue a credit note (nota kredytowa) for returned damaged packaging within 5 business days upon receipt of returned goods and Carrier's report. Carrier's acceptance of credit note shall be evidenced by electronic confirmation.
8. Invoicing and Payment
8.1 Carrier shall submit monthly invoices (faktura) with detailed line items, references to zlecenie transportowe and proof of delivery. Payment due: 30 calendar days from invoice date.
9. Audits, Records and Compliance
9.1 Carrier shall maintain transport records, temperature logs, segregation/audit trail for Bio/Eko and allergen-free goods, and make them available for Customer audit or for regulatory inspection by UTK/GITD.
9.2 Parties shall comply with competition law (UOKiK), data protection and applicable sanitary regulations.
10. Force Majeure
10.1 Events beyond reasonable control suspend obligations; affected party shall notify within 48 hours and use reasonable efforts to mitigate.
11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
11.1 Governing law: Republic of Poland. Disputes to be resolved before the competent courts in Warszawa unless Parties agree mediation.
12. Miscellaneous
12.1 Notices shall be in writing; language of Contract: English with relevant Polish terms. Amendments only by written agreement.
Executed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (Carrier)     ZooKarma Polska Sp. z o.o. (Customer)
Date: __________________          Date: __________________
(Signatures)
Reference to statutory provisions: Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Carrier shall cooperate with UTK/GITD inspections and comply with sanitary and labeling obligations.

title: PrintMedia Newspaper and Magazine Distribution Agreement (PrintMedia Newspaper and Magazine Distribution — Umowa Dystrybucji Prasy)
text:
CONTRACT No. CTR-2025-023
PrintMedia Newspaper and Magazine Distribution Agreement
Between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (headquartered: ul. Marszałkowska 10, 00-001 Warszawa; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań), hereinafter "LogiCore", and Agora S.A. (publisher of Gazeta Wyborcza), ul. Czerska 8/10, 00-732 Warszawa, hereinafter "Publisher".
Preamble
Pursuant to the Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and subject to supervision by UTK, GITD and compliance with UOKiK rules, the parties agree the terms for distribution of daily newspapers and periodicals across the Mazowsze region.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall perform scheduled transport and distribution of: (a) daily newspapers (Gazeta Wyborcza and affiliated editions) — time-critical; (b) magazines (weekly and monthly titles) — scheduled but less time-critical. Services cover direct transport from Publisher's printing plant in Piaseczno to approximately 2,500 kiosks (RUCH/Kolporter network) across Mazowsze.
1.2 Night operations: loading starts from 22:00 at the Piaseczno plant. Delivery runs commence at 01:00 and complete no later than 05:00 local time, seven (7) days per week. All deliveries of daily newspapers must be completed and kiosks stocked by 05:00 to meet commuter demand.
2. Freight Characteristics and Equipment
2.1 Newspapers are heavy: a single pallet of Gazeta Wyborcza is approximately 800 kg. LogiCore shall supply suitable vehicles and handling equipment to safely transport pallets of this weight including tail-lift or liftgate and certified pallet jacks.
2.2 Each transport run will be accompanied by a list przewozowy / CMR as required. A zlecenie transportowe will be issued by the Publisher prior to each run with quantities, addresses, and KPI targets.
3. Returns and Reverse Logistics
3.1 Unsold copies will be collected during the next scheduled delivery run to the same kiosks. LogiCore will segregate returns, prepare return documentation and deliver returns to Publisher's designated facility at Piaseczno.
3.2 Publisher credits LogiCore for return transport costs as set out in Annex A. Return legs will preferentially carry recycled paper or other sustainable cargo where practicable to minimize empty running and reduce CO2 emissions.
4. Performance Standards, Penalties and KPIs
4.1 Time-critical deliveries: all daily newspaper deliveries must be completed by 05:00. Failure to meet deadlines attracts penalties as set out below.
4.2 Penalties (currency converted for domestic contract): agreed conversion rate 1 EUR = 4.50 PLN. Therefore: late delivery penalty: EUR 50 = 225 PLN per late route (reader complaints); missed route penalty: EUR 500 = 2,250 PLN per missed route (kiosks empty). Penalties are payable net and may be offset against monthly faktura amounts following notice and 14 days cure period.
4.3 Annual contract value is EUR 310,000 (approx. 1,395,000 PLN at 1 EUR = 4.50 PLN). Parties acknowledge print circulation decline estimated at 5–8% per annum; pricing and estimated volumes will be reviewed accordingly.
5. Routing, Flexibility and Optimization
5.1 Parties authorise quarterly route optimisation meetings. LogiCore will propose route changes reflecting kiosk network changes, traffic patterns, and efficiency gains. Publisher approval required for material changes affecting delivery windows.
5.2 LogiCore shall maintain contingency plans for vehicle failure, adverse weather, strikes or extraordinary events and shall notify Publisher per communication protocol.
6. Sustainability
6.1 Return legs to the Piaseczno printing plant shall prioritise carriage of recycled paper and environmentally preferable loads. LogiCore will report quarterly on empty kilometers, fuel consumption and steps to reduce CO2.
7. Insurance, Liability and Compliance
7.1 LogiCore will maintain insurance covering goods in transit, carrier liability under Prawo Przewozowe/CMR, and third-party liability. Parties agree liability limits consistent with Polish law; will cooperate fully on claims handling.
7.2 Compliance with UTK, GITD regulations and social/driver safety rules is mandatory.
8. Invoicing and Payment
8.1 LogiCore shall invoice monthly (faktura) for services rendered. Payment terms: 30 days net. Publisher may request detailed run reports and delivery confirmations prior to payment processing.
9. Force Majeure and Termination
9.1 Force majeure events are governed by Kodeks Cywilny. Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period; immediate termination permitted for repeated missed routes or material safety violations.
10. Confidentiality
10.1 Commercial information, route lists and customer contacts are Confidential Information. Disclosure limited to authorised personnel. Post-termination non-disclosure continues for 24 months.
11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
11.1 Agreement governed by Polish law. Disputes to be resolved by amicable negotiation then by courts in Warszawa.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________
For Agora S.A. _____________________________
Annexes: Annex A (Rates and Return Credits), Annex B (Kiosk List and Route Matrix), Annex C (KPIs and Reporting Templates)
Effective date: upon signature. Contract reference: CTR-2025-023.

title: FrozenSeafood Deep-Freeze Fish Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and NordFish Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-024

This Transport and Logistics Agreement (the Agreement) is concluded on 01 March 2025 by and between:

1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, ul. Marszałkowska 100, 00-001 Warszawa, with operational depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter: Carrier or LogiCore), and

2. NordFish Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Rybna 12, 81-345 Gdynia (hereinafter: Shipper or NordFish).

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in refrigerated and deep-freeze transport services and operates HACCP-compliant cold chain solutions with ATP-certified reefers. NordFish is an importer and distributor of frozen seafood requiring deep-freeze transport from Gdynia/Gdańsk ports to cold storage facilities and processing plants across Poland. The Parties agree to the following terms under Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) and in conformity with applicable EU requirements, UTK, GITD and UOKiK guidelines.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Carrier shall provide deep-freeze transport, handling and distribution of frozen seafood (raw frozen fish and processed seafood products) from the ports of Gdynia and Gdańsk to agreed cold storages, processing plants and distribution centres across Poland (including but not limited to Warszawa, Poznań, Wrocław, Katowice, Szczecin). Services include vessel reception, pallet transfer, customs liaison, sanitary documentation handling and delivery to consignee.
1.2 Annual value: EUR 560,000 (five hundred sixty thousand Euro). Services will be ordered via zlecenie transportowe and confirmed by written order or electronic acknowledgement.

2. Product and Temperature Requirements
2.1 Raw frozen fish: maintained at -25°C continuous. Processed frozen seafood products: maintained at -18°C continuous.
2.2 Temperature monitoring: continuous electronic temperature monitoring with 15-minute logging interval is mandatory for all shipments. Monitoring data must be transmitted to both Parties' systems in real time and archived for a minimum period of 24 months.
2.3 Alarm and defrost policy: alarm threshold set at -15°C for any cargo. Defrost zero tolerance - any recorded temperature above -15°C for the load constitutes a temperature breach and triggers full batch rejection per clause 6.
2.4 Vehicle standards: all vehicles used by Carrier shall be HACCP-certified reefer trailers with valid ATP certificate and fit for carriage of frozen foodstuffs. Carrier shall maintain documentation proving ATP status, maintenance history and HACCP compliance on demand.

3. Traceability and Chain of Custody
3.1 Carrier shall implement MSC chain of custody procedures for certified sustainable seafood shipments and maintain traceability 'from vessel to plate'. Every pallet must be tracked with unique pallet ID and contain metadata: catch date, vessel ID, species, lot number and origin. Electronic manifest and CMR/list przewozowy must reference pallet IDs.
3.2 Any traceability gap (missing required pallet metadata or inability to reconcile pallet to vessel manifest) shall incur a traceability penalty of EUR 5,000 per occurrence and may be considered material breach enabling termination by Shipper after remedy period.

4. Documentation and Import Compliance
4.1 Carrier shall assist with import documentation: health certificate, SVD (Świadectwo Weterynaryjne), customs clearance and any required sanitary/phyto certificates. Carrier shall procure and retain copies of documents for audit.
4.2 For cross-border legs where CMR applies, Carrier shall issue CMR consignment note; for domestic distribution Carrier shall issue list przewozowy and faktura in accordance with Orders.

5. Handling, Loading and Unloading
5.1 Docking, pallet handling and transfers must preserve cold chain. Any manual handling shall be performed under HACCP protocols, using pre-cooled forklifts and pallet jacks. Temperature log must accompany each transport leg.

6. Defrost, Breach and Remedies
6.1 Defrost zero tolerance: any recorded temperature > -15°C (i.e. warmer) for a consignment = full batch rejection as per Shipper instruction. Carrier acknowledges that such condition may render product unsafe or commercially unsellable.
6.2 Penalties: For each confirmed temperature breach Carrier shall pay EUR 1,500 per breach as liquidated damages, plus shall reimburse full product replacement cost (replacement at market price including transport and customs) and cover reasonable associated disposal costs. Traceability gap penalty per clause 3.2 applies in addition: EUR 5,000.
6.3 Parties agree that the stated penalties are pre-estimates of loss and not a penalty contrary to applicable provisions of Kodeks Cywilny; they do not limit Shipper's right to claim additional proven damages in case of gross negligence or willful misconduct.

7. Insurance and Liability
7.1 Carrier shall maintain cargo insurance covering temperature-related loss and general liability insurance at customary market limits. Proof of insurance shall be provided on commencement and annually thereafter.
7.2 Carrier liability for loss, damage or delay shall be governed by applicable transport law including Prawo Przewozowe and international conventions where applicable (CMR), subject to Article 6 herein regarding strict temperature liabilities.

8. Audits, Compliance and Certification
8.1 Shipper reserves the right to audit Carrier facilities and records for HACCP, ATP documentation, temperature logs, traceability records and MSC chain of custody compliance with reasonable notice. Carrier shall cooperate and provide access.
8.2 Carrier shall comply with inspections by UTK, GITD or other competent authorities and shall notify Shipper of any regulatory findings that affect services.

9. Seasonal Capacity and Scheduling
9.1 Parties acknowledge seasonal peaks: Lent (February-March) and Christmas (November-December). Carrier shall allocate sufficient fleet and resources to meet Shipper's forecasted demand in these periods subject to timely notifications.

10. Invoicing and Payment
10.1 Invoicing shall be monthly in EUR for international legs and costed separately in PLN for any domestic-only legs if agreed. Invoices (faktura) shall reference contract CTR-2025-024 and specific zlecenie transportowe numbers. Payment terms: 30 days from invoice date unless otherwise agreed in writing.
10.2 Penalties due under clause 6 may be deducted by Shipper from Carrier invoices with notice, pending final reconciliation.

11. Force Majeure, Term and Termination
11.1 Force majeure events per Kodeks Cywilny excuse performance for duration of event. Parties shall notify each other promptly.
11.2 Term: this Agreement is effective from date above and continues for 12 months and then automatically renews for successive 12-month terms unless terminated with 90 days' written notice. Immediate termination for material breach (including repeated temperature breaches or traceability failures) is permitted.

12. Confidentiality and Data
12.1 Parties shall maintain confidentiality of commercial and operational data and comply with applicable data protection requirements. Temperature logs and traceability data are operationally shared and not to be disclosed to third parties except as required by law or regulatory authorities.

13. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
13.1 This Agreement governed by Polish law. Disputes to be resolved by courts of competent jurisdiction in Warszawa, unless Parties agree arbitration. Applicable statutory provisions of Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym shall apply.

14. Final Provisions
14.1 Amendments only in writing signed by authorised representatives. Annexes: Service Level Agreement (SLA), HACCP procedures, ATP certificates, MSC chain of custody certification and sample forms for zlecenie transportowe and CMR/list przewozowy.

Signed for and on behalf of the Parties:

LogiCore Sp. z o.o.                             NordFish Polska Sp. z o.o.
_____________________________                    _____________________________
Name:                                            Name:
Title:                                           Title:
Date:                                            Date:

Contract reference CTR-2025-024. Annual value: EUR 560,000. Currency: EUR for international carriage; PLN for agreed domestic settlement.
