title: FurniturePro Home Delivery Agreement – White‑Glove Service
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-025
FurniturePro Home Delivery Agreement (White-Glove) between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and FurniturePro Polska Sp. z o.o.

This agreement is made on 01 March 2025 by and between:
- LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Przykładowa 12, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland (LogiCore), operator and carrier, and
- FurniturePro Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. E-commerce 3, 01-100 Warszawa, Poland (Client), online furniture retailer.

RECITALS
LogiCore operates distribution and delivery services from its Warszawa distribution center and depots in Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw and Poznan. The parties wish to define terms for white-glove home delivery of furniture to customers across Mazowsze. This contract references applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and consumer rights under Ustawa o prawach konsumenta. Regulatory compliance includes UTK, GITD and UOKiK where relevant.

1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
1.1 LogiCore will perform white-glove home delivery services (usluga dostawy z wniesieniem i montażem) for Client's customers in Mazowsze. Services include: two-person crew, carry to room of choice, unpacking, removal of packaging, optional assembly if purchased by customer, and placement. Each delivery is performed pursuant to a zlecenie transportowe and accompanied by a list przewozowy or CMR where required.
1.2 Service area: origin Warszawa distribution center to customer homes across Mazowsze (urban and suburban routes). Vehicles to be used are box trucks with tail-lift, equipped with furniture blankets, straps and floor protection mats.

2. DELIVERY WINDOWS, NOTIFICATIONS AND CUSTOMER CONTACT
2.1 Deliveries are booked into 4-hour customer-selected windows. LogiCore shall provide an SMS notification to the customer approximately 30 minutes prior to arrival and re-confirm with a phone call if requested on the zlecenie transportowe.
2.2 If LogiCore anticipates missing a scheduled window, Client will be notified immediately and an alternative window offered. Failure to notify and missed window shall incur penalties as set in clause 7.

3. EXECUTION, CREW AND ASSEMBLY
3.1 Standard crew: two-person delivery team per vehicle for white-glove service. Crew responsibilities: safe transport from vehicle to room of choice, unpacking, removal of packaging, internal condition check and basic placement. Assembly is optional and charged separately at EUR 25 per item if purchased; LogiCore driver teams are trained on the top 50 SKUs identified by Client.
3.2 All assembly work shall be performed with reasonable skill and care. Complex assembly beyond trained SKUs will be referred back to Client unless prior agreement is made; additional charges will apply.

4. CONDITION CHECKS, PACKING AND DAMAGE CONTROL
4.1 Damage prevention is a priority. Client will conduct condition checks at warehouse prior to dispatch including photographic documentation (before photos) logged against each delivery item and attached to the zlecenie transportowe. LogiCore will take photos on vehicle loading and immediately on delivery (after photos) to record condition on arrival.
4.2 In the event of damage discovered on delivery, the crew will prepare a written report and photographic evidence. Parties agree that in case of damage caused during transport or delivery, Client will be indemnified for full item cost as documented on the invoice, subject to verification of chain of custody and evidence.

5. RETURNS AND CONSUMER RIGHTS
5.1 Client acknowledges consumer rights under Ustawa o prawach konsumenta including 14-day return right for distance sales. LogiCore will manage customer returns and pickups on Client instruction. Returns will be scheduled in 4-hour windows and handled as white-glove pickups with equivalent condition checks and photographic documentation.
5.2 Return logistics, restocking charges and disposition will follow Client's return policy. LogiCore will issue a report to Client for each return including fotos and condition notes.

6. EQUIPMENT, VEHICLES AND SAFETY
6.1 Vehicles: box trucks with tail-lift, appropriate load securing equipment, furniture blankets, moving straps, corner protectors and floor protection mats supplied by LogiCore. All vehicles and drivers comply with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and required certifications.
6.2 Health and safety: crews shall use floor protection mats and shall take reasonable measures to protect customer premises.

7. PENALTIES, CANCELLATIONS AND FAILURE TO DELIVER
7.1 Penalty for missed delivery window (failure to arrive within contracted 4-hour window without prior notification) is EUR 100 per occurrence, payable to Client on invoice credit within 14 days.
7.2 Penalty for failed delivery due to crew arrival and customer not at home (first attempt only, where appointment was confirmed) is EUR 200 per failed delivery.
7.3 For damaged goods proven to have been caused by LogiCore during handling or transport, LogiCore shall be liable for full item cost as invoiced by Client to the end-customer, subject to evidence from the before/after photographic record. Liability limits otherwise follow mandatory provisions of Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe.

8. PAYMENT AND ANNUAL VALUE
8.1 The annual contract value is estimated at EUR 390,000. Specific rates, per-delivery fees, assembly fees (EUR 25 per item) and additional services shall be invoiced monthly on a faktura from LogiCore to Client, payable within 21 days.

9. CUSTOMER SATISFACTION AND KPI
9.1 After each delivery LogiCore will send an NPS survey to the end-customer. Target NPS score for services delivered under this agreement is greater than 60. Parties agree to quarterly reviews of NPS and operational KPIs; remedial plans will be agreed when targets are missed.

10. AUDIT, DATA AND COMPLIANCE
10.1 Client may audit delivery records, photographic evidence and zlecenia transportowe upon reasonable prior notice. Both parties will comply with applicable data protection obligations.
10.2 Compliance with UTK, GITD and UOKiK guidance and with relevant transport and consumer laws is required. Disputes resolved under Polish law; competent courts in Warszawa.

11. TERM AND TERMINATION
11.1 Term: 12 months from signature, then renewable by mutual agreement. Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period.

12. MISCELLANEOUS
12.1 Force majeure, assignment and confidentiality terms as standard under Polish commercial practice apply. Notices by e-mail and confirmed by standard mail as set out in contact addenda.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have executed this Agreement via authorized representatives.

For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.                              For FurniturePro Polska Sp. z o.o.
Name:                                                 Name:
Title:                                                Title:
Date:                                                 Date:

Annexes: A. Service rates and fee schedule B. Top 50 SKU assembly list C. Reporting and NPS template D. Operational contacts and escalation matrix

This document is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland and references Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o prawach konsumenta as applicable.

title: HumanitarianAid Emergency Relief Logistics Framework Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Polski Czerwony Krzyż (PCK)
text:
Agreement No.: CTR-2025-026
Date: [to be completed]

Parties:
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa (headquarters), depots in Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw and Poznan, hereinafter "LogiCore", a carrier and logistics operator organized under Polish law.
2. Polski Czerwony Krzyż (PCK), hereinafter "PCK", a humanitarian society acting in the interest of emergency relief operations.

Recitals:
This Framework Agreement establishes standby and activation logistics services for emergency response (natural disasters, refugee crises, pandemic response) originating from PCK warehouse Warszawa Annopol to disaster sites across Poland and neighbouring countries, in accordance with applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and EU regulations where applicable.

Definitions:
- zlecenie transportowe: each individual activation/order issued by PCK to LogiCore.
- CMR/list przewozowy: transport document for cross-border shipments.
- Activation: commencement of mobilization of vehicles and personnel from standby.

Scope of Services:
LogiCore shall provide transport, handling, storage-in-transit and distribution services for humanitarian goods including food supplies, potable water, blankets, medical kits, tents and hygiene packages. Primary origin point: PCK warehouse Warszawa Annopol. Destination: disaster sites within Poland and in neighbouring countries (Ukraine, Belarus where applicable, Lithuania, Slovakia, Czechia, Germany) and other destinations as agreed.

Standby Capacity and Activation:
LogiCore commits to maintaining standby capacity of:
- 10 vehicles ready to mobilize on 24-hour notice,
- 20 vehicles ready to mobilize within 72 hours for scaled activations.
Vehicles include box trucks, refrigerated units and curtain-siders as required. PCK shall issue zlecenie transportowe to activate capacity. Each activation will specify goods, quantities, special handling and required documentation (CMR/list przewozowy for cross-border).

Cross-border and Customs:
Where cross-border movement is required, parties will apply humanitarian exemptions and simplified customs procedures in coordination with national customs authorities and, for international operations, with UNHCR and relevant UN/ICRC channels. LogiCore shall assist in preparing export/import manifests, CMR notes and any required humanitarian declaration. For international activations, currency and invoicing shall be in EUR; domestic operations invoiced in PLN unless otherwise agreed.

Cost Model and Payments:
This contract is a framework agreement with pre-agreed rates for vehicle/day, tonnage, mileage and handling. The framework annual retainer value: EUR 120,000. Actual cost will be calculated per activation based on pre-agreed rates and actual services rendered. In a major disaster year activations are estimated to total EUR 200,000 to EUR 500,000. LogiCore will issue faktura for each activation within 14 days of completion; payment terms: 30 days net unless otherwise agreed. Domestic PLN invoices shall reference applicable PLN rates; international EUR invoices shall be payable in EUR.

Service Levels and Humanitarian Exception:
SLA response times: 24 hours for domestic activations; 72 hours for cross-border activations. Due to the humanitarian nature of operations, no contractual penalty clause shall apply for delays or failure to meet SLA where delay arises from force majeure, security concerns, customs delay, UN/partner coordination or other humanitarian exceptions. Parties agree to act in good faith and to prioritize life-saving deliveries.

Volunteer Coordination:
LogiCore drivers may be augmented by PCK volunteers. All volunteer deployment shall be supervised by a designated LogiCore supervisor or driver-in-charge, who retains operational responsibility for health & safety and compliance with transport regulations. Volunteers must be briefed and sign any required safety acknowledgements. PCK shall ensure volunteers have appropriate ID and any authorizations required by authorities.

Liability, Insurance and Compliance:
LogiCore will maintain liability and cargo insurance appropriate to the scope of operations and compliant with Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Parties shall comply with directives of UTK, GITD and other relevant authorities. Liability for loss or damage shall follow applicable law; limited liability provisions in standard transport contracts apply except where higher standards are mandated by specific humanitarian agreements or mandatory law.

Reporting and Documentation:
For international activations LogiCore will provide UN OCHA-format logistics reports, including movement plans, vehicle manifests, delivery confirmations and inventory reconciliation. For cross-border consignments CMR and customs documents will accompany shipments. For domestic operations, appropriate list przewozowy or Waybill shall be provided.

Activation Procedure:
1. PCK issues zlecenie transportowe to LogiCore operations desk.
2. LogiCore confirms receipt and mobilization time within 4 hours for domestic, 12 hours for cross-border initial acknowledgement.
3. Vehicles mobilize from nearest depot (Warszawa, Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw, Poznan) per SLA.
4. Delivery confirmation and signed list przewozowy/CMR to be returned within 48 hours of completion.

Force Majeure:
Neither party shall be liable for failure to perform caused by events beyond control (natural disaster, civil unrest, border closures, pandemic-related restrictions). Parties will use best efforts to mitigate impact and resume services.

Term and Termination:
Term: 24 months from date of signature unless earlier terminated. Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period. PCK may suspend activations on public policy or security grounds.

Governing Law and Dispute Resolution:
This Agreement is governed by Polish law. Disputes shall be resolved by amicable negotiation; failing that, by arbitration in Warsaw under Polish arbitration rules or by competent courts in Poland as agreed.

Miscellaneous:
Amendments shall be in writing. Confidential information shall be protected. Coordination with UNHCR or other international partners shall be as required for cross-border humanitarian response. UOKiK compliance and competition law considerations are acknowledged.

Signatures:
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
For Polski Czerwony Krzyż: ____________________

Annexes (to be attached): Rate Card (EUR/PLN), Vehicle List and Specifications, Insurance Certificates, Activation Forms, UN OCHA report template, Sample CMR and list przewozowy forms.

title: AutoJIT Automotive Just-In-Time Delivery Agreement — CTR-2025-027
text:
AGREEMENT CTR-2025-027
AutoJIT Automotive Just-In-Time Delivery Agreement

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 45, 00-001 Warszawa, Polska, NIP: PL0000000000 ("Carrier" / "LogiCore"). Depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań.
2. FCA Powertrain Polska Sp. z o.o. (Stellantis), ul. Produkcyjna 1, 43-300 Bielsko-Biała, Polska ("Customer" / "Stellantis").

Preamble
This Agreement (CTR-2025-027) sets forth the terms under which LogiCore will provide Just-In-Time (JIT) milk-run delivery services of critical automotive components to the Customer's assembly line in Bielsko-Biała. The Parties acknowledge the high sensitivity of automotive JIT operations and agree to the service, performance, liability and penalty provisions herein.

Definitions
- "JIT Window": arrival at assembly line dock within ±15 minutes of scheduled dock time.
- "Components": engine parts, transmission assemblies, wiring harnesses and related items supplied by eight (8) specified Tier 1 suppliers listed in Annex A.
- "Milk-run": single vehicle collecting components from multiple supplier sites on an optimized route per zlecenie transportowe.
- "ASN": Advanced Shipping Notice transmitted electronically via LogiCore EDI system to Stellantis MES.
- "Sequenced Delivery": deliveries requiring exact assembly sequence identification by colour-coded labels as specified in Annex B.

Term and Scope
This Agreement is effective as of the date of the last signature and shall continue for an initial term of twelve (12) months, automatically renewable unless terminated in accordance with Article 18. Annual contract value is agreed at PLN 6,440,000 (EUR 1,400,000) as estimated volume/charges.

Operational Parameters
- Volume: 12–16 deliveries per operating day, 6 days/week. Plant operates 3 shifts.
- JIT Requirement: All Components must arrive at the designated assembly line dock within the JIT Window of ±15 minutes.
- Milk-run: LogiCore will operate optimized milk-run routes collecting from eight (8) nominated Tier 1 suppliers (Annex A). Each vehicle run is to minimize dwell time and comply with delivery windows.
- Sequencing: Where Sequenced Delivery is specified, components must be presented in exact assembly sequence with colour-coded labels and barcodes compatible with Stellantis MES.

EDI, ASN and Documentation
- LogiCore shall transmit an ASN via its EDI platform to Stellantis MES at least 60 minutes prior to arrival. ASN must include batch, sequence position, pallet ID, weight, and pallet barcode.
- Each physical consignment must be accompanied by a CMR or list przewozowy and signed zlecenie transportowe when applicable. Invoices (faktura) will be issued monthly unless otherwise agreed.

Performance Standards
- Punctuality: Compliance with the JIT Window is a material obligation. Failure to meet JIT Window constitutes a late delivery or, if the failure causes a production interruption, a line stop.
- If a delivery is late but prior to any line stop, Carrier accepts a contractual penalty of PLN 23,000 (EUR 5,000) per late delivery, payable within 14 days of invoice deduction by Customer.
- Line-stop: If Customer's assembly line stops due to missing or incorrect Components attributable to Carrier, Customer shall bill Carrier the full line-stop cost of PLN 69,000 per minute (EUR 15,000/minute). Carrier accepts full responsibility for such line-stop costs where Carrier's breach is the proximate cause.

Contingency and Backup
- LogiCore shall maintain a dedicated backup vehicle permanently stationed within fifteen (15) minutes driving time of the Bielsko-Biała plant, available for instant dispatch on Customer call. The backup vehicle must be fully equipped to continue the milk-run without sequence disruption.
- If the backup vehicle is dispatched, LogiCore shall notify Customer's Materiały/Expedycja and record dispatch time in the ASN and CMR/list przewozowy.

Liability and Insurance
- Carrier shall carry insurance covering cargo, liability for loss, damage and third-party claims as required by Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and applicable UTK/GITD regulations. Evidence of insurance must be provided on request.
- Carrier's liability for direct pecuniary losses arising from delayed or missing deliveries shall include contractual penalties above and, where line-stop occurs, full line-stop costs without limitation to other remedies.
- Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to limit liability for gross negligence or wilful misconduct under Kodeks Cywilny.

Claims, Audits and Compliance
- All claims for shortages, damage or sequence errors must be reported within 2 hours of occurrence and followed by written claim within 24 hours. Relevant documentation (CMR/list przewozowy, ASN, photos) must be preserved.
- Customer reserves audit rights to inspect Carrier's processes, vehicles and documentation for compliance with this Agreement, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, UTK and GITD requirements.
- Carrier must comply with competition law and fair practices; UOKiK guidance applies.

Invoicing and Payment
- LogiCore will issue faktura monthly for services rendered. Customer may set off penalties and recoverable line-stop costs from amounts owing. Payment terms: 30 days net unless otherwise agreed in writing.

Force Majeure
- Parties shall be excused for non-performance due to force majeure events beyond their control per Kodeks Cywilny. Given the critical nature of JIT, Parties must use commercially reasonable efforts (including use of backup vehicle) to mitigate impact.

Termination
- Either Party may terminate for material breach if the breach is not cured within 10 calendar days after written notice. Customer may terminate immediately if repeated late deliveries or any single preventable line-stop results in cumulative line-stop charges exceeding PLN 690,000 (EUR 150,000) within a rolling 30-day period.

Confidentiality
- Parties shall maintain confidentiality of operational data, supplier identities and pricing. Disclosures required by law (including UTK, GITD enquiries) must be notified to the other Party.

Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
- This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be referred first to escalation between operational managers and, if unresolved, to arbitration in Warsaw or the competent courts in Warszawa in accordance with Polish law.

Miscellaneous
- Annexes (A — supplier list; B — sequencing & colour-code specification; C — service level matrix) form integral parts of this Agreement.
- Parties acknowledge that transport is subject to CMR and Prawo Przewozowe where applicable and that each delivery will be accompanied by appropriate list przewozowy/CMR documentation.

Signatures
For and on behalf of LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Name: ________________________
Title: Head of Transport Operations
Date: ________________________

For and on behalf of FCA Powertrain Polska (Stellantis)
Name: ________________________
Title: Logistics/Materials Manager
Date: ________________________

Annexes and operational attachments referenced above are appended and shall be updated by written amendment signed by both Parties. The Parties declare that they have read and understood the provisions of this Agreement and accept them as binding under Kodeks Cywilny and applicable transport law.

title: Textile/Fashion Seasonal Collection Distribution Agreement — CTR-2025-028
text:
AGREEMENT NO. CTR-2025-028

Textile/Fashion Seasonal Collection Distribution Agreement
between
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Koszykowa 59, 00-660 Warszawa, Polska ("LogiCore")
and
Modna Polska S.A., ul. Piotrkowska 120, 90-001 Łódź, Polska ("Client")

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in professional logistics, transport and store distribution services with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań. Client is a Polish fashion brand owning 65 own-brand retail stores across Poland and a distribution centre (DC) in Łódź. Parties conclude this agreement in accordance with the provisions of the Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and subject to inspections and supervision by UTK, GITD and applicable consumer protection rules (UOKiK).

1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall provide transport, store distribution and visual merchandising logistics for Garment-on-Hanger (GOH) and flat-packed clothing, accessories and associated POS materials from Client's DC in Łódź to 65 Client stores in Poland.
1.2 Services include: order pick & pack at DC, pre-sorting by store planogram, tagging by rack position, transport to store, on-site rack placement support (as agreed), reverse logistics for end-of-season returns, inventory reconciliation and reporting.
1.3 Collections: Four (4) scheduled seasonal collections per calendar year + flash collections with guaranteed 2-week turnaround from zlecenie transportowe acceptance to store delivery when requested by Client, subject to availability and peak-period constraints.

2. Operations and Routing
2.1 Primary route: Łódź DC -> 65 retail stores across Poland. Delivery windows and routing schedules shall be agreed quarterly and updated in the operations schedule annex.
2.2 Peak periods: pre-Christmas (November) and pre-summer (April–May). Parties acknowledge higher volume and agree to capacity planning, staff and vehicle allocation in advance of peaks.

3. Visual Merchandising and Packaging
3.1 Deliveries shall be organised by store planogram and pre-sorted by rack position (left-to-right, top-to-bottom) and labelled accordingly on each reusable garment bag or folding plastic crate.
3.2 Packaging standards: reusable garment bags for GOH, branded hanger covers, folding plastic crates for accessories and flat-packed items. All packaging remains property of the Client unless otherwise agreed; LogiCore responsible for handling and return of reusable packaging to DC on rotation schedule.

4. Returns and Reverse Logistics
4.1 End-of-season unsold stock shall be collected from stores and returned to the Łódź DC for markdown and outlet disposition. Collections to be planned within 21 days of end-of-season notification unless otherwise agreed.
4.2 LogiCore shall provide segregated handling of returns and issue a detailed list przewozowy and return manifest per shipment.

5. Fees, Invoicing and Annual Value
5.1 Annual value of services estimated at EUR 280,000. Fees include transport, handling, pre-sorting and reporting as specified in the tariff annex. Parties may agree surcharges for peak periods and extraordinary services.
5.2 Invoices (faktura) shall be issued monthly, payable within 30 days net. Late payments accrue interest per Kodeks Cywilny statutory rate.

6. Performance Standards and Penalties
6.1 Timely delivery for new collection launch is critical. If delivery for a store collection launch is late causing a store opening without new stock, LogiCore shall pay Client a contractual penalty of EUR 300 per affected store per occurrence.
6.2 For late restock deliveries which disrupt normal operations, a penalty of EUR 100 per affected store per occurrence applies.
6.3 Parties acknowledge limitation of liability clauses elsewhere in this Agreement; penalties do not preclude Client seeking damages under applicable law.

7. Insurance, Liability and Claims
7.1 LogiCore shall maintain insurance covering cargo, third-party liability and professional indemnity sufficient for the scope of services. Liability for loss or damage shall be governed by Prawo Przewozowe and relevant international conventions where applicable (CMR for cross-border movements).
7.2 Claims for loss or damage must be notified within statutory terms and supported by list przewozowy, photographic evidence and inventory records.

8. Sustainability and Reporting
8.1 LogiCore commits to a carbon-neutral delivery target by 2027 for operations under this Agreement. Interim measures include fleet optimisation, modal shift where feasible and use of low-emission vehicles.
8.2 For every shipment LogiCore will provide a CO2 report (per-shipment emissions in kg CO2e), methodology and data sources. Annual aggregated CO2 performance reports and progress updates toward the 2027 target shall be delivered to Client.

9. Compliance and Audits
9.1 Parties shall comply with applicable Polish and EU law, including Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, safety rules, and UOKiK consumer regulations. LogiCore will cooperate with UTK/GITD inspections and provide records on request.
9.2 Client may audit operational records once per calendar year with reasonable notice.

10. Term, Termination and Force Majeure
10.1 Term: 24 months from execution, renewable by written agreement. Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period. Immediate termination permitted for insolvency.
10.2 Force majeure events relieve performance obligations to the extent impacted, provided timely notice is given.

11. Confidentiality and Data Protection
11.1 Parties shall maintain confidentiality of trade secrets, planograms, pricing and customer data. Personal data processing shall comply with Polish and EU data protection law (RODO).

12. General Provisions
12.1 Amendments must be in writing. Notices shall be in Polish or English to designated contacts.
12.2 Governing law: Polish law. Disputes shall be settled by the competent courts in Warszawa, unless parties agree arbitration.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________
For Modna Polska S.A. ____________________
Date: __________

Annexes: Operations Schedule, Tariff Annex, Packaging & Planogram Standards, CO2 Reporting Methodology.

title: RetailChain Multi-Store Replenishment Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Drogeria Natura Sp. z o.o.
text:
CONTRACT No. CTR-2025-029

This Transport and Replenishment Agreement (the "Agreement") is made on this day between:

1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., a limited liability company with registered seat in Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw and Poznan, NIP [...] ("LogiCore"); and
2) Drogeria Natura Sp. z o.o., a retail chain operator ("Customer").

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in transport, logistics and cross-docking services. Customer operates a chain of retail drugstores. Parties agree to a multi-store replenishment service for 180 stores located in northern Poland (regions: Pomorze, Warmia-Mazury, Podlaskie) under the following terms and conditions, in accordance with applicable Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) and subject to oversight by UTK, GITD and UOKiK where relevant.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall provide scheduled replenishment transport and related services for mixed cargo consisting of cosmetics, household chemicals, health products and seasonal items ("Goods").
1.2 Temperature control: shipments will be transported in two temperature profiles: ambient and controlled 15-25°C for temperature-sensitive cosmetics. All vehicles and trailers used by LogiCore for controlled shipments shall be certified and maintain continuous temperature records and alarms; temperature excursions will be reported to Customer within 4 hours.
1.3 Delivery network: services cover 180 stores in Pomorze, Warmia-Mazury and Podlaskie. Each store will receive deliveries 2x/week on a scheduled day and within a pre-agreed delivery window (zlecenie transportowe and delivery window specified in Annex A).
1.4 Cross-docking: certain supplier pallets will flow through LogiCore's DC without putaway (cross-dock). Cross-docked pallets will be identified on the supplier's manifest and treated per Annex B (flow-through handling, minimal dwell time, direct allocation to store shipments).
1.5 Returns management: LogiCore will collect returns (damaged goods, expired products, seasonal remnants) on a monthly collection cycle, consolidate returns at LogiCore DC and prepare credit reports for Customer. Special urgent collections may be arranged subject to separate fee schedule.
1.6 Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI): LogiCore will monitor store stock levels via EDI integration. Based on agreed min/max par levels and EDI stock feeds, LogiCore will trigger replenishment recommendations and issue zlecenie transportowe for execution.

2. Performance, KPIs and Promotional Peaks
2.1 Delivery frequency and schedule: each store shall be replenished twice per week on predetermined weekdays and delivery windows. Deviations require 72 hours' notice or acceptance by Customer.
2.2 Promotional peaks: three (3) major promotions per calendar year where volume may increase by up to +50%. Customer will provide advance notification at least 4 weeks prior to promotional start and cooperate on temporary capacity planning. LogiCore shall use reasonable endeavours to meet peak volumes; additional costs for extended equipment or third-party carriage will be pre-approved and invoiced.
2.3 KPIs: on-time delivery rate (target 98%), short delivery rate (target <0.5%), damages rate (target <0.2%). Monthly KPI reports will be provided.

3. Pricing, Annual Value and Billing
3.1 Annual contract value is agreed at EUR 720,000 (seven hundred twenty thousand euros). Parties acknowledge payments and invoices (faktura) may be issued in EUR. For domestic accounting purposes payments may be converted to PLN at the rate specified on the invoice; VAT and local taxes apply as per law.
3.2 Invoicing: LogiCore will submit monthly invoices with detailed manifests (CMR / list przewozowy, EDI confirmation). Payment terms are thirty (30) days net from receipt of faktura unless otherwise agreed in writing.

4. Penalties and Claims
4.1 Late delivery penalty: EUR 150 per occurrence for deliveries outside the agreed delivery window which materially affect store operations.
4.2 Short delivery penalty: EUR 300 per occurrence where items are missing from the delivery manifest at the point of receipt (short delivery).
4.3 Damages penalty: EUR 100 per occurrence for visible damages attributable to carrier handling.
4.4 Application: Penalties are subject to verification and documentary evidence (signed delivery note, photographic evidence, store acceptance). Penalties may be offset against invoices. Parties reserve rights under Kodeks Cywilny for greater proven loss. Penalties do not exclude parties' rights to claim indemnity for consequential losses where permissible by law.

5. Liability and Insurance
5.1 Carrier liability will be governed by applicable provisions of Prawo Przewozowe and, where applicable, international CMR rules for cross-border legs. LogiCore maintains cargo insurance covering transported goods; copies of insurance certificates shall be provided upon request.
5.2 Liability exclusions: LogiCore shall not be liable for damaged or expired products arising from Supplier packing defects, force majeure, or Customer instructions that deviate from agreed handling.

6. Operational Provisions
6.1 Documentation: each delivery must be accompanied by a complete manifest, CMR or list przewozowy where appropriate. EDI confirmations must be provided in the agreed format.
6.2 Temperature control records: for controlled shipments a temperature log will be attached to each delivery. Sustained excursions must be reported immediately and investigated.
6.3 Cross-dock handling: pallets marked for flow-through will not be put away; LogiCore will perform QC checks, pallet re-labelling if necessary, and ensure on-time dispatch to stores.
6.4 Store access and windows: Customer will ensure store acceptance times and provide loading/unloading facilities. Repeated failed delivery attempts may incur re-delivery charges.

7. Data Sharing, Confidentiality and Audit
7.1 Parties agree to maintain confidentiality of commercial terms, pricing and customer data. Data exchanges via EDI shall comply with applicable data protection laws.
7.2 LogiCore will provide monthly operational reports and allow Customer audits of logistics processes with reasonable notice.

8. Returns, Credits and Reconciliation
8.1 Monthly returns cycle: LogiCore will collect returns once monthly, reconcile returns manifest and submit a returns report to Customer. Credits for accepted returns will be applied following mutual acceptance and supporting documentation.

9. Term, Termination and Force Majeure
9.1 Term: this Agreement commences on signing and continues for 12 months, renewable automatically unless terminated with 90 days' written notice.
9.2 Termination for cause: either Party may terminate for material breach if breach remains uncured 30 days after notice.
9.3 Force majeure: performance is excused for events outside reasonable control (including regulatory interventions by UTK/GITD). Affected Party shall notify the other without undue delay.

10. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
10.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be resolved by competent courts in Warszawa, unless Parties agree mediation.

Annexes: Annex A (Store schedule and delivery windows), Annex B (Cross-dock procedures), Annex C (EDI and VMI specifications).

Signed for and on behalf of LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Drogeria Natura Sp. z o.o.

LogiCore Sp. z o.o.               Drogeria Natura Sp. z o.o.
[Authorized signatory]            [Authorized signatory]
Date:                             Date:

References: Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Documentation: CMR, list przewozowy, faktura, zlecenie transportowe.

title: ChemRaw Chemical Raw Materials Transport Agreement — ChemRaw: Transport of Soda Ash, Salt and Calcium Chloride
text:
Transport Agreement CTR-2025-030

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 88, 00-620 Warszawa, Poland, with operational depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter 'LogiCore' or 'Carrier'); and
2. CIECH S.A., ul. Miodowa 5, 00-246 Warszawa, on behalf of CIECH Soda Polska (Inowrocław) (hereinafter 'CIECH' or 'Shipper').

Preamble
This Agreement (Contract ID CTR-2025-030) sets forth terms for carriage, handling and related services for bulk chemical raw materials produced by CIECH (notably soda ash, salt, and calcium chloride), which are UN-classified dangerous goods and transported under ADR classes 8 (corrosive) and 9 (miscellaneous) as specified by CIECH in Safety Data Sheets (SDS). The Parties acknowledge applicable Polish and European law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, ADR, Rozporządzenie REACH and EU customs rules.

1. Subject and Scope
1.1 Carrier shall perform international and domestic transport services for bulk chemicals from CIECH Soda Polska, Inowrocław to industrial customers across Poland and the Federal Republic of Germany, and when agreed, to the Czech Republic and Slovakia (routes DE, CZ, SK).
1.2 Commodities covered: soda ash (sodium carbonate), salt (sodium chloride), calcium chloride, and related packaged forms including IBCs (Intermediate Bulk Containers) and road tank containers. All materials shall be transported in accordance with UN classification assigned by CIECH and listed on the SDS.
1.3 Annual estimated value of services: EUR 980,000. This is an estimate and not a firm commitment of volume.

2. Duration
2.1 This Agreement is effective from 01 March 2025 until 28 February 2026 unless earlier terminated in accordance with clause 12.

3. Documentation and Compliance
3.1 Each shipment must be accompanied by: CMR (for international legs), zlecenie transportowe, complete SDS (Safety Data Sheet) in Polish and English, UN number and proper shipping name, and certificates required by customs for cross-border carriage.
3.2 REACH: The Parties shall comply with Rozporządzenie REACH. CIECH shall provide any registration numbers and exposure scenario information required. LogiCore shall not alter product packaging or change composition.
3.3 ADR and DGSA: All transport operations shall comply with ADR provisions including ADR 1.8.3.1 emergency response requirements. LogiCore shall designate and make available its Dangerous Goods Safety Adviser (DGSA) contact details to CIECH and receive shipment notifications. Emergency telephone numbers and incident reporting protocol shall be provided with each consignment.

4. Equipment, Cleaning and Segregation
4.1 Authorised equipment: road tank containers, tank swap bodies, certified IBCs. Use of equipment not conforming to ADR or owner specifications is prohibited.
4.2 Cleaning: Tank containers and IBCs must be cleaned between loads. Cleaning is mandatory at certified facilities; a certificate of cleanliness (certificate czystości) signed by the cleaning provider must accompany each load. Failure to provide certificate will result in refusal of carriage and may trigger penalties.
4.3 Dedicated fleet: A dedicated, clearly identified fleet shall be assigned for food-grade sodium bicarbonate (sodium bicarb) where applicable, with separation from streams carrying corrosive or non-food-grade materials. Food-grade vehicles require dedicated documentation and hygiene certificates.

5. Safety, Emergency Response and Environmental Protection
5.1 DGSA oversight: LogiCore's DGSA shall audit procedures, training records and vehicle compliance quarterly and upon reasonable request by CIECH or competent authorities (UTK, GITD).
5.2 Emergency response: In case of incident, Parties shall follow ADR 1.8.3.1, notify local emergency services and the DGSA immediately, provide incident reports and cooperate with responders. CIECH shall provide product-specific emergency instructions from SDS.
5.3 Environmental incidents: Immediate containment, remediation and notification to competent authorities (e.g., GITD, UTK, and UOKiK where applicable) are obligatory. Parties shall coordinate with local hazardous materials teams.

6. Cross-border Operations, Permits and Customs
6.1 Cross-border transport to DE, CZ, SK requires valid ECMT permits or bilateral authorisations where applicable. LogiCore shall be responsible for obtaining and maintaining necessary road permits for international legs.
6.2 CMR international waybills shall be used for international carriage. Customs declarations and export/import formalities are to be completed by the Party designated on the zlecenie transportowe: typically CIECH as consignor for export customs, LogiCore may act as declarant if agreed in writing.

7. Insurance, Liability and Indemnity
7.1 Carrier shall maintain insurance covering cargo (minimum EUR 1,000,000 per event), third-party liability and environmental liability consistent with ADR and Prawo Przewozowe.
7.2 Liability rules shall follow applicable law (Prawo Przewozowe, Kodeks Cywilny) subject to mandatory ADR and international conventions. Parties shall indemnify each other for breach, wilful misconduct, or negligence.

8. Penalties and Remedies
8.1 Monetary penalties for breach: EUR 3,000 for each confirmed ADR compliance violation attributable to Carrier; EUR 10,000 for each environmental incident caused by Carrier's proven negligence; EUR 500 per incident of late delivery attributable to Carrier (beyond agreed delivery window). Penalties are without prejudice to claims for actual damages and insurance recoveries.
8.2 Recurrent or material breaches allow the non-breaching Party to terminate the Agreement with immediate effect (clause 12).

9. Fees, Invoicing and Payment
9.1 Domestic carriage (within Poland) shall be invoiced in PLN; international carriage and cross-border legs invoiced in EUR. Invoices (faktura) to be issued monthly, payable within 30 days net, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
9.2 VAT, customs duties and ancillary charges will be handled per applicable rules; additional handling or demurrage charges require prior written approval.

10. Audits, Records and Confidentiality
10.1 Each Party shall maintain records of transports, cleaning certificates, SDS distribution, training and DGSA reports for at least 5 years and allow audits by the other Party with reasonable notice.
10.2 Confidential commercial information shall be protected and not disclosed except to authorities or as required by law.

11. Force Majeure
11.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure due to force majeure events (acts of God, regulatory prohibitions, war, strikes). The affected Party shall notify the other without delay and mitigate effects.

12. Termination
12.1 Either Party may terminate for material breach not remedied within 30 days of notice. Termination does not affect liabilities accrued prior to termination.

13. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
13.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be resolved by competent courts in Warszawa, unless Parties agree to arbitration.

14. Miscellaneous
14.1 Any amendments must be in writing and signed by authorised representatives.
14.2 Contacts: Operational and DGSA contacts for both Parties shall be exchanged prior to first dispatch under this Agreement.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ______________________ Date: ______
For CIECH S.A.: ____________________________ Date: ______

Annexes (integral): Annex A — SDS list and UN numbers as provided by CIECH; Annex B — Cleaning providers and certificate template; Annex C — DGSA contact and emergency response protocol; Annex D — Rates schedule and incoterms per zlecenie transportowe.

title: DiplomaticCourier Embassy Supplies — Service Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and MSZ RP (Framework)
text:
Agreement No. CTR-2025-031

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter: "LogiCore"), a Polish limited liability company, VAT ID ..., acting under the relevant provisions of Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym;
2. Ministerstwo Spraw Zagranicznych Rzeczypospolitej Polskiej (MSZ RP), acting via the Framework Agreement for Diplomatic Logistics (hereinafter: "MSZ").

Preamble
This Framework Contract (CTR-2025-031) governs carriage and security services for embassy supplies originating from MSZ warehouse (Warszawa) to Polish diplomatic missions in Berlin, Praha (Prague), Wien (Vienna), Bratislava and Vilnius, including but not limited to office furniture, IT equipment and diplomatic correspondence (non-pouch).

Scope of Services
1. LogiCore will provide door-to-door transport and handling of shipments described in individual zlecenie transportowe issued under this framework. Shipments include office furniture, IT equipment and non-pouch diplomatic correspondence. International transport documentation (CMR / list przewozowy) shall accompany each consignment where applicable.
2. Standard response time for confirmed orders is up to 5 (five) business days from receipt of zlecenie. Urgent consignments to be executed within 48 (forty-eight) hours upon order confirmation.
3. No co-loading with cargo of other customers is permitted. Subcontracting is prohibited unless expressly authorised in writing by MSZ. Any permitted subcontractor must meet the same security and compliance standards as LogiCore.

Security and Personnel
1. All drivers assigned to MSZ consignments shall hold Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa at the appropriate level and sign driver NDAs (non-disclosure agreements) prohibiting disclosure of travel routes, cargo content and client identity.
2. Vehicles shall be equipped with continuous GPS tracking and tamper-evident seals for IT equipment. GPS data shall be available to MSZ upon request and retained in accordance with data protection laws and MSZ instructions.
3. Chain of custody documentation must accompany IT equipment from pickup to delivery, including seal numbers and timestamps. Destination IT officer sign-off required at delivery; any discrepancies must be recorded on the list przewozowy/CMR and reported to MSZ within 4 hours.

Diplomatic Protocol and Customs
1. LogiCore shall respect diplomatic protocols, embassy access procedures and accreditation rules. For shipments claiming diplomatic exemption, MSZ will provide required customs-exempt documentation in accordance with the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations. LogiCore shall cooperate with customs authorities and present documentation as instructed by MSZ. Where necessary, escorts and access passes will be coordinated by MSZ.
2. LogiCore acknowledges the sensitivity of diplomatic consignments and will follow specific entry procedures at each mission (Berlin, Prague, Vienna, Bratislava, Vilnius). Any delay at mission entry must be documented and immediately reported.

Insurance and Liability
1. In compliance with government requirements, LogiCore shall maintain insurance coverage of EUR 1,000,000 per shipment for physical loss or damage, and provide certificates of insurance at MSZ request.
2. Liability for loss, damage or security breach shall be governed by this Agreement, Prawo Przewozowe and, where applicable, CMR provisions. LogiCore shall implement reasonable measures to prevent theft, tampering or unauthorized access.

Confidentiality and Prohibitions
1. Drivers, couriers and employees handling MSZ consignments shall be bound by NDA obligations. LogiCore shall not disclose any details of MSZ operations or cargo contents to third parties, including press, without prior written MSZ consent.
2. No subcontracting or co-loading with third-party cargo is permitted without MSZ written consent. Any breach constitutes material breach.

Performance, Penalties and Remedies
1. Annual framework value is estimated at EUR 160,000; actual remuneration shall be based on individual orders and confirmed rates.
2. Penalties: EUR 1,000 per confirmed security breach (including loss of sealed IT equipment, unauthorized disclosure, or failure of personnel to present Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa), and EUR 500 per late delivery unless delay is caused by force majeure or MSZ actions. Penalties are without prejudice to claims for full damages.

Compliance and Audits
1. LogiCore shall comply with UTK, GITD and UOKiK regulations where applicable and permit MSZ or its designee to audit security, personnel clearance and chain-of-custody records upon reasonable notice.

Term and Termination
1. This framework shall commence on signature and continue for 24 months unless earlier terminated for material breach or convenience with 30 days' notice. Immediate termination permitted for serious security breach.

Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
1. This Agreement is governed by Polish law. Applicable regulatory references include Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Diplomatic privileges governed by Vienna Convention shall be observed.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
For MSZ RP: ____________________
Date: __________

Annexes: A – List of authorised drivers and Poświadczenie Bezpieczeństwa copies; B – Standard operating procedures (SOP) for embassy deliveries; C – Insurance certificate; D – Sample chain of custody form.

title: RecyclingGlass Reverse Logistics Agreement for Glass Containers between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Vetropack Polska S.A.
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-032
RecyclingGlass Reverse Logistics Agreement
Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 100, 00-001 Warszawa, Polska, depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań (hereinafter "LogiCore"), a Polish limited liability company, registered under KRS, VAT PL..., BDO registration ...; and
2. Vetropack Polska S.A., ul. Przykładowa 1, 63-800 Gostyń (factory: Gostyń) (hereinafter "Vetropack"), together the "Parties".
Recitals
LogiCore will provide reverse logistics services for collection, transport and delivery of empty glass bottles and jars (including cullet) from retail chains and HoReCa across Mazowsze and Śląsk to Vetropack's factory in Gostyń. Parties agree terms below in accordance with applicable Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe), Ustawa o odpadach and Prawo o ruchu drogowym, and subject to regulatory oversight (GITD, UTK where applicable, UOKiK considerations).
Scope of Services
1. Collection: regular weekly collections from forty-five (45) designated collection points (zlecenie transportowe schedule attached as Annex A) and on-demand collections for overflow. Weekly routes cover Mazowsze and Śląsk, consolidating loads to Gostyń.
2. Material: color-sorted glass — clear (bezbarwne), green (zielone), brown (bursztynowe). Contaminated loads (foreign materials, non-glass, mixed colors beyond agreed tolerance) constitute rejection at factory gate.
3. Volume: estimated 800–1,200 tonnes per month, with seasonal variation (higher volumes in summer months due to increased beer/HoReCa consumption).
Equipment and Compliance
1. Vehicles: roll-off containers for intact bottles/jars, walking floor trailers for cullet (broken glass). All vehicles meet roadworthiness, ADR where applicable, and GITD/Inspections. Drivers carry CMR/list przewozowy for each transport.
2. Weight and Axles: glass is heavy; LogiCore shall ensure strict compliance with axle load limits per Prawo o ruchu drogowym and local weight restrictions. Overloading is prohibited; any fines or penalties from authorities (GITD) caused by LogiCore non-compliance are borne by LogiCore.
3. Environmental & Waste Law: transport and handling shall comply with Ustawa o odpadach. LogiCore is registered in BDO and will provide BDO numbers and waste transfer documentation. Vetropack will be provided with full documentation for acceptance.
Service Levels, Quality & Penalties
1. Scheduling: LogiCore to perform weekly scheduled collections from 45 points; on-demand collections to be executed within 24 hours of request where operationally feasible.
2. Acceptance: Vetropack reserves right to reject contaminated loads at gate. Contaminated loads: penalty EUR 100 per rejected load plus cost of return or disposal by LogiCore.
3. Late collection: due to limited storage at retailers, any scheduled collection performed later than agreed window incurs penalty EUR 200 per occurrence.
4. Reporting: daily manifests, weight tickets, and BDO waste transfer notes to be transmitted electronically within 24 hours of delivery.
Pricing, Invoicing and Annual Value
1. Annual estimated value: EUR 230,000. Price schedule per tonne and per call-out attached as Annex B. Monthly invoices (faktura) issued in EUR, payable within 30 days net.
Liability and Insurance
1. Liability governed by Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe. LogiCore maintains commercial liability insurance and cargo insurance covering transported material. Damage to third parties, losses, or contamination caused by LogiCore or its subcontractors are indemnified by LogiCore.
2. Subcontracting permitted with prior written consent; LogiCore remains fully responsible for subcontractor performance.
Termination
1. Either Party may terminate for material breach with 30 days' notice if breach not cured. Immediate termination for repeated contamination incidents or regulatory non-compliance jeopardizing Vetropack operations.
Force Majeure
1. Neither Party liable for delays due to force majeure; obligations suspended during event. Parties to mitigate and notify promptly.
Regulatory and Audit Rights
1. LogiCore to maintain BDO, waste manifests, vehicle permits, and compliance records for inspection by Vetropack. Vetropack may audit once per year with reasonable notice.
Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
1. This Agreement governed by Polish law. Disputes resolved by competent courts in Warszawa unless Parties agree mediation.
Miscellaneous
1. Confidentiality: commercial terms and route lists are confidential. 2. Entire agreement; amendments in writing.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________ Date: ___
For Vetropack Polska S.A. ________________ Date: ___
Annexes: A — Collection points & schedule; B — Price list and invoicing rules; C — Insurance certificates; D — BDO and waste codes.

title: LivestockFeed Animal Feed Bulk Transport Agreement — Bulk Feed Logistics Services
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-033
LivestockFeed Animal Feed Bulk Transport Agreement

This Agreement is entered into on 01 April 2025 between:
1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, ul. Example 10, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter “Operator” or “LogiCore”), and
2) PasFeed Polska Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Poland (hereinafter “Customer” or “PasFeed”).

Preamble
LogiCore undertakes to provide bulk transport services for animal feed produced by PasFeed, including pellets, meals and mineral supplements, in accordance with the terms below, applicable Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) and industry standards including GMP+ International B4. Regulatory authorities relevant to the services include UTK, GITD and UOKiK.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Operator will transport bulk animal feed from PasFeed mills in Kutno and Bydgoszcz to farms and collection points across Mazowsze, Podlasie and Warmia-Mazury. Routes and schedules shall be agreed by zlecenie transportowe issued by Customer at least 48 hours before loading except in agreed emergency cases.
1.2 Freight will be carried in pneumatic bulk tankers (blow-off) and tipper trailers suitable for dry bulk feed. Vehicles shall be maintained and operated in a manner compliant with GMP+ International B4.
1.3 Products: feed pellets, meals, mineral supplements and other bulk non-liquid feed materials as specified in individual orders.

2. Hygiene, Cross-Contamination Prevention and Cleaning
2.1 Medicated feed versus non‑medicated feed: under no circumstances shall medicated feed be transported in the same vehicle with non‑medicated feed without a documented full cleaning and sanitisation cycle consistent with GMP+ B4. Cross‑loading without cleaning is strictly prohibited.
2.2 Cleaning certificates: Operator shall provide a cleaning certificate (certyfikat czyszczenia) after every load and prior to loading a different product type. Certificates must specify vehicle registration, date/time, cleaning method, responsible person and signature. Certificates shall accompany the list przewozowy/CMR and be retained for 24 months and made available for audits.
2.3 Cross-contamination liability: In the event of contamination attributable to Operator, Operator shall pay a penalty of EUR 1,000 per confirmed cross-contamination event and shall indemnify Customer for recall, disposal and reputational costs to the extent permitted under this Agreement.

3. GMP+ Compliance and Audits
3.1 Operator represents that transport services will be GMP+ certified per GMP+ International B4 standard. Operator will permit scheduled and reasonable unscheduled audits by Customer or Customer’s designee. Non-conformities identified in an audit must be remedied within the agreed corrective action timeframe.

4. Drivers and Biosecurity
4.1 Drivers shall be trained in farm biosecurity protocols including, but not limited to, wheel wash procedures, respect for restricted zones, use of boot covers, vehicle movement controls and on-farm hygiene. Training records will be provided upon request.
4.2 Drivers shall carry and follow farm-specific biosecurity instructions provided with each zlecenie transportowe.

5. Schedules, Seasonality and Performance
5.1 Services are seasonal and year-round: peak volumes occur in autumn (silage supplement season); steady volumes are maintained year-round for dairy and poultry supply chains. Operator shall allocate capacity accordingly.
5.2 Late delivery: timely feeding is critical. Late deliveries attributable to Operator shall incur a penalty of EUR 100 per late delivery event (per delivery note) where time-critical schedules were specified on the zlecenie transportowe.

6. Documentation, Invoicing and Value
6.1 Each carriage shall be accompanied by a list przewozowy/CMR, zlecenie transportowe and cleaning certificate. Faktura will be issued monthly in EUR and paid within 30 days net unless otherwise agreed.
6.2 Annual estimated value of services under this Agreement is EUR 310,000. This estimate is non-binding and actual volumes may vary; pricing and tariffs are set out in Annex A.

7. Liability and Insurance
7.1 Parties’ liability is governed by applicable law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe). Operator shall maintain cargo and liability insurance adequate for bulk feed transport and provide certificates of insurance upon request.
7.2 Penalties specified in clauses 2.3 and 5.2 are liquidated damages and payable without prejudice to Customer’s right to claim additional proven losses.

8. Force Majeure and Termination
8.1 Force majeure events shall be governed by clause consistent with Kodeks Cywilny. Repeated failure by Operator to meet cleaning or biosecurity obligations, or two confirmed cross-contamination events within 12 months, shall constitute material breach and entitle Customer to termination for cause.

9. Confidentiality and Competition
9.1 Commercial terms, routes and customer lists are confidential. Competition law compliance (UOKiK) must be observed; any exchange of commercially sensitive information shall be limited and documented.

10. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
10.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of Poland. Disputes shall be settled by negotiation or, if unresolved, by courts competent for Warszawa.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________
For PasFeed Polska Sp. z o.o. ______________

Annexes: Pricing (Annex A), Vehicle Specification and Maintenance (Annex B), GMP+ Certification Copies, Driver Training Records, Sample Cleaning Certificate template.

title: SteelDistribution Steel Products Transport Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and ArcelorMittal Poland S.A.
text:
CONTRACT No. CTR-2025-034
SteelDistribution — Steel Products Transport Agreement
between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (hereinafter "LogiCore") and ArcelorMittal Poland S.A. (hereinafter "Customer")

1. Parties and Scope
1.1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. [Headquarters], Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań, NIP/REGON as per company records, provides road transport and logistics services. Customer: ArcelorMittal Poland S.A., a steel producer and distributor.
1.2. This Agreement (Umowa) governs the carriage, handling and delivery of steel products (coils, plates, beams, rebar, tubes — collectively "Goods") from Customer's mills (primary origins: Dąbrowa Górnicza and Kraków) to construction sites, fabricators and service centres across Poland and EU where agreed.
1.3. Contract number: CTR-2025-034. Annual value estimated at EUR 1,100,000 (one million one hundred thousand euros).

2. Transport Services
2.1. Vehicle types: coil wells (saddle trailers) for coils, flatbeds with stanchions for plates/rebar/tubes, extendable trailers for long beams. All equipment must be appropriate for extremely heavy loads and meet statutory requirements (Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Prawo Przewozowe).
2.2. Weight-critical handling: Parties acknowledge individual coils may weigh up to 25 tonnes. Carriage must ensure strict axle weight distribution and comply with road axle limits and vehicle loading guidance. Overloading or improper axle distribution is prohibited.
2.3. Load securing and lashing: All loads must be secured in accordance with EN 12195-1. The driver (kierowca) is responsible for securing the load at all times and shall complete a load securing checklist (formularz) before departure. LogiCore will train drivers and provide documentation; Customer will advise of any specific cargo constraints.
2.4. Weather protection: Tarpaulin cover (plandeka) is mandatory for coils and plates during rain or when weather may cause rusting. Failure to cover, if attributable to LogiCore, constitutes breach of contract with remedies as below.

3. Operations and Coordination
3.1. Routes and scheduling: Regular routes originate from Dąbrowa Górnicza and Kraków mills to construction sites, fabricators and service centres. Seasonal pattern: March–November construction peak; winter months reduced but steady forwarding to fabricators.
3.2. Crane offload: Most deliveries require crane offload at destination. LogiCore will coordinate crane services with sites and confirm crane readiness on zlecenie transportowe. If destination lacks crane and Customer fails to notify, additional costs and delays will be allocated per clause 6.
3.3. Documentation: Each carriage will be accompanied by CMR where applicable, Polish list przewozowy or appropriate transport documents. Any deviations shall be reported immediately.

4. Safety, Liability and Incidents
4.1. Safety critical incidents: Load shift causing danger, loss of cargo integrity or risk to persons is a material breach. In the event of a load shift incident attributable to LogiCore or its subcontractors, penalty EUR 5,000 per incident applies, plus full liability for damage and remediation.
4.2. Late delivery penalty: EUR 200 per late delivery event unless delay results from force majeure, acts of God, Customer site restrictions or documented third-party delays beyond LogiCore's control.
4.3. Insurance: LogiCore shall maintain insurance covering carriage (OC przewoźnika, cargo insurance) consistent with Prawo Przewozowe and CMR limits. Detailed insurance policy summaries will be provided on request.

5. Legal and Regulatory Compliance
5.1. Both Parties shall comply with applicable Polish and EU law, including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and guidance from regulator bodies UTK, GITD and competition rules under UOKiK.
5.2. Drivers and vehicles must comply with axle weight limits, tachograph rules and technical roadworthiness.

6. Prices, Payments and Remedies
6.1. Pricing: Rates per load, per km and ancillary services shall be agreed in annexed rate sheet. Invoices (faktura) shall be issued monthly and payable within 30 days unless otherwise agreed.
6.2. Adjustments: Seasonal demand (peak months March–November) may trigger rate adjustments by mutual agreement; winter duty rates shall be accommodated within agreed annual value.
6.3. Penalties and deductions: Customer reserves the right to deduct penalties (EUR 200 per late delivery; EUR 5,000 per safety-critical load shift) from invoices or seek recovery separately. Recurrent breaches may trigger termination.

7. Subcontracting and Depots
7.1. LogiCore may subcontract carriage but remains fully liable under this Agreement. Subcontractors shall meet the same technical and insurance standards.
7.2. Depot handling and temporary storage may be performed at LogiCore depots (Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań) under agreed terms.

8. Term, Termination and Force Majeure
8.1. Term: This Agreement is effective upon signature and runs for 12 months, renewable unless notice provided 60 days prior to expiry.
8.2. Termination for cause: material breach (including repeated load-shift incidents or failure to secure loads per EN 12195-1) permits termination with immediate effect.
8.3. Force majeure: Events beyond reasonable control relieve performance obligations for the duration of such event; affected Party must notify the other without undue delay.

9. Confidentiality and Competition
9.1. Both Parties shall treat operational data, pricing and Customer information as confidential. Compliance with UOKiK obligations is required.

10. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
10.1. This Agreement is governed by Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny). Disputes shall be referred to the competent court in Warszawa unless Parties agree arbitration.

11. Miscellaneous
11.1. Amendments must be in writing. Operational instructions (instrukcje) and specific zlecenie transportowe will be issued per load.
