For ArcelorMittal Poland S.A. ........................  Date: __ / __ / 2025

Annexes: Rate Sheet, Driver Load Securing Checklist (EN 12195-1 compliance), Insurance Certificates, Depot Handling Rules, Crane Coordination Protocol.

title: CosmeticsDistro Beauty Product Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Inglot Sp. z o.o.
text:
COSMETICSDISTRO BEAUTY PRODUCT DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT
Contract No.: CTR-2025-035
Parties: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska, Warszawa (headquarters), depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań (hereinafter: Carrier/LogiCore) and Inglot Sp. z o.o., Przemyśl factory and registered office in Poland (hereinafter: Principal/Inglot).
Preamble: This Agreement governs transport, distribution, store replenishment and returns handling for high-value, temperature-sensitive and fragile cosmetics and pre-merchandised display units from Inglot factory in Przemyśl to approximately 80 own-brand stores and department store counters across Poland and selected EU destinations (DE, FR, and shipments to UK via Northern Ireland following post-Brexit requirements).
1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall provide dedicated regular linehaul, multi-drop distribution, temperature-controlled transport, specialist packaging handling, visual merchandising delivery (pre-merchandised display units delivered ready for shelf placement) and reverse logistics for returns and disposal. Services include door-to-shelf delivery, placement of display units, and collection of returns per Section 6.
1.2 Routes: domestic (PL) linehauls from Przemyśl to LogiCore depots and direct to stores; cross-border to DE, FR and UK (via NI). All international movements will be executed with appropriate CMR consignment notes or list przewozowy and compliant customs documentation where required.
2. Product characteristics and handling requirements
2.1 Temperature sensitivity: Certain SKUs degrade when exposed to temperatures exceeding 30°C. LogiCore will provide temperature-controlled vehicles with continuous data logging, 24/7 remote monitoring and alerts. Any exposure >30°C will be recorded and notified to Inglot within 4 hours of detection.
2.2 Fragility: products include glass bottles, mirrors and compacts. Target breakage rate is less than 0.2% per shipment. Specialized cushioning, palletisation standards and trained handlers will be used. Breakage metrics will be reported monthly.
2.3 Visual merchandising: display units arrive pre-merchandised, labelled and ready for immediate shelf placement. Installation teams will follow Inglot merchandising guides and provide photographic proof-of-delivery (POD).
3. Performance standards and KPIs
3.1 On-time delivery: LogiCore shall meet scheduled delivery windows. Late deliveries incur a penalty of EUR 200 per incident, payable to Inglot upon invoice deduction.
3.2 Damage liability: For premium products damaged due to carrier negligence, LogiCore will reimburse the full retail value of the affected items. A formal claims process, supported by POD, photos and CMR/list przewozowy, will be used.
3.3 Breakage KPI: monthly breakage rate must remain below 0.2%. Repeated failure triggers corrective action plan and potential termination for cause under Clause 10.
4. Pricing and payment
4.1 Annual contract value is estimated at EUR 270,000. Rates for domestic PL movements will be denominated and invoiced in PLN when agreed as domestic services; cross-border services and penalties are invoiced in EUR. Invoices (faktura) payable within 30 days net.
4.2 Seasonal peaks: surcharge structure for November (Christmas gift sets), February (Valentine's) and May (Mother's Day) to be agreed in annex; LogiCore shall provide capacity planning and additional resources during these windows.
5. Customs, regulatory and compliance
5.1 Cross-border shipments to EU states will observe Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Prawo Przewozowe and CMR obligations. UK shipments via Northern Ireland will follow post-Brexit customs complexity and Northern Ireland Protocol requirements; additional customs declarations, EORI handling and potential transit documentation will be managed by LogiCore or nominated customs broker at Client cost.
5.2 LogiCore warrants compliance with regulatory bodies UTK, GITD, and consumer protection obligations under UOKiK where applicable. All carriers used will be licensed and insured.
6. Returns, recalls and destruction
6.1 Quarterly collection: damaged stock, expired products and end-of-line returns shall be collected quarterly from designated depots or stores. LogiCore will issue a zlecenie transportowe for collections and provide a certificate of destruction upon final disposal. Costs for destruction are part of service fees unless volume exceeds forecast, when additional charges apply.
6.2 Product recalls: In the event of a recall, LogiCore will execute expedited reverse logistics per Inglot instructions and applicable law (Kodeks Cywilny for contractual obligations and consumer law for safety recalls).
7. Insurance and liability
7.1 LogiCore will maintain goods-in-transit insurance and public liability insurance. Liability for loss or damage follows CMR and Prawo Przewozowe rules. Where contract stipulates full retail reimbursement for premium items, LogiCore's maximum reimbursement shall include value of goods plus reasonable handling costs.
8. Data, reporting and audits
8.1 LogiCore will provide weekly operational reports, monthly KPI dashboards (including temperature excursions, breakage rate, on-time delivery %), and ad-hoc reports during peak seasons. Inglot retains the right to audit relevant records upon reasonable notice.
9. Force majeure and dispute resolution
9.1 Standard force majeure applies. Parties will endeavor to mitigate disruptions.
9.2 Governing law: Polish law. Disputes resolved by courts of Warszawa unless parties agree otherwise.
10. Term, termination and confidentiality
10.1 Term: 24 months from signature, renewable. Early termination for material breach, including repeated failure to meet breakage KPI or unresolved temperature excursion incidents.
10.2 Confidentiality: Commercial and product information classified confidential shall be protected. Competition law compliance under UOKiK is required.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.______ For Inglot Sp. z o.o.______
Date: ______
Annexes: Service Level Annex, Pricing Annex, Customs Procedure Annex, Seasonal Capacity Plan.

title: TireLogistics Seasonal Tire Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Michelin Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
AGREEMENT CTR-2025-036
TireLogistics Seasonal Tire Distribution

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 10, 00-001 Warszawa, depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań ("LogiCore");
2. Michelin Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Przykładowa 1, 10-100 Olsztyn ("Michelin").

Preamble
LogiCore and Michelin conclude this agreement for seasonal distribution and reverse logistics of passenger and light-truck tires produced at Michelin factory (Olsztyn) and from Michelin import warehouse, delivering to approx. 300 tire service centers (serwisy opon) across Poland. Parties recognise extreme seasonality of the cargo: 70% of annual volume concentrates in two peak months (April and October).

Term
This Agreement is effective from 01.03.2025 to 28.02.2026, renewable by mutual written consent.

Scope of Services
LogiCore will provide: (a) line haul and last-mile transport from Olsztyn factory and import warehouse to ~300 locations; (b) pre-season overflow storage 2,000 m² for build-up; (c) handling, stacking and palletisation; (d) reverse logistics and collection of used tires (odzysk opon) in compliance with Ustawa o odpadach and Michelin's recycling programmes.

Seasonality and Capacity
Season windows: summer tires distribution March–May (peak: April), winter tires distribution September–November (peak: October). Peak staffing and fleet: dedicated 10 vehicles operational during peak months. Off-peak baseline: 3 standard vehicles. Parties agree dynamic load planning; LogiCore will submit weekly schedule (zlecenie transportowe) during peaks.

Storage and Handling
LogiCore provides 2,000 m² overflow storage during pre-season. Tires shall be stacked max 4 tires high per pallet. No heavy cargo to be placed atop tire pallets due to deformation risk. All pallets and loads will be accompanied by CMR or appropriate list przewozowy and handling instructions. Any deviation from stacking rules requires Michelin's prior written approval.

Service Levels and Penalties
Timely delivery is critical. Late delivery penalty per delivery: EUR 100 during peak months (April, October) and EUR 50 off-peak. Penalties apply where delay is attributable to LogiCore and results in documented loss of opportunity for service centre (e.g., customers waiting). Penalties are deductible from next faktura or invoiced separately. Service Level Agreement (SLA) target: 98% on-time deliveries weekly during peak.

Returns and Reverse Logistic
LogiCore will collect used tires for recycling (odzysk opon) under Michelin's instruction. Collections to comply with Ustawa o odpadach; LogiCore to maintain documentation for waste chain and handover to authorised recyclers. Reverse logistics costs are included in the annual fee unless otherwise agreed in zlecenie.

Fleet, Drivers, Compliance
LogiCore shall operate vehicles meeting technical and legal requirements under Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Drivers hold required qualifications and tachograph records. LogiCore will cooperate with UTK, GITD, and allow audits by Michelin or authorised regulator upon notice.

Compensation
Annual contract value: EUR 410,000 (four hundred ten thousand euros). Pricing covers transport, overflow storage up to 2,000 m² during pre-season, handling, stacking within specified limits, and standard reverse logistics. Faktura monthly with 30-day payment term unless otherwise agreed.

Liability and Insurance
LogiCore liable for loss or damage resulting from its negligence; limits per shipment follow CMR conventions where applicable, supplemented by commercial liability insurance. Parties shall carry adequate cargo and liability insurance; certificates available on request.

Force Majeure
Events beyond reasonable control (force majeure) suspend obligations; affected party must notify other party promptly. Seasonal surges shall not constitute force majeure.

Confidentiality and Competition
Both parties maintain confidentiality of commercial terms, customer lists and transport data. Compliance with UOKiK rules on competition and antitrust.

Termination
Either party may terminate for material breach after 30 days cure period. Termination for convenience requires 90 days' notice outside peak windows.

Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
This Agreement governed by Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe). Disputes resolved by Polish courts in Warszawa, unless parties agree mediation.

Miscellaneous
Amendments only by written annex. Operational instructions provided as annexes (route lists, service centre contacts, handling rules). All transport accompanied by CMR or list przewozowy. LogiCore will provide monthly reports on volumes, capacity utilisation, returns collected and storage usage.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________ Date: ______
For Michelin Polska Sp. z o.o. ____________________ Date: ______

Annexes: Annex A – Route List (Olsztyn + import warehouse to 300 serwisy opon); Annex B – Handling & Stacking Rules; Annex C – Reverse Logistics & Waste Handover Procedure (Ustawa o odpadach compliance).

title: BookDistribution Publishing Logistics Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Wydawnictwo Znak Sp. z o.o.
text:
CONTRACT CTR-2025-037
BookDistribution Publishing Logistics Agreement
Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 88, 00-545 Warszawa (hereinafter: LogiCore), depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań.
2. Wydawnictwo Znak Sp. z o.o., ul. Basztowa 8, 31-143 Kraków (hereinafter: Znak).
Preamble
This Agreement sets out terms for national and direct-to-retail distribution of printed books (including heavy, low-margin, time-sensitive new releases), parcel fulfilment for e-commerce, and quarterly returns. The parties acknowledge the special requirements of publishing logistics: strict embargo dates, coordinated national launch, high pallet weights, vehicle payload constraints and seasonal volume fluctuations. Annual contract value is estimated at EUR 190,000.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 Transport and distribution of publication runs from printing houses in Kraków and Białystok to Znak's central warehouse (hereinafter: Warehouse) and direct deliveries to bookstore chains including Empik and Świat Książki, according to zlecenie transportowe issued by Znak.
1.2 Coordinated national launch logistics: LogiCore will execute a coordinated delivery plan to ensure all designated stores receive and stock new releases on the same calendar day (embargo date). Deliveries visible to the public prior to the embargo date constitute an embargo breach.
1.3 Parcel fulfilment for Znak's e-commerce: order picking and parcel-level dispatch through LogiCore's parcel network with tracking, returns handling and integration with Znak's order management system.
1.4 Quarterly returns: unsold copies shall be collected from retail partners quarterly under standard right of return practises in publishing (prawo zwrotu). Returns to be transported to Warehouse or returned to Znak's nominated location per Znak's instructions.
2. Operational Parameters
2.1 Pallet weight: full pallet of hardcovers = 900 kg. Parties acknowledge vehicle payload is critical; LogiCore will assign vehicles with appropriate payload capacities. Any deviation in vehicle payload affecting performance must be approved in writing.
2.2 Documentation: domestic shipments accompanied by list przewozowy and internal zlecenie transportowe; international movements (if any) by CMR. Packing lists must state number of pallets, net/gross weight, and ISBNs.
2.3 SLA: For embargoed releases, deliveries must be completed during the agreed delivery window on the embargo date. For non-embargoed standard deliveries to Warehouse or retail: 24-72 hours depending on route.
3. Seasonal Peaks and Capacity
3.1 Parties acknowledge peak periods: Christmas season (Oct–Dec) with up to +60% volume increase, school year start (Aug–Sep) for textbooks, and literary awards season. LogiCore will provide scaled resources and notify Znak of capacity constraints at least 30 days prior to peak.
4. Pricing, Invoicing and Annual Value
4.1 Annual estimated value: EUR 190,000. Rates for transport, pallet handling, parcel fulfilment, storage and returns attached as Annex A. Invoices (faktura) issued monthly, payment terms: 30 days net. Currency: EUR.
5. Penalties and Credits
5.1 Embargo breach (early delivery such that books are visible to public before embargo date): penalty EUR 500 per incident (per delivery stop where visibility occurred). 5.2 Late delivery (missed agreed delivery window on embargo date): penalty EUR 100 per delayed delivery stop. 5.3 Penalties shall be offset against invoices; unresolved disputes to be settled within 30 days. Penalties do not exclude right to claim greater damages for gross negligence or willful misconduct under Kodeks Cywilny.
6. Liability and Insurance
6.1 LogiCore maintains carrier liability and cargo insurance covering full agreed value of goods. Liability limits follow Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, except for losses caused by willful misconduct or gross negligence where full compensation applies. 6.2 For parcel fulfilment, standard parcel network liability and insurance levels apply; additional insurance available upon request.
7. Returns and Credit Notes
7.1 Returns processed quarterly. Upon receipt and inspection, Znak will receive condition reports and credit notes for accepted returns. Discrepancies discovered within 14 days of return receipt must be notified.
8. Compliance and Authorities
8.1 Both parties will comply with applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and regulations from UTK, GITD and UOKiK. Data processing in relation to orders and recipient data will comply with RODO/GDPR.
9. Force Majeure
9.1 Delays resulting from force majeure (including but not limited to strikes, natural disasters, governmental embargoes) suspend obligations for the duration of the event. The affected party must notify the other promptly.
10. Term and Termination
10.1 Term: 12 months from signature, auto-renewal for successive 12-month periods unless terminated with 60 days' written notice. 10.2 Immediate termination for material breach (including repeated embargo breaches) after 14-day cure period.
11. Confidentiality
11.1 Parties shall keep confidential all commercial terms, launch schedules, and embargo information. Breach entitles non-breaching party to injunctive relief and damages.
12. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
12.1 Governing law: Republic of Poland. Disputes to be resolved by court with jurisdiction over Warszawa, or by arbitration if agreed. Polish law references include Kodeks Cywilny and relevant transport statutes.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Name: ___________________ Title: __________________ Date: _______
For Wydawnictwo Znak Sp. z o.o.
Name: ___________________ Title: __________________ Date: _______
Annexes: Rate Card (Annex A), Operational Plan for Embargo Launches (Annex B), EDI/Integration Spec for e-commerce, Insurance Certificates.

title: FlowerFresh Cut Flower Logistics Agreement — Contract CTR-2025-038 between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and FloraHolland Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
FLOWERFRESH CUT FLOWER LOGISTICS AGREEMENT
Contract No.: CTR-2025-038
Date: ___________________
Parties:
1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 120, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland, NIP: _______, hereinafter "LogiCore" (carrier/contractor);
2) FloraHolland Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Różańska 5, 02-148 Warszawa (subsidiary of Royal FloraHolland), hereinafter "Customer" (zleceniodawca).
Recitals
A. The Customer purchases cut flowers (roses, chrysanthemums, mixed cut-flowers) from auctions and imports (Netherlands/Kenya/Colombia) for distribution to wholesalers across Poland. These goods are extremely perishable: value degrades by the hour; maximum 48 hours farm-to-vase.
B. LogiCore provides specialised temperature-controlled transport and handling services meeting strict post-harvest requirements.
Agreement
1. Definitions
- "Zlecenie transportowe" means each transport order placed by the Customer.
- "Batch" means the set of pallets/shippers documented on a single CMR / list przewozowy for a zlecenie.
- "Temperature breach" means any recorded ambient compartment temperature outside 2-5°C for more than 15 continuous minutes.
2. Scope of Services
2.1 LogiCore shall collect imported consignments at Warszawa Okęcie Airport (EPWA) following arrival from Netherlands, Kenya, Colombia and other specified origins, verify required import documentation (phytosanitary certificate, invoices, airway bill), and deliver to Customer-designated wholesalers across Poland (Warszawa, Kraków, Poznań, Wrocław, Gdańsk, and regional depots).
2.2 Transport is time-critical: auction results available at 05:00 CET. Customer will confirm zlecenie by 05:30 where applicable. LogiCore shall ensure loading completed by 08:00 CET and final delivery to wholesalers by 16:00 CET same day unless otherwise agreed in writing.
2.3 Vehicles shall be equipped with active ethylene filters (ethylene-free environment), reefer units with temperature control capable of maintaining 2-5°C ±0.5°C, adjustable humidity control (relative humidity 90±5% as standard for roses), and continuous temperature/ethylene logging with GPS telematics.
2.4 Vehicles transporting roses for bucket-shipment shall carry potable water buckets and secure bucket mounts; drivers trained in bucket-shipment handling.
3. Vehicle and Handling Requirements
3.1 All vehicles shall be reefer-equipped with ethylene scrubbing/adsorption units, adjustable humidity control, and internal temperature/ethylene monitoring configured to alert LogiCore operations and Customer in real time.
3.2 Pallets must be ISPM-15 compliant. LogiCore will inspect ISPM-15 stamping at airport; missing compliance will be reported immediately. Customer is responsible for remediation costs and any delays if pallets fail ISPM-15 or phytosanitary checks.
3.3 CMR / list przewozowy shall accompany each international road leg. Airway bill and phytosanitary certificate must be presented at handover. LogiCore will not load consignments lacking required phytosanitary certificate; any consequent delay/costs are Customer's responsibility unless LogiCore caused failure.
4. Time-Critical Service Levels and Peaks
4.1 Standard SLA: Load by 08:00 CET; deliver by 16:00 CET same day. Auction results at 05:00 CET; Customer commits to provide timely zlecenie transportowe.
4.2 Extreme peaks: Valentine's Day (14 Feb) — expected volume up to 5x normal; Mother's Day — up to 3x normal; All Saints' Day — up to 4x normal (chrysanthemums). Parties shall pre-plan capacity, with Customer providing forecasts no later than 30 days prior to peak events. Additional temporary rates for peak surcharges will be agreed by addendum.
5. Penalties and Liability
5.1 Temperature breach: Upon confirmed temperature breach (per clause 2.2), LogiCore shall be liable for a compensation penalty of EUR 500 per temperature breach event affecting a Batch. Customer may deem an affected Batch as unsaleable and require collection and composting; LogiCore shall reimburse costs related to composting and pay the penalty.
5.2 Time penalty: If delivery occurs after 12:00 CET on the day of loading, LogiCore shall pay EUR 200 per hour for each hour late thereafter until delivery. Penalties accrue hourly from 12:00 CET. Delivery after the contractual delivery time (16:00) may additionally be subject to agreed loss mitigation measures and dispute resolution. Penalties are payable by invoice deduction or offset against faktura payable to LogiCore.
5.3 Liability shall otherwise be governed by applicable provisions of Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Parties acknowledge regulatory oversight by UTK, GITD and consumer protection by UOKiK where applicable.
6. Customs and Phytosanitary Compliance
6.1 At Warszawa Okęcie Airport LogiCore will verify phytosanitary certificate and ISPM-15 pallet compliance prior to acceptance. Absence or irregularity entitles LogiCore to refuse carriage or to quarantine at Customer cost. Customer indemnifies LogiCore for fines, storage or re-export costs arising from missing or false phytosanitary documentation.
7. Commercial Terms
7.1 Annual estimated contract value: EUR 340,000 (subject to actual volumes). Rates and surcharge schedule attached as Annex A. Invoices (faktura) shall be issued monthly net 14 days unless otherwise agreed.
7.2 For peak periods (clause 4.2) LogiCore may apply agreed peak surcharges; parties will endeavour to agree surcharges in writing at least 21 days prior to peak.
8. Force Majeure
8.1 Neither party is liable for delays due to force majeure events (act of God, war, strikes, major airspace closure, extraordinary weather) but must notify other party within 6 hours. Time-critical nature of goods will require best-efforts mitigation and coordination.
9. Term and Termination
9.1 This Agreement commences on signature and continues for 12 months. Termination for material breach (including repeated temperature breaches or failure to meet loading/delivery SLAs) may be made with 7 days' cure period. Immediate termination possible for gross negligence causing loss of consignments.
10. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
10.1 Polish law governs this Agreement. Disputes to be referred to courts in Warszawa. Applicable statutes include Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.
11. Miscellaneous
11.1 Notices in Polish/English exchanged by email and followed by zlecenie transportowe. Amendment only by written annex signed by authorised representatives.
Signatures:
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________
For FloraHolland Polska Sp. z o.o. ____________________
Date: ____________________
Annexes: Annex A — Rates and Peak Surcharge Schedule; Annex B — Vehicle & Equipment Certification (ethylenefilters, reefer calibration, humidity control); Annex C — SOP for bucket-shipped roses and water bucket maintenance.
Executed in two counterparts.

title: PaintCoatings Industrial Paint Distribution Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Śnieżka S.A.
text:
Agreement CTR-2025-039
PaintCoatings Industrial Paint Distribution Agreement
Parties: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Headquarter, Warszawa, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań (hereinafter "Carrier/LogiCore") and Śnieżka S.A., a Polish manufacturer of paint and coatings (hereinafter "Principal/Śnieżka"). Effective date: 01 March 2025. Governing law: Republic of Poland (Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym). Regulatory oversight: UTK, GITD, UOKiK as applicable.
1. Scope of services
1.1 Carrier shall provide transport, storage-in-transit and distribution of finished paint and coatings produced at Principal factories in Lubzina and Brzeźnica to retail chains (Castorama, Leroy Merlin, OBI) and registered paint distributors in Poland and intra-EU routes as agreed in zlecenie transportowe.
1.2 Product types: (a) Water-based paints (non-hazardous for transport); (b) Solvent-based paints classified ADR Class 3 (flammable liquids). Transport documentation: CMR/list przewozowy and, for ADR loads, full ADR documentation on board and available electronically.
2. ADR / non-ADR dual logistics and segregation
2.1 Mixed loads permitted only within ADR exemption thresholds and packing instructions specified by ADR for limited quantities. Where mixed loads occur, strict segregation must be maintained on vehicle by sealed physical partition or dedicated compartments. Water-based non-ADR products must be separated from ADR Class 3 solvent-based products and secured to prevent cross-contamination, spillage and heat transfer.
2.2 Carrier is responsible for driver training, ADR certifications and vehicle signage. Carrier shall comply with ADR provisions and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym when transporting solvent-based products; UTK/GITD inspections to be facilitated.
3. Temperature and handling requirements
3.1 Temperature-controlled carriage: water-based paints must not be exposed to temperatures at or below +5°C (freezing or near-freezing storage degrades product quality). All products must not exceed +30°C in transit or storage. Where temperature-controlled vehicles are used, records (DATAs log) must accompany each delivery. Deviations trigger immediate notification and hold at consignee pending inspection.
3.2 Special handling: 10 L buckets on pallets require correct stacking and load restraint. Palletisation patterns, strap tension and pallet integrity checks are mandatory. Maximum pallet weight and axle loading must comply with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and current vehicle GVW limits; Carrier shall ensure pallet weights do not exceed agreed limits (standard maximum pallet weight shall not exceed 800 kg unless otherwise approved in writing).
4. Routes and schedules
4.1 Primary origins: Lubzina, Brzeźnica. Primary destinations: Castorama, Leroy Merlin, OBI national depots and contracted regional paint distributors. Seasonal peak: spring (March–June) constitutes approximately 55% of annual volume; Carrier shall allocate additional capacity accordingly and submit seasonal capacity plan by 01 February each year.
5. Quantity, invoicing and contract value
5.1 Annual estimated value of services: EUR 450,000 (approx. PLN 2,025,000 at indicative conversion 1 EUR = 4.50 PLN). Final billing and faktura in EUR or PLN shall be agreed on zlecenie transportowe; domestic operations will be invoiced in PLN unless agreed otherwise.
6. Quality assurance, returns and defective product handling
6.1 In-transit damage: damaged cans, particularly custom-tinted or bespoke formulas, are considered critical. Color matching is often irreplaceable; Carrier must implement special protective measures and immediate reporting procedures. For any damaged or leaking can, Carrier shall isolate the pallet, notify Principal within 2 hours of receipt or discovery, and await disposition instructions.
6.2 Returns: defective batches, product with expired shelf life or consignments rejected by consignee shall be returned to the factory (Lubzina or Brzeźnica) for recycling or environmentally-compliant disposal. Return costs and responsibility allocated as per incident root cause: Carrier responsible where transport negligence proven; otherwise costs borne by Principal. Disposal must comply with applicable environmental law and provide relevant certificates.
7. Penalties and liability
7.1 Timeliness: late delivery penalty EUR 100 per late delivery (approx. PLN 450) shall apply after agreed delivery windows elapsed and failure to meet corrective action. Chronic delays allow Principal to recover documented damages and to reassign volumes.
7.2 Product quality breach: in the event of frozen water-based paint leading to batch rejection, penalty EUR 500 per rejected batch (approx. PLN 2,250) plus full cost of return and recycling/disposal. Penalties do not limit Principal's right to claim full damages under Kodeks Cywilny where losses exceed these amounts.
7.3 Liability: Carrier liability limited according to Prawo Przewozowe for carriage but extended for willful misconduct or gross negligence including failure to segregate ADR/non-ADR loads, failure to maintain temperatures, or improper palletisation leading to color-damaging incidents.
8. Performance metrics and audits
8.1 KPI: On-time delivery, temperature compliance, damage rate, correct segregation. Quarterly performance review; failure to meet KPIs for two consecutive quarters permits Principal to terminate for cause.
8.2 Audits: Principal or its designated auditor may audit Carrier facilities, vehicles and records (including DATAs log, CMR, ADR documentation) with reasonable notice; compliance with UOKiK competitive rules respected.
9. Insurance and certificates
9.1 Carrier shall maintain adequate motor and cargo insurance covering ADR exposures and declared values including special-value bespoke tint batches. Proof of insurance and ADR certificates to be supplied annually and on request.
10. Term and termination
10.1 Term: 12 months commencing effective date with automatic renewal unless 90 days' written notice of termination. Immediate termination for material breach (temperature failures causing batch rejection, repeated segregation failures, or regulatory non-compliance).
11. Miscellaneous
11.1 Notices in Polish or English. Dispute resolution: Polish courts; attempts at amicable settlement and mediation prior to litigation. Contract interpreted in accordance with Polish law; Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny provisions governing carriage apply.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ____________________ Date: ___
For Śnieżka S.A. ________________________ Date: ___
Annexes: SOP for ADR/non-ADR segregation; Temperature monitoring specification; Seasonal capacity plan; Price schedule and tariffs (annual estimated value noted above).

title: EnergyParts Solar Panel Distribution — Transport and Logistics Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and SolarTech Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
CONTRACT No. CTR-2025-040
EnergyParts Solar Panel Distribution — Transport and Logistics Agreement
Between:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 10, 00-590 Warszawa (headquarters), with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań, NIP/REGON on file, hereinafter "LogiCore";
and
SolarTech Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Łódzka 5, 90-001 Łódź, hereinafter "Customer" or "SolarTech".
Preamble
This agreement ("Agreement") governs carriage, handling, storage, delivery and reverse logistics for photovoltaic (PV) modules ("panels"), inverters and mounting systems supplied by SolarTech and transported by LogiCore from the import warehouse in Łódź to installation companies across Poland. This Agreement is governed by applicable Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and applicable EU rules (CMR where applicable). Regulatory bodies referenced include UTK, GITD and UOKiK.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall provide multimodal transport, loading/unloading, protective packaging controls, staging at regional depots, last-mile delivery and reverse logistics (returns to manufacturer) in accordance with the Customer's zlecenie transportowe and agreed schedules.
1.2 Annual contract value is agreed at EUR 380,000 in Year 1, with forecast growth of +25% annually. Parties acknowledge seasonal spikes (March–September) and government subsidy deadlines that may increase required capacity.
2. Goods Description and Special Handling
2.1 Panels: framed glass-surface PV modules, fragile glass surfaces, heavy (25 kg each), dimensions approx. 2.0 m x 1.0 m. Manufacturer specifies edge-standing transport only; stacking permitted up to manufacturer-specified maximum stack height per pallet. Panels must never be transported flat; micro-cracks render panels useless and are considered total loss.
2.2 Inverters and mounting systems: delivered on separate pallets. Electronics require anti-static handling, ESD-safe packaging, and dedicated pallet positions; temperature/humidity controls applied where applicable.
2.3 LogiCore shall ensure trained personnel, edge protectors, appropriate dunnage, and CMR-compliant securing. Loading instructions (list przewozowy and zlecenie transportowe) will indicate edge orientation and stack limits.
3. Scheduling, Coordination and Installation Support
3.1 Deliveries shall be scheduled to match installation crew availability on-site. SolarTech will provide appointment windows and on-site contact details; LogiCore will confirm ETA not less than 24 hours prior to delivery for standard orders and 4 hours for last-mile windows when requested.
3.2 Seasonal adjustments: during March–September and subsidy deadlines, parties shall implement surge capacity plans. Additional vehicles or weekend shifts will be agreed and priced by annex.
4. Liability, Insurance and Penalties
4.1 Insurance: LogiCore shall maintain insurance covering full replacement value per panel, explicitly covering glass breakage and micro-cracks. Policy details and certificates shall be provided to SolarTech on commencement and upon renewal.
4.2 Liability: in case of breakage caused by LogiCore negligence (including failure to adhere to edge-standing, stacking limits or ESD handling), LogiCore shall be liable for full cost of the affected panels (replacement at invoice value) and for any documented installation delays directly attributable to the damage.
4.3 Penalties: for late delivery not excused under Force Majeure or agreed exceptions, LogiCore shall pay EUR 200 per affected delivery occurrence. For panel breakage where the panel is rendered unusable (including micro-cracks), LogiCore shall reimburse full replacement cost of each panel as invoiced by SolarTech or the manufacturer.
5. Returns and Warranty Reverse Logistics
5.1 Defective panels identified on delivery or during installation are to be returned to the manufacturer under warranty. LogiCore will manage reverse logistics, including collection, transport and delivery to the designated manufacturer address. Return shipments shall be accompanied by documentation (RMN/warranty note) and segregated to prevent cross-contamination with good stock.
5.2 Costs for warranty returns caused by LogiCore negligence shall be borne by LogiCore. Costs for returns covered by manufacturer warranty but not caused by transport may be invoiced to SolarTech per agreed rates.
6. Invoicing and Payment
6.1 Domestic transport charges and ancillaries under this Agreement shall be invoiced in EUR per contract terms; each faktura will reference CTR-2025-040 and include zlecenie transportowe numbers and CMR/list przewozowy references. Payment terms: 30 days net unless otherwise agreed in annex.
7. Compliance and Records
7.1 LogiCore warrants compliance with applicable transport regulations, permits and driver qualifications, and shall cooperate with UTK, GITD inspections and provide records on request. Data related to goods, routes and customers shall be treated as confidential (business secrets) subject to UOKiK considerations.
8. Force Majeure
8.1 Neither party is liable for non-performance due to force majeure (strikes, natural disasters, regulatory embargoes). Parties will use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate effects and resume performance.
9. Term and Termination
9.1 Term: 24 months from signature, auto-renewable unless terminated with 90 days' notice. Early termination for material breach, insolvency or repeated defaults allowed.
10. Notices and Governing Law
10.1 Notices delivered by registered mail or e-mail to contractual addresses. Governing law: Republic of Poland; competent courts in Warszawa.
Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
For SolarTech Polska Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
Date: __________
Annexes: Price Annex, Surge Capacity Plan, Insurance Certificates, Manufacturer Handling Specifications.

title: PoolChemicals — Swimming Pool Chemical Distribution Agreement (CTR-2025-041)
text:
AGREEMENT CTR-2025-041
PoolChemicals — Swimming Pool Chemical Distribution Agreement
between LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 100, Warszawa (headquarters) with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań ("Carrier" / "LogiCore") and AquaChem Sp. z o.o., factory in Tarnów ("Shipper" / "AquaChem").

Preamble
LogiCore undertakes carriage, storage and distribution of pool chemicals produced by AquaChem under the conditions below. This Agreement is governed by relevant Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and applicable ADR regulations. Regulatory oversight: UTK, GITD and consumer/pro-competition provisions of UOKiK remain applicable.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 Goods: chlorine (UN 2880, Class 5.1 — oxidizer), pH regulators (not oxidizing; some acid-based reducers), and algaecides. 1.2 ADR: All chlorine products designated UN 2880 Class 5.1 shall be transported and handled strictly in accordance with ADR. Non-ADR applies to pH regulators and algaecides where classification permits. 1.3 Typical routes: factory (Tarnów) to pool supply distributors, municipal swimming pools, hotels across Poland and EU. 1.4 Service model: predominantly groupage for small orders of 2–5 pallets to small distributors; also full-load deliveries for large accounts.

2. Seasonal Profile and Capacity
2.1 Parties acknowledge pool seasonality: extremely concentrated volumes March–June (pool opening season); minimal volumes November–February. Carrier shall allocate additional capacity and ADR-compliant vehicles in March–June. Schedules (harmonogram) and seasonal forecasts shall be exchanged quarterly.

3. Packaging, Segregation and Incompatibility
3.1 Packaging must comply with ADR and Shipper warranties on hazardous classification. 3.2 STORAGE/TRANSPORT INCOMPATIBILITY: Chlorine products MUST NEVER be stored or co-loaded in the same compartment, pallet, vehicle or container with acid-based pH reducers or any incompatible substances. Segregation and physical separation are mandatory. Co-loading of chlorine with incompatible acids or reducers constitutes a material breach and triggers penalties.

4. Documentation and Compliance
4.1 Each shipment must be accompanied by correct zlecenie transportowe, list przewozowy / CMR where applicable, ADR transport document for UN 2880 goods, safety data sheets (SDS), and faktura. 4.2 Vehicles and drivers transporting ADR chlorine must carry ADR carriage equipment, personal protective equipment, and an onboard chlorine spill kit. Driver training in oxidizer hazards and emergency procedures is mandatory; records to be provided on request.

5. Emergency Response and Safety
5.1 Emergency response: For any chlorine incident, Carrier shall follow ADR emergency procedures and notify local emergency services, Shipper, and receiving party immediately. Chlorine spill kit must be present on each vehicle carrying UN 2880. 5.2 Liability for negligent emergency handling remains subject to limits below and Polish law.

6. Penalties and Remedies
6.1 ADR Violation (any transport of UN 2880 not meeting ADR requirements): EUR 2,000 per event, payable within 14 days of invoice. 6.2 Incompatible Co-loading (chlorine with acid-based pH reducers): EUR 500 per event. 6.3 Late delivery (beyond agreed delivery window without prior written consent): EUR 100 per incident. 6.4 Penalties are without prejudice to claims for additional damages permitted under Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe.

7. Financial Terms and Insurance
7.1 Annual contract value is estimated at EUR 180,000. Invoicing in EUR. Payment terms: 30 days net unless otherwise agreed. 7.2 Carrier shall maintain insurance including OC przewoźnika (liability for carriage) and cargo insurance with limits adequate to the transported hazardous cargo and in accordance with Prawo Przewozowe and market practice.

8. Operational Provisions
8.1 Route planning shall prioritize safety and, for ADR chlorine loads, routes approved for hazardous materials. 8.2 Depot handling at LogiCore facilities (Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań) must maintain segregation protocols; no storage of chlorine adjacent to acids. 8.3 Small orders: LogiCore will provide groupage (consolidation) services for 2–5 pallet consignments, optimizing hub-and-spoke runs.

9. Force Majeure and Termination
9.1 Force majeure defined as events beyond control (natural disasters, strikes, regulatory embargoes). Parties shall notify and mitigate. 9.2 Material breach (e.g., ADR violation, incompatible co-loading) entitles non-breaching party to termination after cure period of 7 days.

10. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
10.1 Governing law: Republic of Poland. Disputes to be settled first by negotiation; unresolved disputes by competent courts in Warszawa. Relevant transportation law: Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and applicable ADR rules.

11. Miscellaneous
11.1 Compliance with UTK and GITD inspections is mandatory. Parties shall cooperate with UOKiK inquiries if any. 11.2 Amendments in writing. 11.3 Notices in Polish or English.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — authorized representative
Name: ___________________  Date: __________
For AquaChem Sp. z o.o. — authorized representative
Name: ___________________  Date: __________

End of Agreement CTR-2025-041

title: GasIndustrial Industrial Gas Cylinder Logistics Agreement between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and Linde Gaz Polska Sp. z o.o.
text:
Agreement No. CTR-2025-042

This Transport and Cylinder Management Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into on the Effective Date below by and between:
- LogiCore Sp. z o.o., with registered office in Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań ("LogiCore"), and
- Linde Gaz Polska Sp. z o.o., registered in Poland ("Customer" or "Linde").

Preamble
LogiCore provides specialised transport and handling services for industrial gas cylinders (oxygen, nitrogen, argon, CO2, acetylene and specialty gas mixtures) subject to ADR Class 2 (compressed, liquefied, dissolved). The Parties wish to set out terms for safe, compliant, and reliable logistics including full/empty exchange, cylinder tracking, and medical oxygen handling.

1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall perform carriage, delivery and cylinder management services ("Services") for cylinders supplied by Linde from filling plants in Kraków and Wrocław to industrial users across Poland including welding shops, hospitals and food processors. Typical routes are Kraków -> regional users and Wrocław -> regional users; the Parties may agree additional routes by zlecenie transportowe.
1.2 Volume: approximately 2,000–3,000 cylinders per week, steady year-round. Annual value: EUR 580,000.
1.3 All consignments shall be accompanied by appropriate transport documents (CMR or list przewozowy) and ADR documentation as required.

2. ADR and Safety Compliance
2.1 LogiCore shall comply with ADR requirements for Class 2 gases. Vehicles, stowage, segregation and securing shall meet ADR provisions including but not limited to 7.5.2 (segregation), 7.5.7 (securing of cylinders), and applicable packing instructions.
2.2 Safety-critical requirement: acetylene and oxygen MUST be segregated on the same vehicle per ADR 7.5.2. Failure to segregate constitutes a material breach and triggers penalties (Clause 7).
2.3 Vehicle requirements: vehicles must be open-sided or ventilated body enabling natural ventilation and secure lashing points for cylinder racks. Cylinder securing per ADR 7.5.7 (approved cradles, racks, restraints) is mandatory.

3. Cylinder Management and Tracking
3.1 Full/Empty Exchange: LogiCore will operate a full/empty cylinder exchange system as directed by Linde. Exchanges shall be recorded on each delivery ticket and reflected on the faktura.
3.2 Tracking by serial number: every cylinder movement shall be recorded by serial number (nr seryjny). LogiCore will maintain electronic records for traceability and reporting, accessible to Linde on request.
3.3 Cylinder testing: LogiCore shall check cylinder test dates (ważność badania) at point of collection; any cylinder with expired testing or missing markings will be rejected and returned to Linde without carriage.

4. Medical Oxygen and Specialty Gases
4.1 Medical-grade oxygen: deliveries of medical oxygen require dedicated vehicles (no co-loading with industrial gases) and compliance with GDP where applicable for medical-grade gases. Dedicated vehicles shall be cleaned, certified and used exclusively for medical oxygen during periods of carriage.
4.2 Specialty gas mixtures: handling instructions provided by Linde shall be followed; compatibility and segregation rules apply.

5. Performance, Schedule and KPIs
5.1 Timeliness: deliveries to agreed time windows. Standard tolerance: +/- agreed window per zlecenie transportowe. Late delivery penalty set out in Clause 7.
5.2 Reporting: weekly operational reports including volumes, incidents, lost or rejected cylinders, and cylinder serial number reconciliation.

6. Liability, Insurance and Regulatory Compliance
6.1 LogiCore shall procure and maintain insurance covering cargo, liability and passenger/third-party where applicable. The Parties acknowledge the applicability of Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Regulatory compliance with UTK, GITD and UOKiK guidelines is required.
6.2 Where international carriage occurs, CMR provisions shall apply as appropriate.

7. Penalties and Compensation
7.1 Segregation Violation: EUR 3,000 per violation where acetylene and oxygen are found co-loaded or improperly segregated in breach of ADR 7.5.2.
7.2 Lost Cylinder: EUR 500 per lost cylinder (acknowledging cylinders as expensive assets) subject to serial-number reconciliation; this amount is without prejudice to replacement cost recovery under Kodeks Cywilny.
7.3 Late Delivery: EUR 100 per late delivery occurrence defined in Clause 5.
7.4 Penalties may be set off against payments; LogiCore retains the right to contest alleged breaches with documented evidence.

8. Payments, Invoicing and Audit
8.1 Invoices (faktura) shall be issued in EUR, payable within 30 days net unless otherwise agreed. Invoicing will reference zlecenie transportowe numbers and cylinder serial numbers for exchanges.
8.2 Linde may audit LogiCore operational records and cylinder tracking data upon reasonable notice.

9. Inspection, Rejection and Returns
9.1 LogiCore will reject cylinders with expired test dates (ważność badania) and notify Linde immediately. Such rejected cylinders will not be carried until certified.
9.2 Any damage or non-conformity discovered at delivery must be reported on the list przewozowy/CMR. Claims follow procedures in the Prawo Przewozowe and Kodeks Cywilny.

10. Force Majeure
10.1 Neither Party is liable for failure due to events beyond reasonable control (force majeure) including extraordinary road closures, regulatory injunctions by UTK/GITD, or acts of public authorities.

11. Term and Termination
11.1 Term: this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for 24 months, renewable by mutual agreement. Immediate termination is permitted for repeated safety breaches (including segregation violations).

12. Confidentiality and Data Protection
12.1 The Parties shall maintain confidentiality of commercial and operational data and comply with applicable data protection laws.

13. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
13.1 This Agreement is governed by Polish law (Kodeks Cywilny). Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration or courts in Warszawa unless agreed otherwise.

14. Miscellaneous
14.1 Amendments require written form. Contacts for operations, safety (including ADR compliance) and invoicing shall be maintained in Annex A.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________  Date: __/
For Linde Gaz Polska Sp. z o.o.: _________________  Date: __/

Annexes (incorporated): Annex A – Operational Contacts; Annex B – Vehicle and Equipment Specification; Annex C – KPI and Reporting Template.

Effective Date: [dd/mm/2025]

References: ADR (European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road), Prawo Przewozowe, Kodeks Cywilny, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, guidance of UTK, GITD and UOKiK.

title: TextileRecycling Collection and Transport Agreement — TextileRecycling Used Clothing Collection
text:
AGREEMENT No. CTR-2025-043

TextileRecycling Used Clothing Collection Agreement

concluded on this day between:

1) LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Centralna 12, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland, NIP: XXXXXXXX, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań ("LogiCore"), and
2) ReWear Polska Sp. z o.o., ul. Sortownicza 3, 90-001 Łódź, Poland ("ReWear").

Preamble
LogiCore is engaged in collection and transport logistics. ReWear operates a textile sorting and reuse facility in Łódź. The parties enter this Agreement in respect of collection of used clothing from street containers and charity drop-off points as set out below, under and subject to the provisions of the Kodeks Cywilny, relevant environmental law (including Ustawa o odpadach and BDO obligations), Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.

§1 Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore shall collect used clothing from approximately 500 street containers and charity drop-off points located across Warszawa and Łódź in accordance with optimized collection routes ("Services").
1.2 Collection shall be performed by LogiCore using GPS-tracked vehicles including compactor trucks and box trucks with tail-lift. Vehicle types and registration numbers shall be provided to ReWear on commencement and updated in monthly reports.
1.3 Container fill levels shall be monitored by sensors and GPS telematics; fill-level triggers shall automatically create a zlecenie transportowe and schedule collection on the next available optimized route.
1.4 ReWear acknowledges and agrees that LogiCore provides transport and collection only; LogiCore shall deliver all collected material to ReWear's sorting facility in Łódź. LogiCore shall not perform sorting, classification or onward processing of textiles.

§2 Waste Classification & Regulatory Compliance
2.1 The parties acknowledge that collected used clothing remains classified as waste until properly sorted and recovered by ReWear. ReWear shall maintain up-to-date BDO registration for receipt and treatment of material. ReWear is responsible for any obligations under Ustawa o odpadach and BDO after material admission at its facility.
2.2 LogiCore shall perform transport in compliance with Prawo Przewozowe and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, maintain required vehicle documents and cooperate with regulatory bodies (UTK, GITD) upon lawful request.

§3 Volume, Performance Standards and Seasonality
3.1 Estimated volumes: 200–300 tonnes per month on average. Parties acknowledge seasonal peaks (notably January post-Christmas surge) and shall cooperate to allocate additional transport capacity as required.
3.2 LogiCore shall use route optimization to achieve timely collection and minimize overflowing containers. Sensor-trigger thresholds and service-level parameters shall be agreed in the Operational Annex.

§4 Fees, Invoicing and Annual Value
4.1 The annual contractual value of services is agreed as EUR 190,000 (one hundred ninety thousand euros). Payments shall be made upon monthly faktura issued by LogiCore, payable within 30 days unless otherwise agreed in writing.
4.2 Transport documents accompanying loads shall include a CMR or list przewozowy as applicable; each delivery to ReWear shall be confirmed by a signed delivery note at the Łódź facility.

§5 Penalties and Remedies
5.1 In the event a container is reported overflowing and is not collected by LogiCore within 24 hours of notification (or within 24 hours of an automated critical sensor alarm), LogiCore shall pay to ReWear, or to the municipal authority where public complaint risk arises, a contractual penalty of EUR 100 per overflowing container per incident. This penalty is without prejudice to liability for proven additional damages under Kodeks Cywilny.

§6 Environmental Reporting and Data
6.1 LogiCore shall provide monthly reports showing tonnage collected per month by location (Warszawa / Łódź), vehicle kilometres, and route efficiency.
6.2 Quarterly environmental reports shall include a CO2 savings calculation based on agreed emissions factors, methodologies and route telematics; parties will agree the calculation method in the Operational Annex.

§7 Container Maintenance and Damage Reporting
7.1 LogiCore shall report damaged, graffitied, or otherwise compromised containers to ReWear within 24 hours of discovery. ReWear shall arrange repair or replacement of containers at its cost unless damage is caused by LogiCore's negligence, in which case the parties shall agree corrective action and cost allocation.

§8 Liability and Insurance
8.1 Each party shall maintain adequate insurance for its activities (transport, employer's liability, third party liability) and provide certificates on request. Liability is governed by Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo Przewozowe as applicable.

§9 Term and Termination
9.1 This Agreement commences on signature and is for an initial term of 24 months. Either party may terminate for material breach after 30 days' cure period or immediately for regulatory non-compliance that jeopardizes operations or reputation.

§10 Miscellaneous
10.1 Notices shall be in writing. Disputes shall be settled by arbitration in Warszawa or by Polish courts if required. The parties agree to cooperate with UOKiK and other regulators on sector matters.
10.2 Amendments require written signed annexes. Operational details (sensor thresholds, routing platform credentials, SLA metrics) shall be set out in the Operational Annex which forms an integral part of this Agreement.

Signed for LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
Signed for ReWear Polska Sp. z o.o.: ____________________
Date: ____________________

title: LabEquipment Scientific Instrument Transport Agreement — CTR-2025-044
text:
LabEquipment Scientific Instrument Transport Agreement
Contract No.: CTR-2025-044
Date: [dd/mm/2025]

Parties
1. LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, Poland, with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań, registered under Polish law, hereinafter LogiCore, carrier and service provider.
2. LabTech Instruments Polska Sp. z o.o., hereinafter Client, purchaser and consignor of scientific and laboratory equipment.

Preamble
This Agreement governs the carriage, handling, monitoring, installation support and liability for extremely sensitive scientific instruments including but not limited to mass spectrometers, HPLC systems and electron microscopes (hereinafter Instruments). The Parties agree services shall be provided in accordance with applicable Polish and European law, including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and with regard to applicable regulatory bodies UTK, GITD, and UOKiK. International legs to Poland shall be governed additionally by CMR where applicable.

Scope of Services
LogiCore shall provide white-glove transport and handling services for Instruments imported via major hubs (Frankfurt am Main, Amsterdam Schiphol, Wien/Vienna) and delivered to universities and research institutes across Poland, including but not limited to Warszawa, Kraków, Wrocław, Poznań, Gdańsk, and research facilities in Katowice and Lublin. Services include: collection, customs coordination, escorted road transport, accelerometer monitoring, climate-controlled enclosures in transit, specialist loading/unloading, and basic installation placement support per manufacturer specifications.

Annual Value and Volume
The Parties acknowledge low frequency/high value operations. Annual contract value is EUR 290,000. Typical declared value per instrument for insurance purposes ranges EUR 100,000 to EUR 500,000. Certain instruments may have replacement values exceeding EUR 500,000.

Service Standards and Technical Requirements
1. Vibration Limits and Monitoring
   a. Maximum permissible acceleration during transport: 0.3 g (peak). Exceedance of 0.3 g is a material breach.
   b. Continuous accelerometer monitoring is mandatory for each shipment. Data loggers with time-stamped records shall be fitted to the Instrument crate and to the vehicle chassis. Accelerometer logs shall be provided to Client within 24 hours of delivery.
2. Climate Control
   a. Transport environment shall be maintained at 18-22 degrees C and relative humidity 40-60 percent.
   b. Systems must prevent condensation. Desiccant, sealed crates, and active heating/cooling as required will be used.
3. Vehicle and Handling
   a. Transport by anti-vibration air-ride suspension vehicles only.
   b. White-glove specialized crew 2-3 persons per load with training in moving sensitive optics and electronics.
   c. Use of approved shock-absorbing crates and manufacturer's mounting rigs where required.

Calibration, Recalibration and Pre-Shipment Requirements
1. Client shall ensure Instruments are calibrated per manufacturer procedure prior to collection. A certificate of calibration shall accompany each shipment.
2. Any recorded event during transit that meets or exceeds 0.3 g or any other shock/vibration event reasonably likely to affect instrument performance shall trigger recalibration. Recalibration costs borne by Client or Carrier shall follow the liability provisions below. Indicative recalibration cost range: EUR 5,000 to EUR 20,000 per instrument, depending on model and scope.

Liability, Penalties and Indemnities
1. Penalty for Vibration Exceedance. For each confirmed vibration exceedance event attributable to Carrier operations, Carrier shall pay a contractual penalty EUR 5,000 to Client, without prejudice to further claims for proven damages.
2. Recalibration Costs. If accelerometer logs or post-delivery inspection indicate shock/vibration sufficient to require recalibration, Carrier shall be responsible for recalibration costs up to EUR 20,000 per instrument when the shock is attributable to Carrier. If attributable to force majeure or third party, Parties shall apportion cost per evidence.
3. Physical Damage and Replacement. Carrier shall be liable for physical loss or damage caused by Carrier negligence up to full replacement value declared for the Instrument. For certain Instruments with replacement values exceeding EUR 500,000, full replacement cost is payable subject to insurance recovery. Liability is subject to limits established in applicable transport law (Prawo Przewozowe and CMR where applicable) except to the extent Parties agree higher contractual liability as set forth herein.
4. Deductibles and Limits. Where insurance pays, Carrier shall cooperate with Client and insurer in claims handling (see Insurance section). Nothing in this Agreement shall limit Client rights under Kodeks Cywilny for gross negligence or willful misconduct.

Insurance
1. Client and Carrier shall ensure appropriate insurance coverage. Carrier will arrange transit insurance where requested by Client and agreed in written zlecenie transportowe. Declared value per instrument typically between EUR 100,000 and EUR 500,000; value declared for each shipment shall be set out in the list przewozowy and CMR where applicable.
2. Parties shall exchange insurance certificates and contact points prior to first shipment. Carrier shall comply with insurer conditions to avoid denial of cover.

Documentation, Monitoring and Acceptance
1. Each shipment shall include: CMR or list przewozowy, packing list, certificate of calibration, accelerometer and GPS logs, customs documentation and a signed protocol of delivery (protokol doręczenia) at consignee.
2. Client or consignee shall inspect Instruments at delivery. Any visible damage shall be recorded immediately. Acceptance without recorded reservations shall not preclude latent defect claims supported by monitoring data.

Installation Support
LogiCore shall provide crew trained in basic instrument placement and set-down per manufacturer specifications. This does not include formal installation, alignment, complex calibration or commissioning unless separately agreed and priced. Any assistance beyond placement shall be provided under a separate service order.

Fees, Invoicing and Payment
1. Fees for transport, handling and crew services shall be set out in Appendix A and in individual zlecenie transportowe. International legs invoiced in EUR; domestic Polish legs may be invoiced in PLN if agreed.
2. LogiCore shall issue faktura within 7 days of delivery. Payment terms: 30 days net unless otherwise agreed.

Force Majeure and Safety Compliance
Carrier shall adhere to Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, road safety rules and any UTK or GITD requirements for cross-border and domestic transport. Events outside reasonable control (force majeure) shall be notified within 48 hours.

Term and Termination
This Agreement commences on signature and continues for 12 months, renewable by mutual consent. Either Party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period.

Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
Polish law shall govern. Disputes shall be settled amicably or before the competent Polish courts in Warszawa. Parties may seek interim relief where necessary for preservation of rights.

Miscellaneous
Amendments must be in writing. Confidentiality of technical data and instrument specifications shall be maintained. References to law: Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo Przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym. Regulatory authorities: UTK, GITD, UOKiK.

Signatures
For LogiCore Sp. z o.o. ............................
Name/Title

For LabTech Instruments Polska Sp. z o.o. ............................
Name/Title

End of Agreement CTR-2025-044

title: EUFunding Grant-Funded Equipment Distribution Agreement (Umowa dystrybucji sprzętu - CTR-2025-045)
text:
This Agreement (Agreement No. CTR-2025-045) is made between:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, with operational depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań and a central warehouse in Radom (hereinafter "LogiCore" or "Carrier"), and
Polska Agencja Rozwoju Przedsiębiorczości (PARP), (hereinafter "PARP" or "Principal").
Preamble: PARP, as an implementing body of EU-funded SME support programs, has awarded a contract under Prawo zamówień publicznych (Pzp). LogiCore shall perform transport and distribution of EU-funded equipment including machinery, IT infrastructure, and laboratory equipment to grant recipients across all 16 voivodeships of Poland in strict compliance with public procurement and EU audit requirements.
1. Scope of Services
1.1 LogiCore will collect equipment from the central warehouse in Radom and deliver to grant beneficiaries at addresses across all 16 voivodeships. Routes will be planned to optimize timeliness and security and shall be recorded by GPS route log for each vehicle.
1.2 Equipment categories: industrial machinery, IT hardware and peripherals, laboratory equipment, accessories and spare parts. Each consignment will include a packing list with serial numbers (numer seryjny), model, and condition at dispatch.
1.3 Deliveries to be accompanied by CMR (where applicable), list przewozowy, delivery protocol (protokół doręczenia) and photo documentation (loading, en route, unloading). All documents shall satisfy EU audit rules and Pzp clauses.
2. Compliance and Law
2.1 LogiCore shall perform services in accordance with Polish law including Kodeks Cywilny, Prawo przewozowe, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and all obligations under Pzp. LogiCore acknowledges oversight by UTK, GITD and UOKiK where applicable.
2.2 All actions under this Agreement must enable PARP to demonstrate compliance to EU institutions and to NIK (Najwyższa Izba Kontroli) during audits.
3. Documentation and Archival
3.1 At delivery the recipient must sign the delivery confirmation (potwierdzenie odbioru), verify and sign off serial numbers, and sign a condition report (protokół stanu technicznego). Photo evidence at unloading and a timestamped GPS route log are mandatory.
3.2 All documentation (invoices, CMRs, delivery protocols, photo documentation, GPS logs, serial number verification sheets) shall be submitted to PARP electronically within 3 working days of delivery and archived by LogiCore for the period required by EU regulations and PARP (minimum 10 years or the period specified in funding agreement).
3.3 Failure to produce complete documentation upon audit shall be treated as non-compliance per clause 7.
4. Timeframes and Milestones
4.1 Deliveries are time-bound to grant milestones specified by PARP. LogiCore shall meet delivery deadlines; delays expose grant recipients to risk of grant clawback and expose LogiCore to contractual penalties.
5. Insurance
5.1 LogiCore shall maintain insurance covering full replacement value of all transported equipment. Insurance documentation evidencing cover for cargo, transit, theft, and accidental damage in accordance with EU funding rules shall be provided to PARP prior to first dispatch and upon each policy renewal.
6. Value and Invoicing
6.1 Framework annual value: EUR 210,000 (subject to allocation in the relevant EU programming period and subsequent amendments). Invoices (faktura) shall be issued in EUR unless otherwise directed by PARP for specific domestic disbursements.
6.2 All invoices, delivery reports, and CMRs shall be available for inspection by NIK and other authorised auditors.
7. Penalties and Liability
7.1 Late delivery: EUR 300 per occurrence (per delayed consignment) payable by LogiCore to PARP.
7.2 Incomplete documentation discovered in an EU audit: EUR 1,000 per instance, payable by LogiCore to PARP. Repeated findings may lead to contract termination and claims for consequential losses.
7.3 LogiCore liability is subject to limits provided by applicable law (Kodeks Cywilny and Prawo przewozowe) unless gross negligence or willful misconduct is established.
8. Audit and Transparency
8.1 PARP, NIK, and EU auditors shall have full access to relevant records, warehouses, vehicles, GPS logs, and personnel involved in performance. LogiCore shall cooperate fully with audits and provide certified copies of all requested documentation promptly.
9. Term and Termination
9.1 Term: this Agreement enters into force upon signature and continues for the program period or until services equivalent to the EUR 210,000 framework value are completed, unless earlier terminated for material breach.
9.2 Termination for cause includes substantial failure to comply with Pzp, persistent late deliveries, or repeated audit findings.
10. Final Provisions
10.1 Disputes shall be governed by Polish law and resolved in Polish courts unless parties agree alternative dispute resolution. This Agreement complements obligations under Pzp and does not release either party from statutory duties under transport and public procurement law.
10.2 Contact and notices: formal notices shall be sent to LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (Warszawa) and PARP addresses on file.
